clean_text: ['this was the second time i have stayed at alamkulkul. first time was lovely in a villa. this time we were in a family room. the room was old and not well maintained. we were located on when i ask the reception staff was there another family room they looked at me blankly and said no that s your room. it s lost its charm and class from years ago. sorry would recommend or stay again. much better in bali for the same price.', 'we stayed in the quad room on the third floor. the room didn t smell of mould like some reviews mentioned which was a relief. having four single beds was a need replacing. large gap under the door letting mozzies in. no drawers for clothes. breakfast was very basic and not particularly enjoyable. overall the hotel just lacked atmosphere. it s a shame because the location and room layout were great but the place really needs some attention.', 'this resort was awful. i wouldn t even call it a resort. looks nothing like the photos, so old. very villa doors the only entry to the main bedroom and we had to seek the staff 3 4 times a day to unlock the door. they told us there was an issue with the door and it was to be fixed. this did not happen during our stay and at times we would have to wait for half an hour for a staff member to come to the villa and unlock the door. breakfast was not included in our stay but very reasonably priced and delicious. hotel is a short walk to beachwalk and across from the beach. we could see the beach fireworks every night from our villa but they were very loud. the price we paid for the 5 nights at the hotel was expensive for bali pricing and i expected the villa to be clean and well maintained. it was not worth the money we paid.', 'unfriendly staff and poor service. we requested a uncomfortable. not worth the price or hassle.', 'not a welcoming check in. we were given warm dry towels to cool down with. the resort is extremely run down and in need of major maintenance. the villa bathroom and pool towels always had stains on them. the breakfast was the worst l have ever had. they do not cater for westerners, no bacon is provided. we ate out for breakfast. towelservice is not job in the pool bar.', 'booked for one night before fly out. checked the review of the hotel and could see many positive feedbacks with good price, so i decided to stay. the staffs here are so nice strong sewerage smell all night with some noise from the next door. i felt like im in a jail. just fyi, im not a sensitive person and i have never provided this level of negative feedback. its very 'stayed here for 4 nights in a family room, which was nice and tidy plenty of room. nice pools, one garden pool which also looked cloudy but a larger pool with a bar which was pleasant. specials on cocktails at 4, with friendly barman. all staff very polite but i feel they are lacking execution in the food department for a boutique resort. i have many years experience in this field of work so i what it was... as soon as we saw the room i realised we had made a mistake... it is very dark and gloomy, being a bungalow with a low thatched roof, it is very old and dated. the bed is very hard, combined_reviews: i stayed overnight for transit. the staff were very helpful, especially ms. niken. i had a room on the second floor with a pool view. the room was clean and well maintained. there cleanliness and maintenance need a lot of improvement. the rooms felt worn out, and in places where maintenance had been done, it was clear that it was poorly executed. the bathrooms were the most disappointing aspect of the room terrible conditions that i didn t expect from a resort of this caliber. however, my real concern, and the reason i m writing this review, lies in the experience we had regarding food safety and the handling of my son s severe nut allergy. we had a really unsettling experience at ganesha sanskrit a chain, so we assumed the standards would be consistent . despite repeatedly asking if a dish contained nuts three times in total , and being assured by three different members of staff, including the manager, that the dish was nut free, my son ate the meal and shortly after started feeling unwell. we didn t initially suspect the food, but as his condition worsened, we realized it must have been the meal. my son ended up very sick and required medication. when we informed the staff, they told us, after the fact, that the sauce had cashews in it. we were already in a state of panic, and to make matters worse, no one checked on my son s wellbeing. after rushing to our room, my husband settled the bill, and we requested a sick bowl, only to be handed a pyrex bowl from the kitchen no empathy or support whatsoever. the real kicker came when we attempted to raise the issue with guest relations. they escalated the matter to the food and beverage manager, who called us the next day with an offer of a free meal in the same restaurant which we turned down immediately. my son no longer trusts them, and understandably, we are extremely concerned. conclusion if you have any food allergies or intolerances, i would strongly advise caution at this resort. this experience could have turned out much worse, and it s alarming that a resort of this size doesn t have better protocols in place for dealing with such serious issues. there are many families with is impressive to look at. it is in a great beachfront location, on a beautiful sandy stretch of beach close to bali collection shops and the main nusa dua shopping and restaurant strip. the all multiple dining options in house meaning there is no need to go out of the resort to eat. japanese was the best overall but for atmosphere lunch at the beach bar and grill was awesome. even mini bar is included but it is quite frankly not much no chocolates, no spirits, no nuts none of the normal things one would expect. in the 6 days we drank one beer. the rooms are in serious need of renovation upgrading and general maintenance. it is clear from our experience that this is really hit and miss. the first room we were allocated was one of the grande rooms we paid extra for the higher category larger room. our room was quiet, clean and large however that s where the positives ended. the air conditioning barely worked. the hot water was not hot luke warm if lucky or otherwise cold so showers were not much fun. the bar fridge was set to the warmest setting and was not full on arrival but filled afterwards and of course everything was put in hot meaning nothing was drinkable for at least 24 hrs and then only after we had set the fridge to cold. there was only one power point in the room and it didn t work so we had to unplug the lamp and use that socket. and finally the bed it was like sleeping on concrete. we tried getting engineering to fix the air con and the hot water but it took 2 days to get anywhere with multiple whatsapp message conversations, phone calls and in person visits to the front desk. after all that on day 3 we were told that they couldn t do anything to fix either issue. if this is the grande room i d hate to think what the standard of the classic deluxe rooms is like. a lot of the room basics were also missing no room service menu, no option to have sheets changed on your bed, no spa menu in the room. so many things that are hotel basics in a 3 star hotel were just lacking. eventually one of the managers samuel offered to change rooms and ended up moving us to the honeymoon suite in the ayodya palace area. this was only after the food and beverage manager widana spoke to him. we had come across him by accident while looking for the hotel clinic which no longer exists despite signs all over the resort saying it does and he asked us how everything was going. this suite was smaller and noisier but everything worked at least aircon was cold, hot water was hot, bed was comfortable, power points worked. thankfully. in this room we did have a room service menu and a card to tell housekeeping if we wanted sheets towels changed. strangely though the room set up is such that you cannot see the tv without getting out of bed. it is a 4 poster bed with curtains draped around it meaning the tv view is blocked by both the posts and the drapes. odd layout. this room also came with the benefit of using the ayodya lounge for breakfast and the dedicated pool area for palace guests. the breakfast in the lounge was much quieter than at waterfall restaurant and while only a small selection it was perfectly adequate and had the benefit of a la carte egg ordering. service in the lounge was hit and miss with some staff being extra helpful and others just rudely saying no when asking for bottled water. my wife used the spa on our second last day and unfortunately had to abandon the treatment less than half way through. this was due to construction building works going on directly above the spa. what was meant to be a quiet blissful session instead was noisy and stressful. so much so the staff member stopped the session saying there is no way you can relax under these conditions. well done to her for doing so but why would you take bookings under these circumstances at all and why would you not tell people when booking? food and drinks quality was largely ok. cocktails tended to be made very weak however if you ordered a spirit they were always happy to give you a double which was odd. cocktails also were not made using ice in shakers so were very rarely cold not sure if the bartenders have been properly trained in this art. food was good but more often came out lukewarm cool due to the time taken and the wind by the beach . probably the only hotel where they deliver your food with plates completely uncovered to the elements vs having a steel dish cover to keep things warm. the wine list was horrendous. we tired multiple wines and all were undrinkable. we settled on one australian wine that was reasonable a semillon sb blend looking it up on vivino it retails bakc home for 7aud..... enough said. front desk service was almost non existent. whenever we would ring the front desk housekeeping assistant manager lines nobody would answer the phone and this would go on for hours. we would try the whatsapp service and sometimes they would take 4 5 hrs to respond. if we went up to the front desk in person and told them they would just say maybe there s something wrong with your phone. there was never any acknowledgment or apology for the lack of service. lastly the majority of the resort is paved with shiny tiles. and when there is any level of humidity always in bali they become dangerously slippery. the foyers connecting every room and every building have these tiles and we slipped on them on multiple occasions. the worst area for this is around the infinity pool on the beachfront. leading up to the pool area are slate type rough surface tiles which are excellent for grip but all around the pool slippery shiny tiles again and these are terribly slippery. just some really silly design features that are dangerous and should be rectified. so overall while we relaxed and enjoyed ourselves its not a place we would choose to return to. that said if the rooms are all upgraded to a 5 star standard, staff are trained in proper service standards and basic facilities amenities improved then this could be a great resort.', 'beautiful resort in nusa dua more suited to families and honeymooners not my thing nice for a week but wouldn t come back we bought the all inclusive combined_reviews: terrible stay. i m currently staying at this hotel and writing this while it s still fresh. check in is at 3 pm no exceptions. even after a long overnight flight or with a small child, you will be left waiting until 3. that s just awful. our room looks out onto a garden area, but every morning we are woken up by loud renovation or construction noise. if you wanted rest, forget it. to make things worse, the bars and restaurants on site charge about three times more than places outside. if you don t buy all inclusive, you ll be forced to go off the property repeatedly to eat a real pain. don t overpay for convenience that doesn t exist. choose a different hotel one that s quieter, better organized, more accommodating and actually cares about guests. i won t be coming back. our stay at ayodya resort bali aug sep 2025 was deeply disappointing and distressing. the room we were given had a strong sewerage smell, mould, and a dirty plastic mat in the shower. the toilet and shower did not work properly, and i became violently ill overnight from the food vomiting and diarrhoea for several hours . my mother had to search for staff at 3am to help with the bathroom issues. when i raised these concerns, the hotel s response was shocking the manager and another staff member came into my room while i was visibly unwell and pressured me to sign a legal disclaimer in exchange for a refund. the document required me to waive my rights and agree not to post any reviews. i refused, as no guest should be forced to sign away their rights, especially while sick. this experience completely ruined our holiday. i would not recommend this resort to anyone seeking a safe, clean, or professionally managed environment. the service and the food was very good. refurb. i didn t expect a grotty shower curtain in a 5 star resort 2 great choice of food but the meat was usually of poor standard. uncooked burgers and gristly chicken made us stick to pizza, rice and chips unfortunately 3 the hotel bar area was beautiful and relaxing, overlooking the gardens, but the same 5 minute long music was played continuously over and over again, which became incredibly annoying. couldn t be changed sometimes ? it has so much potential if the above were fixed it would be lovely resort a bit dated. service times need improvement due to staff numbers . but the staff were excellent and did what they could. better value and service elsewhere. we checked out a day early ayodhya resort is a place that left me with mixed feelings. let me break it down pros the lobby and external appearance of the resort are absolutely stunning. the traditional architecture is both impressive and welcoming, and everything looks pristine and well maintained. food quality is generally decent, with dishes being well prepared and flavorful, though the variety could definitely be better. cons rooms are definitely dated. they re not a huge issue for me, but cleanliness and maintenance need a lot of improvement. the rooms felt worn out, and in places where maintenance had been done, it was clear that it was poorly executed. the bathrooms were the most disappointing aspect of the room terrible conditions that i didn t expect from a resort of this caliber. however, my real concern, and the reason i m writing this review, lies in the experience we had regarding food safety and the handling of my son s severe nut allergy. we had a really unsettling experience at ganesha sanskrit a chain, so we assumed the standards would be consistent . despite repeatedly asking if a dish contained nuts three times in total , and being assured by three different members of staff, including the manager, that the dish was nut free, my son ate the meal and shortly after started feeling unwell. we didn t initially suspect the food, but as his condition worsened, we realized it must have been the meal. my son ended up very sick and required medication. when we informed the staff, they told us, after the fact, that the sauce had cashews in it. we were already in a state of panic, and to make matters worse, no one checked on my son s wellbeing. after rushing to our room, my husband settled the bill, and we requested a sick bowl, only to be handed a pyrex bowl from the kitchen no empathy or support whatsoever. the real kicker came when we attempted to raise the issue with guest relations. they escalated the matter to the food and beverage manager, who called us the next day with an offer of a free meal in the same restaurant which we turned down immediately. my son no longer trusts them, and understandably, we are extremely concerned. conclusion if you have any food allergies or intolerances, i would strongly advise caution at this resort. this experience could have turned out much worse, and it s alarming that a resort of this size doesn t have better protocols in place for dealing with such serious issues. there are many families with children bart_summary: The hotel charges three times more to eat in the bars and restaurants on site. Check in is at 3 pm no exceptions. The toilet and shower did not work.

2hours still they didn t send even after so many calls.gave excuses that we are busy. they charged for small things like 2pcs of lemon slices or onion rings. morning breakfast is not good for vegetarians. even they charged for coffee at breakfast that sounds so sad for 5star hotel.', 'wasn t a great start, booked for 3 adults, i should have stipulated 3 single adults but our room only had 1 king bed which isn t acceptable for 3 people so had to pay extra 400 aus to upgrade to larger room. resort is fine, didn t like the location though. worst thing was we ate at gracie kelly s and my son felt really sick immediately after eating, straight back to the room and he started violently vomiting, called for the doctor who gave him an injection but it didn t help at all, 8.5 hours of hell through the night with him vomiting and stomach cramps. didn t realise until home and looked at the pic of his burger and noticed the bacon was raw which obviously gave him food poisoning. i emailed the resort with pictures and requested them to reimburse me for the doctors bill his food of 200 aust, they denied it was their fault saying how safe they are with their food and offered next time a food bev credit on our next stay, unfortunately we wont be returning after our experience, so i again requested a refund they never replied. very poor service from a well know establishment and considering the photo clearly shows uncooked bacon and was the pubs fault he was so sick. not happy at all', 'this was our 8th visit and bali dynasty did not disappoint. the room we stayed in was comfortable although the bathroom looked a little tired. the staff was amazing. no request was too much. from the moment we checked in to the when we checked out we were made to feel welcome. the spa offers a good variety of treatments to suit everyone s needs. the newly renovated kids club looks amazing. the bfast has good variety and the staff will try to accomodate any requests. the teamed meals could be better but overall still pleasant. the resort has a good mix of entertainment for all ages. i am looking forward to my next visit.', 'dynasty gave us a lovely stay. the rooms were comfortable and pillows lovely to sleep on. there was a good selection of buffet breakfast each day at h2o s. for us travelling with a 11 and 9 yr old they enjoyed the daily games the hotel offered and movie night with dinner. staff were lovely especially especially trippa who looked after us. they had two happy hours throughout the day and the food was good, flag service around the pool. kids loved the pools with the slides and beach just outside the resort. there was themed nights with entertainment that was good, we really didn t have to leave the resort when we stayed for the 7 nights. the only con i could say would be the internet isn t strong and doesn t often work which for my older children was a bit frustrating. and if you re looking for a quiet sleep in each morning that won t happen as the resort is very busy early in the morning with younger kids running and playing down the hall ways.', 'this was our first stop in bali family of 7 and only spent 2 nights here, however it was one of our favourite places to stay. the staff were super helpful friendly, there was great variety when it came to food options, and it was easy to walk everywhere. the village tour in the morning was great too. standouts for our family were the pools the breakfasts! thanks so much bali dynasty we ll be back!', 'great resort, good rooms, good location, service impeccable, gardens are amazing and overall a great resort irish pub is excellent. this is an amazing resort for children, and has a great kids club and baby sitting services are excellent.', 'we have just returned from 3 weeks in bali, we spent the last 6 days at the dynasty. pros staff were kind and quick to action any issues. kids club staff were lovely, particularly ari, he was amazing. our kids only went for an hour on 2 different days so we didn t utilize it much but the facilities were great and staff engaged with children. cons our first room stunk of mould dampness. we ve been to bali 4 times and stayed in many different accomodation types and never had that before. we paid more for a kids type room and the playstation didn t work not that we needed it though the bathroom was so run down shower over the bath, dirty in the corners and just so average. they happily moved us to the room facing the pool which was much better with no smell so that was good, but there s no way the price they charge is worth what they re offering. i thought there were more pools but there s only 2 and one is extremely deep so luckily our daughter is a confident swimmer. the food is ok, not great but not bad. the cocktails were sugary juice. breakfast is like a school camp with crying kids and parents yelling at said children. not relaxing at all. some muesli healthier options would be nice. lots of nannys in the kids pool with upset children which was really sad to see. also, i m not a complainer usually, for context i have traveled to many destinations and stayed at so many hotels villas etc and have never complained, but felt it necessary to give my honest thoughts to other families who might have similar expectations to us. so sadly we won t be back.', 'the resort is great with lots of activities for the kids and adults. the pool and water slide were great and kids made friends while at the pool. the food was great quality and a few options in or around the resort. rooms were comfortable, we stayed in a kids suite overlooking the garden. i am glad we were not overlooking the pool. staff were very friendly and helpful. some days there seemed to be not enough staff to cover around the pool and had to wait up to 30 mins for drinks and food which was only after we followed up twice it wasn t overly busy so not sure how they would cope if busy. the spa was great with people offering foot massages around the pool, however on 2 seperate occasions when having a massage in the spa the staff were giggling laughing during the treatment, which was inappropriate and off putting. my daughter felt very uncomfortable when it happened thinking they were laughing at her or me which may not have been the case but it felt that way . the entertainment was great in the evenings, with a magician, balinese dancers, films, fire dancers. kids club was good, although could have had some better activities options for tweens teens as advertised up to 16. waterbom park was a 6 minute walk away and the resort is only about 15 minutes from the airport. the main challenge was constantly being hassled by sellers just behind the resort and on the beach. it would have been better if the resort did not allow them in the space around the resort. overall we had a great time and would consider going back in the future thank you to all the staff that made our stay so good.', 'pros comfortable, well maintained and beautiful resort. very friendly staff and plenty for young kids to do. located in the middle of kuta, so close to the action. great water slide. able to cater for gluten free for a coeliac at breakfast has a separate gf station for breakfast and basic food for lunch dinner. the day spa was awesome skilled therapists in stunning surrounds. cons a couple of areas were no kids, including the swim up bar, which was disappointing. also, they advertise 4 pools, but 2 pools were restricted to use by patrons of pool side rooms and one was the kiddy pool, so really only one pool. overall a lovely stay.', 'we loved this resort for our family holiday! clean poolside facilities, friendly and helpful staff, fun activities, good variety of food choices. the refurbished kids club space was great, although the older age group didn t really follow the timetables activities. they did, however, get outside for all the outdoor pool activities, and the staff were great. the 9yo enjoyed kids club, and both enjoyed the pool and the plentiful number of sun lounges. we also really appreciated the location, the poolside music, the spa treatments, and all the offers the resort had. we travelled with a single friend who made good use of the adults only areas.', 'the staff were friendly and attentive. the breakfasts had a fantastic range of food. the kids pool and the kids club were great! we had a really good time as a family and will be back next year for another 2 weeks!', 'from the moment we walked in my hubby and i said we wouldn t stay here if we didn t have kids which is why it was l perfect for us with our two children 5 7 years old. it s not yuppy and fancy, we ve done that many times in seminyak. this hotel is a well oiled machine that has everything you need on site. the staff have an incredible loyalty to the hotel, you can tell they ve all been there for years and treat it like a home. they all know what they are doing and the place runs like clockwork. it s the best value hotel to stay if you have children and don t want to blow your budget. 4 aud kids meals by the pool, 8 aud cocktails that aren t like a fancy bar in melbourne but they are just what you need for a fun family holiday in bali. we ate the buffet breaky every morning and had the nasi goreng and mee goreng by the pool many time. no one got sick here. the kids club is tight but runs beautifully. the only thing that was really average and i didn t love was the spa on site. toilet was blocked, the massage was average and the shower handle that i used to wash the dripping massage oil off was broken so it was an ice cold shower after a relaxing massage. good thing it was only 40aud. highly recommend the foot massages by the pool though. so convenient while you watch the kids swim. and like 16aud for an hour! we tipped the ladies. thank you dynasty. we will return.', 'lovely resort with friendly, helpful staff, in all areas. facilities were everything we wanted, plenty of activities for children, a nice variety of restaurants on site. rooms clean and well serviced every day. would definitely stay here again!', 'we stayed at the dynasty after seeing how much was to offer in the hotel and how much it catered for kids. the activities were on all the time, themed buffet dinners, every service you want right in the hotel. i hardly saw my teen son because he was off making friends with all the staff. we hadn t even left and our kids were already planning our next trip.', 'amazing quick getaway. was able to have quality time with my family without a horrible price tag. kids loved all the activities we loved happy hour x2. room was clean was well suited to our needs. definitely will be rebooking.', 'we stayed in a pool access room for 9 nights in sep 2023. visually this place is lovely, the grounds and gardens are well kept and while there was a lot to love such as the great pools, kids club, sunken pool bar, comfy beds, there were a few things that missed the mark. the buffet breakfast was average at best and repetitive, juices weren t fresh, not enough variety for a 9 day stay. we had a family room but only 1 bathroom which was quite small. the room itself was run down and in need of an update. housekeeping alway forgot to top something up whether it was hand towels, soap, tissues, shower gel etc,. very annoying. lastly the ambience was very loud and busy, guests were predominantly bogan aussies, the music at the pool bar reflected this. overall we had a good stay here mainly due to the kids club which my 7 year old loved and was happy to spend a few hours there each day, they had indoor and outdoor activities, pizza making, movie night etc,. would recommend if you have young kids under 12 and don t mind noisy.', 'first time in bali and was a great experience at this resort. the staff were so accommodating and friendly. zero complaints, perfect for adults and children as there are wode variety of activities for all ages. food was great, some restaurants were pricy but room service was cheap. happy hour was a daily occurrence being half price cocktails 3.50 aud for a cocktail, you can t complain they all tasted amazing! thank you staff at dynasty for giving me a great first time in bali', 'excellent hotel for a young family. they had great facilities, the kids loved the playground and water slides, and they had full time life guard on duty in all areas. as well as that, excellent food, and activities happening throughout the day. there s also a private beach which is great. i really couldn t recommend this place highly enough.', 'we had a great time, staff is so helpful breakfast buffet had good choices, we had 1 dinner buffet which was pizza and pasta night, that was average. there were some problems with the toilet etc but they fixed it straightaway overall will definitely go again and stay at this place', 'this is an amazing resort, especially for families. what makes it even better is the staff. every single employee at the resort works tirelessly to ensure your stay is amazing. special mention to yuni at front reception who was just incredible.']

places. hotel ambience is good but hospitality is not up to mark. we ordered small amount of cutlery like plates and spoon and waited till 2hours still they didn t send even after so many calls.gave and he started violently vomiting, called for the doctor who gave him an injection but it didn t help at all, 8.5 hours of hell through the night with him vomiting and stomach cramps. didn t realise until home and looked at the pic of his burger and noticed the bacon was raw which obviously gave him food poisoning. i emailed the resort with pictures and requested them to reimburse me for the doctors bill his food of 200 aust, they denied it was their fault saying how safe they are with their food and offered next time a food bev credit on our next stay, unfortunately we wont be returning after our experience, so i again requested a refund they never replied. very poor service from a well know establishment and considering the photo clearly shows uncooked bacon and was the pubs fault he was so sick. not happy at all this was our 8th visit and bali dynasty did not disappoint. the room we stayed in was comfortable although the bathroom looked a little tired. the staff was amazing. no request was too much. from the moment we checked in to the when we checked out we were made to feel welcome. the spa offers a good variety of treatments to suit everyone s needs. the newly renovated kids club looks amazing. the bfast has good variety and the staff will try to accomodate any requests. the teamed meals could be better but overall still pleasant. the resort has a good mix breakfast each day at h2o s. for us travelling with a 11 and 9 yr old they enjoyed the daily games the hotel offered and movie night with dinner. staff were lovely especially especially trippa who looked after us. they had two happy hours throughout the day and the food was good, flag service around the pool. kids loved the pools with the slides and beach just outside the resort. there was themed nights with entertainment that was good, we really didn t have to leave the resort when we stayed for the 7 nights. the only con i could say would be the internet isn t strong and doesn t often work which for my older children was a bit frustrating. and if you re looking for a quiet sleep in each morning that won t happen as the resort is very busy early in the morning with younger kids running and playing down the hall ways. this was our first stop in bali family of 7 and only spent 2 nights here, however it was one of our favourite places to stay. the staff were super helpful friendly, there was great variety when it came to food options, and it was easy to walk everywhere. the village tour in the morning was great too. standouts for our family were the pools the breakfasts! thanks so much bali dynasty we ll be back! great resort, good rooms, good location, service impeccable, gardens are amazing and overall a great resort irish pub is excellent. this is an amazing resort for children, and has a great kids club and baby sitting services are excellent. we have just returned from 3 weeks in bali, we spent the last 6 days at the dynasty. pros staff were kind and quick to action any issues. kids club staff were lovely, particularly ari, he was amazing. our kids only went for an hour on 2 different days so we didn t utilize it much but the facilities were great and staff engaged with children. cons our first room stunk of mould dampness. we ve been to bali 4 times and stayed in many different accomodation types and never had that before. we paid more for a kids type room and the playstation didn t work not that we needed it though the bathroom was so run down shower over the bath, dirty in the corners and just so average. they happily moved us to the room facing the pool which was much better with no smell so that was good, but there s no way the price they charge is worth what they re offering. i thought there were more pools but there s only 2 and one is extremely deep so luckily our daughter is a confident swimmer. the food is ok, not great but not bad. the cocktails were sugary juice. breakfast is like a school camp with crying kids and parents yelling at said children. not relaxing at all. some muesli healthier options would be nice. lots of nannys in the kids pool with upset children which was really sad to see. also, i m not a complainer usually, for context i have traveled to many destinations and stayed at so many hotels villas etc and have never complained, but felt it necessary to give my honest thoughts to other families who might have similar expectations to us. so sadly we won t be back. the resort is great with lots of activities for the kids and adults. the pool and water slide were great and kids made friends while at the pool. the food was great quality and a few options in or around the resort. rooms were comfortable, we stayed in a kids suite overlooking the garden. i am glad we were not overlooking the pool. staff were very friendly and helpful. some days there seemed to be not enough staff to cover around the pool and had to wait up to 30 mins for drinks and food which was only after we followed up twice it wasn t overly busy so not sure how they would cope if busy. the spa was great with people offering foot massages around the pool, however on 2 seperate occasions when having a massage in the spa the staff were giggling laughing during the treatment, which was inappropriate and off putting. my daughter felt very uncomfortable when it happened thinking they were laughing at her or me which may not have been the case but it felt that way . the entertainment was great in the evenings, with a magician, balinese dancers, films, fire dancers. kids club was good, although could have had some better activities options for tweens teens as advertised up to 16. waterbom park was a 6 minute walk away and the resort is only about 15 minutes from the airport. the main challenge was constantly being hassled by sellers just behind the resort and on the beach. it would have been better if the resort did not allow them in the space around the resort. overall we had a great time and would consider going back in the future thank you to all the staff that made our stay so good. pros comfortable, well maintained and beautiful resort. very friendly staff and plenty for young kids to do. located in the middle of kuta, so close to the action. great water slide. able to cater for gluten free for a coeliac at breakfast has a separate gf station for breakfast and basic food for lunch dinner. the day spa was awesome skilled therapists in stunning surrounds. cons a couple of areas were no kids, including the swim up bar, which was disappointing. also, they advertise 4 pools, but 2 pools were restricted to use by patrons of pool side rooms and one was the kiddy pool, so really only one pool. overall a lovely stay. we loved this resort for our family holiday! clean poolside facilities, friendly and helpful staff, fun activities, good variety of food choices. the refurbished kids club space was great, although the older age group didn t really follow the timetables activities. they did, however, get outside for all the outdoor pool activities, and the staff were great. the 9yo enjoyed kids club, and both enjoyed the pool and the plentiful number of sun lounges. we also really appreciated the location, the poolside music, the spa treatments, and all the offers the resort had. we travelled with a single friend who made good use of the adults only areas. the staff were friendly and attentive. the breakfasts had a fantastic range of food. the kids pool and the kids club were great! we had a really good time as a family and will be back next year for another 2 weeks! from the moment we walked in my hubby and i said we wouldn t stay here if we didn t have kids which is why it was l perfect for us with our two children 5 7 years can tell they ve all been there for years and treat it like a home. they all know what they are doing and the place runs like clockwork. it s the best value hotel to stay if you have children and don t want to blow your budget. 4 aud kids meals by the pool, 8 aud cocktails that aren t like a fancy bar in melbourne but they are just what you need for a fun family holiday in bali. we ate the buffet t love was the spa on site. toilet was blocked, the massage was average and the shower handle that i used to wash the dripping massage oil off was broken so it was an ice cold shower after a relaxing massage. good thing it was only 40aud. highly recommend the foot massages by the pool though. so convenient while you watch the kids swim. and like 16aud for an hour! we tipped the ladies. thank you dynasty. we will return. lovely resort with friendly, helpful staff, in all areas. facilities were everything we wanted, plenty of activities for children, a nice variety of restaurants on site. rooms clean and well serviced every day. would definitely stay here again! we stayed at the dynasty after seeing how much was to offer in the hotel and how much it catered for kids. the activities were on all the time, themed buffet dinners, every service you want right in the hotel. i hardly saw my teen son because he was off making friends with all the staff. we hadn t even left and our kids were already planning our next trip. amazing quick getaway. was able to have quality time with my family without a horrible price tag. kids loved all the activities we loved happy hour x2. room was clean was well suited to our needs. definitely will be rebooking. we stayed in a pool access room for 9 nights in sep 2023. visually this place is lovely, the grounds and gardens are well kept and while there was a lot to love such as the great pools, kids club, sunken pool bar, comfy beds, there were a few things that missed the mark. the buffet breakfast was average at best and repetitive, juices weren t fresh, not enough variety for a 9 day stay. we had a family room but only 1 bathroom which was quite small. the room itself was run down and in need of an update. housekeeping alway forgot to top something up whether it was hand towels, soap, tissues, shower gel etc,. very annoying. lastly the ambience was very loud and busy, guests were predominantly bogan aussies, the music at the pool bar reflected this. overall we had a good stay here mainly due to the kids club which my 7 year old loved and was happy to spend a few hours there each day, they had indoor and outdoor activities, pizza making, movie night etc,. would recommend if you have young kids under 12 and don t mind noisy. first time in bali and was a great experience at this resort. the staff were so accommodating and friendly. zero complaints, perfect for adults and children as there are wode variety of activities for all ages. food was great, some restaurants were pricy but room service was cheap. happy hour was a daily occurrence being half price cocktails 3.50 aud for a cocktail, you can t complain they all tasted amazing! thank you staff at dynasty for giving me a great first time in bali excellent hotel for a young family. they had great facilities, the kids loved the playground and water slides, and they had full time life guard on duty in all areas. as well as that, excellent food, and activities happening throughout the day. there s also a private beach which is great. i really couldn t recommend this place highly enough. we had a great time, staff is so helpful breakfast buffet had good choices, we had 1 dinner buffet which was pizza and pasta night, that was average. there were some problems with the toilet etc but they fixed it straightaway overall will definitely go again and stay at this place this is an amazing resort, especially for families. what makes it even better is the staff. every single employee at the resort works tirelessly to ensure your stay is amazing. special mention to yuni at front reception who was just incredible.
summary: buffet breaky was great, we stayed for 9 days so we got a book of vouchers with discounted food cocktails and washing, will be booking again, the hotel is awesome, staff unbelievable, services, food, pools, cleanliness, all the little extras, rooms, location, honestly couldnt find 1 fault, loads of bars, restaurants, gym was great, slides for kids, kids club, activities, unreal came to dynasty to stay with other family with younger children, great place for kids and a great hotel overall with great staff and good food, we only stayed the 4 nights, would have loved to stay longer. if things what you want then this is the place for you treated like kings and queens at the bali dynasty absolutely everything about this place was stellar the rooms, the pool, the bar and many restaurants but the real gem of this place is the amazing amazing staff whether it s the cleaner , the waitress , the pool lifeguards , the reception staff or even the maintenance crew, every single staff member has the most authentic smile and demeanor they generally care for the patrons welfare and do everything and anything to make sure your stay is enjoyable and safe it s a five out of five stars from my family the service was great and staff very friendly. our 5th return to bali dynasty and again did not disappoint a beautiful hotel with wonderful attentive staff, the resort has beautiful gardens and fabulous pools with lots of poolside entertainment for everyone the rooms are great and very clean plenty of choices for food and beverages at very reasonable prices and happy hour twice a day is always a hit lots of shopping and restaurants right out as you step into the street a must stay for families coming to bali yet another 10 10 from us we usually stay at cheaper hotels in bali, but were bringing the kids and booked 2 rooms.
bart_summary: The dynasty is one of Australia's most popular hotels. The hotel is located in the heart of the city of bali.

booking website states otherwise.and when i did pay for it.. it was always cutting out. breakfast is average, not a full buffet only a few hot food selections. mostly yogurt, toast and fruit. they do wifi free in the lobby but pay in te room. food is ok depend what you order .. avoid beef on that island anyway although i like bali gardens we wont be going back .staff all very good helpful . the weddings drives us mad the music is loud , takes over the restaurant .how many weddings do you need a week or two . we are over them wont be going back . they also need to have free wifi it cost me 5 a day not right leew i picked this hotel for quick holiday as the location is really accesible. room average size. simple and minimum amenities. the pillows were not comfortable as it looks worn out. the furniture is very simple. the room i got had no view at all, facing the wall of the restaurant building. it was bit noisy. restaurant they have some restaurants that are at the busy street level and one is at the beach side. the view is nice in the beach restaurant. the food is just ok. in fact, i was expecting something better, but the hotel did not become my favorite. the hotel lacks of the resort feeling. with limited time to pick a resort i opted for somewhere close and central to kuta as i had some traveling with me that had not been to bali before. given the option i would not have stayed in kuta but this was our choice of hotel to stay in. i was pretty disappointed with some of the hotels amenities having the shower go cold on us often when in it and the water turn a dark brown colour on some occasions. it appeared that it could have been the time of day, in the afternoon it seemed to go cold and i was wondering if too many were using the system at the same time? it was also disappointing that wifi is only in the lobby. having younger siblings travel with me i checked in often with my mum to let her know we were all okay and i had to go to the lobby everytime or the other option was to pay to have it in your room. i think bali gardens, to progress, should introduce wifi to all areas of the hotel. the breakfast buffet was average but that is me comparing it to others i have been too and i didn t like that you had to pay for pool towels on using them. you did get the money back once they were returned but thought that was an interesting thing to do, have never had to do that in a hotel prior. unfortunately i don t think i will return to bali gardens. don t get me wrong it was central, the staff were lovely but i just didn t love the hotel. if you are looking for a 5 star resort ..this is not it. the rooms are basic and and so are the bathrooms, but they are clean. the gardens are lovely, very tropical and lush. breakfast in the morning is on the basic side. this hotel is geared for families and not couples, it is very noisy. the staff i will say are very friendly and polite. the up side is the spa is very inexpensive and i was having 1hour long massages everyday this was my highlight . would i stay again . probably not. probably 3.5 would be a more accurate rating this is not a bad hotel but there s just too many small things which let it down. the resorts location is great as everyone says. it has a real balinese feel with it s mainly wood foyer and beautifully manicured tropical gardens. direct access to kuta beach from the back of the resort is a bonus and watching the sunset from the boardwalk restaurant, which has a good menu and is well priced, is amazing and much more laid back and cheaper than going to ku de ta! the main pool is great for families with a kids pool and a nice swim up bar however it does get very busy and noisy so for us the much quieter and more comfortable adults pool the daybeds around the main pool are packed like sardines and have no cushions was ideal. very relaxing. breakfast was very average with lukewarm tar for coffee and limited options throughout our 10 day stay. special mention for the ladies in the egg station who consistently did a great job under much pressure. the main indoor breakfast area was very busy and loud but if you can get a seat outside it was much more pleasant and there is an option to have an a la carte breakfast upstairs at no extra cost. we booked a deluxe room and had asked for a room on the ground floor near the pools and that s exactly what we got. the room was large, clean and very comfortable as was the bed. the shower room no bath didn t have the best lighting but again was clean. on the down side no free wifi in your room. we paid 5 a day for wifi for much of the time it was like travelling back in time to dial up internet, taking ages to connect, shockingly slow speed and constantly dropping out. and the free wifi in public areas was just as bad. i could get much faster more reliable access in any of the local bars restaurants for the price of a bintang. this really is ridiculous in this day age. then there s the towel issues. the fact that most were old threadbare with almost every one we had having stains was bad but the system of paying a 5 deposit having to pick up or exchange your towel from a shop in the foyer which is a few hundred metres from any of the pools is not good. i don t want to sound too critical, the resort has a lot going for it we really did enjoy our stay. with just a few minor changes we would be happy to stay again. the only good thing about this hotel was location which was very centralized. the room was run down and the reception did not honor my request for quite room provided that i am a light sleeper, they put me on the corner room where the exhaust fan of the neighbor mall was located. managed to get move to another room yet still noise however better than the 1st option. i was in shocked when the reception told upon check in that their check out time is 10 am, seeing my face expression the lady informed again that the latest by 12 noon. breakfast spread was disappointing as if it was 2 or 3 star budget hotel there was no many options, however the staffs at the breakfast were polite and very attentive. the worst thing was you have to pay for the wifi inside your room but comp on lobby also there was no mobile signal inside the room. will i stay in this hotel again, thanks but no. well where do i start well yes let me comment on this bali hotel which we will avoid on future trips to bali, the upside is the location which is very good, the downside started at check in, normally check in is at 1400 hrs in all the hotels we have stayed in, so we arrived for the 1400 hrs check in only to be told that check in was at 1500 hrs, so we sat in the steamy hot foyer until our room was ready. they did offer an upgrade so we asked to view the room. wow it was in the basement with a view of rubble and was not at all fresh looking, thanks but no thanks. the room we were allocated had a balcony but it was an effort to open the door as the handle needed replacing, i called for maintenance and he did attempt to fix it but it was only a band aid repair. now when you want a towel for the pool you have to obtain it from a small kiosk at reception, walk down the stairs into the garden area and it is quite a walk to the pool. now if you wish for a fresh towel you have to walk all the way back to reception, wait until other people buy drinks etc then obtain another towel and walk all the way back to the pool area, so why not have towels at the pool like every other organised hotel. now the toilets at the pool are never attended to so you leave the pool walk through wet urine on the floor of the toilet and then stroll back to the pool and guess what, yes your urine coated feet enter the pool and contaminate the water. they have a 5 day charge for wifi in the room which is free in all the hotels we have used, the wifi in the lobby is free, so guess what ! yes the lobby is full of guests using the free wifi in hot steamy conditions. they should be using free wifi in the air conditioned comfort of their room. now the loungers at the pool, well they are so tightly packed together that even houdini would have battled to get onto one, no tables at the side of any loungers for drinks. the pool area is very rarely tidy with leaves and small branches consistently visible on the ground, in fact i cut my ankle with one of the twigs on the ground should have went to specsavers the infinity pool looks great but after testing the water we decided that we would get pool shock so without a wet suit forget it!! we did walk on the beach at the rear of this hotel, but it was so dirty and covered in washed up debris that we gave it up as a bad idea. now the manager of this hotel needs to be trained in the hospitality bussiness or should find employment in another industry as he has not a clue how to run a hotel or maybe he should pay a visit to one of the swiss bell resorts and they will show him how it is done. no problems with the staff as they were very courteous. gordon ramsay would have a field day in there so, no we would highly recommend giving this disaster a miss, so many more hotels to choose from you do not need to put up with this inferior mess this review is for the rest only........... we ate there last night, the duck spring rolls were excellent, but i ordered the seafood platter for main... very disappointing.. it was more a plate than a platter and very greasy and the prawns were hard to eat without getting a mouth full of shell... the coconut bruelle had a lot of coconut in it..... we do prefer the classic version... and surprisingly enough they managed to make a banana split with very little taste to it... i had one the night before at ours rest in garlic lane and it was fantastic... wed only go back to enjoy the sunset and view of the bay an people and maybe have a beer, some duck spring rolls maybe mayb try something diff off the menu... but noo more platters for me... the place looks nice but didnt enjoy my stay that much as it was rainy, and a lot of fallen leaves found on the pool and grassfield outside. also toilet in our room doesnt have a bidet spray and a rag on the floor. but.. buffet dinner was great. reception and customer service are actively responsive to our requests. refreshment is unlimited on the lobby. and room have a safe to store your valuable items. i stayed in this resort as i was meeting friends already booked. such a culture shock after being in ubud. while the staff were very pleasant and helpful i really felt i could have been anywhere. so, if you are after just shopping it will be fine but i feel you like to get away from it all stay away. i am very disappointed and feel as if i still need a holiday. meals at the boardwalk cafe were terrific, pools are lovely but yes a resort andrew i could have been on the gold coast. the bed was two joined together and one side dipped with a hard timber ridge in between. the air con was noisy and i didn t sleep much. i am staying here now and can make these comments from current experience. i m no newcomer to bali and have experienced many two three star establishments that have better and free wifi. we were given a voucher on check in that provided for 1 hour of wifi for our whole 7 days. after that you could purchase for 5 per day. this place offered free in the lobby only and not in the rooms. standing room only around breakfast time when people are trying to check emails etc. it is so poor that i had emails coming through days after they had been sent. imessages regularly failed to send. obviously i am writing this review outside of the resort where strong and free wifi is readily available. stayed here most staff are great except for the pool supervisor i went to the bathroom somebody took the towel of my bed when i spoke to the supervisor and he said i should bring with me to the bathroom never heard anything like that in my life so if you get a towel from the hotel which you have to put a deposit down for make sure you take it to the bathroom with you first time staying at the bali garden. as soon as we got out of the taxi, there was a concierge ready to take our bags up to the reception. staff at the check in were very friendly and checked in quickly. however they charged my credit card for the room after telling me literally a minute prior that they were not going to charge my credit card and that i could pay cash! the hotel is big and the pathway to all the rooms on the left of the hotel overlook a part of the hotel that is dirty and laden with trash at the side of discovery mall so its pretty uninspiring to look at. we asked for a room close to the pool, and they gave us exactly that, on the second floor room 361 so they got that spot on! however there was no view at all!!! the various trees in front of the room balcony are at that perfect height that they block any view to the garden and pool areas. there is also a massive side room window no other rooms would have this except this spot and rooms above but also blocked by palm branches. so the entire view was just looking at tree and palm branches. they really need to trim some of the palms back so you can get a view of something. we paid extra for a garden view, we should have saved the money and paid for a street facing room. the garden areas were nice ish all the shrubs and small flowering plants were nicely manicured, whilst the many ponds are in desperate need of cleaning. the water was murky and dirty. i m surprised that the fish could even see where they are swimming! there was one section where fruit from the tree above had dropped into the pond and were just accumulating and rotting in the water. it looked really horrible. we also tried to get a massage at both of the hotels two spa s, however they said they were fully booked out for the next 3 hours. there was only 6 staff to service a hotel with over 300 rooms! not great! however the real jewel of this hotel is the outdoor pools and dining areas stretching from the middle of the hotel to the very beach front. 1 lovely big spa pool with independently operable jets, 1 large family pool with swim up bar and multiple seating and tables built into the pool for in pool drinking sessions. 1 large pool at the beach side with wonderful views across kuta beach all the way to canggu. sipping cocktails and watching the sunset from here is a great way to spend the afternoon. our bed was average, the pillows were woefully small and the a c made a bit of a racket which we couldn t silence, but for 2 days it was bearable. staff seem really nice and attentive. the restaurants at both the front and rear of the hotel are quite exceptional at service and getting meals out quickly. i only had 2 days here, so i think that with a bit more time, i could find more positives and negatives. i d like to stay again, however for the price, i believe there are better options. we booked and paid for 6 nights...we arrived 2am saturday morning taken to our room or should i say cell the bed was hard the pillows were so flat you could barely see them, only a sheet on the bed no blankets at all. breakfast was all cold and the only place we could use wifi was in the foyer. so after 7 hours at this hotel we checked out. they didn t care we were not happy or try to offer us a better room and definitely would not offer any type of refund. the whole resort facility is old fashioned and the pool water looked not very clean. the near by beach is really dirty and you can see all the garbage floated to the beach. one of the counter staff was not very nice and booked our transport to airport with a wrong time. the inter connecting rooms are quite convenient for family travelling with infants. the bed sheets were in grey color and you could see the stains on it. good location to access kuta commercial street very easily. this is an ok hotel and if you re on a budget and looking for family friendly place then this would be a good place for you location is great it s right on the beach and short walk to kuta art markets the pools good they also have an adults only pool but only open until 6 pm at night and you are lucky if you can get a chair because they get taken pretty quickly and although they have a sign saying cannot reserve chair for longer than 30 minutes they don t enforce it breakfast is nothing fancy but perfectly fine air conditioning in rooms is fantastic but room itself was nothing much,pretty basic and mattress not so comfortable have stayed at bgh on and off since 2005, when it was the original resort before the up grade. back then you could just hope over the garden and be on the beach. once the upgrade was complete with a great pool out the front you sit on the beach chair and lookout onto the beach all day. in the last few years you are no long able to have beach chairs out near the front pool, the reason is the grass is under construction. this was also the case in september last year, how long does it take for grass to grow. if you want to swim in the front pool you need to walk through the middle of the restaurant to get to it. my children put down a towel on the grass near the front bar and was told they are not allowed to sit on the grass. i found this just ridiculous. they where told they need to sit in the main pool area. the main pool no longer has any beach umbrella to shade you during the middle of the day and the chairs are so packed in, to sit down you are required to climb up your chair. this is a beach resort and patrons should be able to sit in beach chairs around the pool and look over the beach. if have stayed in many places in bali and the wifi is an issue at bgh being only accessible in public areas. this is a little behind the time for a resort this size. would i stay there again the answer would be yes. the staff are second to none and make your stay enjoyable. i think bgh is catering more for weddings and the boardwalk restaurant. i understand these are money makers but surely the tables in front of the front pool could be removed during the day and then moved back by 5pm for dinner guests. come on bgh please consider the guests as much as the visitors. we have stayed at bali gardens previously, and our groups tradition if we stay there or not is to have dinner at their fabulous boardwalk cafe restaurant on our last night before flying out. the staff were and are always amazing, but despite it being low season and hotel was not full we were placed in a far wing with the exhaust from the restaurant in front of the hotel rumbling all night??! free wifi was only available in the lobby, so competition was high for seats. we have stayed in much cheaper hotels in the area with wifi everywhere, that combined with several minor maintenance issues left us disappointed. on the up side the location is good and is on the beachfront, the cafe is amazing as are the pools, we had relaxing time in the day spa which was very nice
summary: the restaurants in the resort werent too good..we tried 4 of them..the wok mie was nice to dine in,but room service menu is different,limited and all room service add on extra money per item plus another percentage for some service fee.we ordered a room service wok mie..not good..a pizza,terrible undercooked,almost raw base..the ham was torn up spam type meat,a few bits of pinapple and lumps of cheese,hardly melted or cooked..and overpriced.. drinks at poolbar.. 9 for a vodka cruiser.. 4 bintang..go to mini mart,pick up cruisers for 3 4,bintang 2..kids splashing and jumping around in the pool bar area amongst adults drinking..service slow,especially food .. 3 afternoons they had weddings functions on at 4pm and would kick everyone out of the infinity pool and seating lawn area overlooking the beach around 2 pm,so it was swim with all the kids getting splashed at pool bar or in the spa pool with limited seating... we booked into tari spa for a massage and facial,reasonably priced and a good massage...except we were put outside,in the heat no aircon,in an undercovered open air hut area,bamboo blinds that gave little to no privacy while undressing and laying part naked on the massage tables..right in the middle of the garden next to a main pathway,across from the spa pool..far from quiet and relaxing..gardeners working noisely,snipping pruning,hosing,talking loudly..other people walking past talking,slapping thongs,kids running past yelling.the therapists were constantly interupted by the phone or people just walking in enquiring or they were talking whispering to each other...the area was dirty,flys and insects buzzing around and i spent my time looking at a dirty floor,a cocnut shell with dirt and some fake flowers or the thatched ceiling with a couple of wasp nests..not relaxing,not quiet,not cool hot and sticky,not pleasant.. we stayed 6 nights and payed extra in an apparent upgraded garden view room.it wasnt worth the extra 25 a nite..i looked at a wall,a green pond and the breakfast dining room.. the whole room needs a good scrub and a total overhaul..bathroom was putrid,smelt like raw sewer most of the time,mould around bath grouting,shower curtain mouldy..the shower had no pressure,and hardly any water came out..very difficult to wash your body,let alone hair..the lighting in the bathroom was extremely poor aircon did not work properly..had maintenance come twice..first time they changed batteries in remote,second time they fiddled around outside...it just was not working properly. found a seats on ocean front beautiful as we usually do.not busy at all .no service after 10 mins we had to go and ask for a menu...still no service had to call a lady over to take our drink order then had to call someone to take our lunch order..could of just walked out no one asked if we d like more drinks which we absolutely would have but no not even a bill had to get up and ask ...shocking we picked the family room two bunks one trundle and one king the room was large enough but the quality of the mattress where so poor the bunks and trundle mattresses where about 3 inches depths so the bars underneath could be felt by even the youngest child 7 we had to get extra pilllows and put them underneath which didn t do to much the hotel needs to be revamped to keep its price it charging this hotel only has the beach to offer the pool so small ok, so my family and i travel a lot. sure sign of financial distress of a hotel if you have been to bali before and stayed in a good 3 star hotel there a plenty around you will probably be disappointed with this one, breakfast buffet isnt varied, staff are pretty rude our toilet kept getting blocked up our safe got jammed closed we requested to be in rooms next to our friends when the booking was made 6 months ago and couldn t be accomadated no wifi in the rooms all in all not a bad stay but definitely over priced for outdated property that a has a good location spa was well priced checked in today after a lengthy trip in from the gili s, bit disappointed to be told our superior suite was sold out and we would be upgraded to a family suite, a basement room near the stairwell is not an upgrade !
bart_summary: Hotel was clean and a good size, the bed was like sleeping on wood, not comfortable, pillows good. The mexican restaurant that they own next door was a bit of a let down.

combined_reviews: beautiful atmosphere and we had wonderful experiences at bambu indah. definitely we will recommend to our friends. charming staff they always ready to help. shoutout to wati for guided us to the restaurant xixixixi i enjoy this place a lot, the natural pools is very refreshing and you don t have to take anothe bath coz it s zero chemical!!! ask for him. the experience at bambu indah is something that you will never forget. thanks for everything wati nana,from the reception devi from the restaurant very nice people and they treated us very very good!! beach, you won t waste money on blue taxis if you stay here. we stayed in chamlung mas for 12 days, and apart from our airport transfers, we only used a blue taxi once to go to bali mall galleria. keeping in mind the purpose of our visit was a completely relaxing holiday, not sightseeing or running about all over bali. we were able to walk 30 40 minutes in any direction and get where we wanted to go, restaurants, kuta beach, the bali bomb memorial, bintang supermarket, etc the resort itself is quite old, so don t expect a beautifully presented 5 star experience here, but in saying that this resort has reasonably priced, comfortable rooms with good air conditioning and i can t speak for other rooms but our poolside room 120 had the best cold working fridge of any resort we have ever stayed in, including the ones in 5 star rated resorts. upon arrival, we found our room reasonably clean, although the bathrooms could use some tlc. there is plenty of mould in the shower and around the bathroom in general. beds are very hard and uncomfortable, so if you like a soft mattress, perhaps specify this at the start of your stay and see what they can do for you if anything. the rooms come equipped with a kettle, tea coffee making facilities, a mini fridge, and a safe. 2 bottles of water were provided daily as well. there is a cheap laundry on the lane outside near pad on bene and paradise spa, also in the same location, which is well worth a visit or many. the housekeeping staff in this resort do an excellent job, well, the majority of them anyway. our main housekeeper, desa, was incredible. nothing was any trouble for her, she cleaned our room spotlessly every day and something we noticed one day while we were out by the pool was that if she had to leave the room while she was cleaning, perhaps for towels or such, she went out and locked the door behind her, she didn t just leave the room open if she left. now, this may seem like a trivial thing, but we thought this was an excellent practice, and it showed she cared to keep things safe for our belongings while she was taking care of the room. we did have 1 other housekeeper for 1 day during our stay, i do not know his name, but he was terrible and very lazy. all he did in our room was change our towels. he didn t empty rubbish, sweep floors, tidy, or change sheets. a very lazy young man, we were glad to see desa back the following day. there are 2 pools in this hotel we only used the one on the bottom level as that s where our room was. nice and clean and well maintained. my only criticism of the pool area was that the pool staff did not lay out the pool beds on the lounges every day, and for the first 2 days we were there, everyone was just sitting on the hard, uncomfortable beds. then, 1 day, we saw a mattress appear on one of the beds, and it wasn t long before everyone realised you had to ask for one, and with a bit of reluctance, they would bring 1 out for you. why don t they just put them out every day? i m not sure it s not like they have a hundred day beds around the pool. we took this room with breakfast not included, but the hotel does have a good restaurant, and you can combined_reviews: firstly, the positives. the location is fantastic, it is central and really close to a lot of great restaurants and shopping and other attractions. the staff here are absolutely lovely and really do go out of their way to be helpful. pool area on the ground floor is gorgeous and we spent many an afternoon there enjoying a cocktail. the food in the lobby restaurant is basic, but fresh and tasty and the staff in there are excellent. staff at the front desk are also able to exchange money at a good rate. the negatives the hotel is getting quite tired. it is very obviously in need of repairs and it does reflect on the rooms. we went as a family with 2 x separate deluxe rooms. our room was small half the size of the other deluxe room on the same booking that wasn t too much of a problem though, you don t spend much time in the room so weren t too worried by that. the parts that did bother us though the air conditioning in our room just would not work. it did not matter what temp you set it on or what you did with it, it just would not cool the room down. we didn t manage to sleep much as the room was so incredibly hot all the time. we would actually lay down on the tiled floor to try and cool down. we did mention it at the front desk a few times but nothing was done and we kind of gave up trying. the door to our room also would not close properly...my hubby worked out how to get it to close eventually, but it would still kinda pop open sometimes. doesn t do much for your sense of security when the front door won t secure properly! again, the staff here particularly at the front desk and the housekeeping staff really do an excellent job and champlung mas provides great value for money at the price point it is at and we have already booked to stay again in september this year. just hoping this years room has working ac and a door that locks properly and i can up my review to 5 stars . absolutely terrible. rooms smelt mouldy and multiple leaks in them. bedroom cupboard was broken requested to fix it, it was never once repaired during our stay. pool towels smelt mouldy too. however the staff are wonderful people so kind and accommodating. for them it would be a 5 star review so let s start from the start kettle not included in room rate water leaking from light fitting in bathroom air conditioning not working requested 4 days to be fixed staff at reception could not careless when it rains outside water from ceiling run down wall onto tiles deep water very slippery had fallen over due this major breech of personal security after this it took me 8 hours to speak to manger got the normal rubbish so sorry yes did change rooms but it was all to late as i could no longer guarantee my safety in this hotel i booked a return flight to my own country i lost 7 days accommodation due to the hotel incompetency any professional hotel would offer a refund due to all issues caused the unprofessional behaviour of the hotel staff under no circumstances stay here if you valve safety what a dump. front desk are useless, the hotel is a death trap. doors don t lock properly. booked a deluxe superior pool room and was put on the 4th floor at the very end. someone accessed my room via the balcony and stole my money. changed rooms...the bath tap ran so hot it burnt my foot leaving me unable to go for a swim or even walk the streets. left after 5 days into a 15 day trip. no refund offered, just a lunch at their restaurant. never again. stayed over xmas in 3 x ground floor deluxe pool side rooms. this hotel is not terrible, but it s very average. i d rate it 2 star as we normally stay 3 star in bali . it s in need of some major renovations. rooms and bathrooms are very old and run down. did not eat at the hotel. we paid a premium price because it was over xmas, but wouldn t stay again. this place is off the richter scale, where do i begin actually i know, i show up to the hotel and boy oh boy the render on the building looks like it was put on by a blind ten year old. lobby was quite nice but that s the only positive i have for this place. i get to my room and the stench of p ss fills my nostril s at this point i would rather sleep on kuta beach amongst the rabid dogs. i have a look in the bathroom and fark me dead the grout has been chewed by a squirrel and spat between the gaps in conrad a real shame. pros 1 big pool plenty of beach chairs 2 room is of good size, bathroom is beautiful biggest con poor service most of the staff seem to be trainees do not know what to do. incident 1 at check in i arrived just before 3pm was first told to wait as my room was currently being prepared. when asked how long i need to wait, i was told there was no room available they needed to check would not know how long i had to wait. i understand the check in time is 3pm but surely they can have a better idea if room would be ready at 3pm or later. incident 2 while waiting for my room to be ready, i headed to beach restaurant eight degrees south for lunch. i had to repeat explain the order on the menu to the waitress at least 5 8x as she did not understand. everything was clearly indicated on the menu that for capresse salad, can replace boccincini cheese to burrata. she did not understand what the menu said. i then ordered a beer asked for the wine list. she said she needed to check disappeared without coming back. i then had 3 other different servers, all equally clueless! surely the staff must be trained on what is available on the menu! incident 3 had to order unlikely! i suggest you take your spend it elsewhere with better service.', 'staff was very friendly and helpful, but the overall stay was unsatisfying. we shortened our stay and spent our last night caught the right angle, and the windows were extremely dirty. had to go back and ask them to double check and change rooms for us. 2 their spa is not at all what you pay for. at approx.100usd per person for 90 min, we expected to get pampered. however, they do not have an exclusive spa area! we were walked to the first floor of the suites, and had massages in suite rooms. 3 our second night was in their ocean view suite room, they told us no one booked the room above us, however, there was noise above the whole night and we saw lights and tv on. after 2 days of investigation, they told us a service team was their fixing some issues in that room, which sounds doubtful. it is more probable that staff stayed in the empty room, or the room was booked without showing up in their system. either way shows that there is something seriously wrong with the management. all in all, there are better managed resorts in the area with a wider beach front, in similar price range.', 'really not what we would expect from a 5 star resort. rooms were nice but toilet smelt constantly and shower was hot and cold low to high pressure constantly. had 2 lunches and 3 dinners and all of them were poor in some fashion including but not limited to wrong food delivered, 2 hour wait to be served, entrees and mains served at the same time, steak served rare and well done on the same plate, excuses were only 4 types of canap each everyday, compared with a buffet spread of spring rolls, crab chuwammushi, chicken rolls, veloute, 4 types of crackers, 2 types of salads jellyfish and seaweed , fish croquette, potato croquettes, cookies, among others in the club lounge on the wednesday. i made a complaint to the duty manager on thursday to say that we were disappointed that families were discriminated against in such a blatant way. at check in, we were assured that we would receive all of the benefits offered in the club lounge but this was not the case. he promised that it would be different from thursday but it was in fact even worse on thursday, the cocktail offering was again just 4 canap including one spicy beef cubes, which is of course not suitable for many young children. i have also written to the gm but have received no reply. the duty manager has also not checked back with me to see if i m satisfied with how the complaint was handled. the lobby lounge is also next to the block that is being renovated and hence, families had to put up with hammering renovation noise at least the first three days that we were there. it was hard to understand why families couldn t be accommodated in suku for high tea and cocktails as the restaurant was farther away from the renovation. given that families pay the same to stay in a suite, i m not sure why the hotel has such
