scratch.', 'i stayed at conrad hilton for the duration of 4 nights and have never received such poor service. we waited 1 2 hours for meals which was either incorrect or poor quality. the restaurants were severely understaffed. we requested twin share as i was staying with my father and we were put into a room with a king bed. they took some time to amend this. the bathroom drain had a very bad smell and there were loud renovations all over the resort. we would not recommend staying here.', 'not one for reviews but hoping this will make a difference to someone when choosing the conrad the first impression is almost like its the training ground for all new recruits, most are running around ragged, little interaction not the whole bali experience the hotel is also very under staffed, long waits on the phone, room service is very slow and service is hit and miss around the pool breakfast is like a wild west show, staff giving orders on microphones, nobody doing the work, cutlery, aprons, clean tables not evident wifi keeps dropping out, queried multiple times, just provided with a 24hour access code, poor poor poor no business facilities at the hotel, five star hotel? concierge wouldn t allow us to even use the pc, wasn t authorised, really! hawkers around the pool, smell of petrol from the jet skis in abundance resturants empty, too expensive and as a consequence, no atmosphere on the plus side, the rooms are nice but be very wary feels from a uk perspective, a bad thomsons holiday ps half of the hotel under construction', 'stayed for 5 nights at the end of july. everything was good until a note placed under the door on the night of the 31st. the hotel then turned into a construction site. the lobby and east lounge was all boarded up, construction workers everywhere, noise and associated mess appeared. our stay went from super relaxing to shocking. i wrote to the hotel to offer feedback but all i got was a reply from the rooms division manager outlining the extent of future works. i would have expected more as a diamond member especially when i mentioned i had a stay in november booked booked before i knew of these works . maybe a response from the guest relations hotel manager? there were also existing works in one of the suite blocks, with paint fumes prevalent, half the beach blocked off as a building site storage area and a lift out of order which meant carrying our daughter in her stroller in the dark down a steep flight of stairs. the hotel s housekeeping struggled to replenish the towels daily. on several occasions i had to call for towels despite the room having been serviced. i fear that our stay in november is going to be disastrous as this one. if at the booking stage we had been informed we would have booked elsewhere but we are stuck with a non refundable rate. huge room for improvement. a note under a door to inform guests of major disruption just doesn t cut it conrad bali. do yourselves a favour and book elsewhere.', 'not worth the money even with my diamond status upgrade. my room was so far from the lobby it required a treacherous 15 min walk to the lobby. room is nice and big but that s the only highlight. this hotel is second rate compared to others. believe me, pay a little more and stay at newer resorts like kempinksi or mulia nearby. this resorted is dated and does not feel like bali', 'some rooms are renovated some in process but not too noisy. however, an unrenovated room here is not a vacation but it feels more like a dark cold dungeon. they left the unrenovated rooms exactly the same as 2008. not one update in the room, not even a clock. for example, old coconut tissues boxes and bath room accessories boxes are over 10 years aged. light fixtures burning. they should have been replaced to bring up the darkness of the room. 10 years in an ocean environment, needs work. the unrenovated suites do not have music, not even a docking station clock for the iphone. how poor. on to the renovated rooms. yes they look nice, they are fresher and brighter, but do they work? they have a clock with music by bluetooth. our door didn t bolt lock. its a newly renovated door but poor quality workmanship it didn t work. the same for electric. two plugs pushed out our iphone chargers. we found another, but when we pulled our plug the entire socket came from the wall. one sink hot and cold faucet knobs both turn to the left, the other they turn opposite to out. get used to that. glass shower door bangs the cement. all very shoddy poor workmanship. in the executive lounge on the far side of the resort if you stay in a renovated room, the food is offered but you will be watched and counted on what you take. pretty uncomfortable. that is not the standard of hilton let alone a conrad, at least the many other conrad s we ve visited. generally they offer vegetarian snack for a couple of days mostly meatless everyday. the most surprising is they could not make a good tasting cocktail. they have several on offer. just flat and missing. coke cola in the bottle was the only drink that tasted like it should. the rin restaurant for breakfast which is a floor below the lounge was not nice. mostly due to the staff who were rude and sharp tongued. it was certainly not an exclusive offering, and many of us, including other guests being fed up headed for big restaurant for breakfast. their afternoon tea is a joke. it doesn t matter the flavor of the shake you order, they are all diluted with water. they bring a snack tray but overall the afternoon tea contents are not nice, certainly not of conrad standard. the property could look spectacular but everywhere you look while walking you will find alot of dying greenery and dying trees. there are staff working trimming and watering, but too much dead. there is a rustic look which goes along with the island. but they are a step behind on maintaining the walkways. on the whole, the service people are very good. with this large resort type property it requires management that knows this environment and how to make guests feel welcome and happy during their stay. it is certainly not a management place to count widgets. for example we never ever saw a manager around the property, for one week? if you are lounging in your room, you get the smell and fumes of gas from the boats which are very close to shore. it is sickening. that is certainly not the conrad we expected. we will not return and we would not recommend. hopefully hilton corp will take notice of our s and other s feedback and get to work here.']
combined_reviews: we have stayed at conrad bali twice, and unfortunately our most recent stay has made us change our rating from 4 stars to 1 star. this property has noticeably declined in service quality, operational standards, and attention to detail. first stay may 15 19 during our first stay, we booked the conrad king suite. the room itself was stunning and the suite lounge experience was excellent. however, two key issues kept it from being a 5 star experience 1. noise issues the soundproofing is extremely poor for a luxury property. we could hear guests in neighboring rooms, and every step from the room above. unfortunately, the guests above us had a toddler who consistently woke up around 4 5 am and ran around for hours. it significantly disrupted sleep and comfort. 2. concierge booking failure we booked two activities through the concierge. the snorkeling tour went well, but the full day tour with a conrad car and driver was mishandled. despite confirming twice the day prior, no driver was arranged when we arrived at 9 am. we were forced to wait an additional 30 minutes, which impacted our itinerary and caused us to miss activities because of traffic delays. the driver assigned later was excellent, but this level of miscommunication at a luxury hotel was unacceptable. 3. housekeeping oversights minor but telling on the last two nights, housekeeping repeatedly left only one towel even though two guests clearly occupied the room. we had to request an additional towel twice. small issues, but not aligned with 5 star expectations. at that time we rated the stay 4 stars, believing the issues were situational. second stay downgraded experience and early checkout we returned expecting improvement. instead, we shortened our 6 night stay to 2 nights and moved to the hilton bali resort, where the experience was significantly better. despite booking two standard king rooms, we both diamond members were upgraded to twin deluxe rooms. an upgrade that converts a king bed reservation into pushed together twin beds is not an upgrade. the hollywood king arrangement was uncomfortable, with a visible and noticeable crease between the beds. additionally, amenities that were previously standard now must be requested, such as the stuffed animal welcome gift, which is now just a small keychain. it may sound minor, but it reflects a clear shift away from luxury service and thoughtful guest experience. as part of our reservation we received a usd 100 resort credit. my mother purchased five of the conrad stuffed animals specifically to use that credit. at checkout she was informed that the credit did not apply and was told she needed to pay for them. i only discovered this because i overheard the conversation while checking out myself. when i noted that the same credit had applied to the stuffed animals on our prior stay, the agent initially insisted they believed bottle covers and other items were purchased, which was not true. after insisting, they eventually corrected the bill. although resolved, the experience contributed to a sense that the property is penny pinching and placing the burden on guests to challenge errors rather than delivering seamless service. taken collectively, these issues made the property feel neither consistent nor premium. the property feels like it is being downgraded while still advertising itself as a luxury resort. the hilton bali, where we moved afterward, provided a significantly better experience in every category. final verdict conrad bali no longer reflects the standard traditionally associated with the conrad brand. the service lapses, cost cutting measures, and poor quality control make it difficult to recommend. while our first stay still offered moments of exceptional quality, our second stay made it clear that this property is trending downward. for a truly elevated stay in bali, hilton bali resort currently offers a far superior experience. we strongly recommend booking there instead. we stayed at the conrad for 3 nights as a family me, my wife and two boys . we had booked two adjoining deluxe oceanfront rooms for our stay. what stood out for us during our stay was service! the size of the room was pretty decent as well, the bed was comfortable and the room well appointed overall. however, we felt that other aspects of the stay were just average we did a couple s massage which was good but nothing memorable especially at the price breakfast was basic and the menu did not change. overall, it was just average. we found the menus at the restaurants quite limited. gym was just adequate but nothing exceptional. look of the resort felt slightly dated overall, we did not get a 5 star feel from the resort and a few updates are required. disappointing understanding, but not this time. the first, on duty on the 14th around 2 3 pm was quite thin and elderly. when we arrived by car and asked where to drop it off for check in, he told us to take the equivalent of 1000 usd per night. we also were left a arrival gift of a bandaid on the side of the pool. all the pools are very cold temperature and do not have heaters on them. the ocean is pretty from a far but riddled with seaweed, trash and filth. the water sports that they allow on the water poisons the air with the smell of gas and exhaust, which means even if you pay for a cabana you will hear jet skis all day long and smell gas. i have stayed at many conrads and hiltons over the years diamond member for 10 years and this is seriously one of the most disappointing places. it has the potential to be a great destination but falls short on so many levels, summed up with mis management. firstly the plus, the property is beautiful. we were there for our anniversary and they made us felt well looked after. and the breakfast was great now the downside. i knock off one point for inconsistent service. the main lobby is renovated and check in requires a bit of walking. upon arrival at the check in area there was a long wait and no one to really look after you manage the queue. eg some guests were served welcome drink and given wet towel whilst they wait, but we didn t until we were halfway through check in and the guy asked if we had received those. the hilton honors express queue line wasn t enforced and i had to wait about 15 20 minutes for check in. as it was 2pm, the room wasn t ready so we left and came back to get our keys it again took another 5 10 mins wait to get served. there s a few stuff in the area but perhaps they re not trained to check someone in but it s not a great look when you have guests obviously waiting to be served and the staff just ignores you. upon checking into the room someone knocks at your door to check your mini bar, which is strange and should have been done before i check in. soon after that someone asked if we wanted the room clean which whilst nice is obviously not well timed or organised, i just got in the room. being a diamond member i was given late check out whilst i checked in and that was great. however before i checked out another staff member opened my room to show other guests into the room. he stopped as soon as he realised his mistake but that was not a good experience. all those happen over the spaces of one day, but i still had a great stay so i just knocked off one point there. the second 1 point came from the romantic private dinner at their restaurant which i would recommend you avoid. this meal experience is quite expensive costed more than my room for the two of us in fact. the ambience is good, the service is good. however the food was really bad. now i wasn t expecting fine dining even though the price is that high cause i know i m paying for ambience , but i certainly expected something decent. this food was very subpar we had 4 courses and they were all disappointing. some of the seafood didn t even feel that fresh, eg we had a barramundi which felt a bit off and almost tasted like a defrosted basa. many of the dishes weren t well thought through, e.g. a steak that was a bit too salty, paired with a random bunch of things like a mini lasagna and some mashed peas that looked and tasted like bad airplane food, carrot puree and some stir fry greens which was decent . this property is huge grounds are mostly beautiful but the service is extremely poor not of a 5 star or even 4 star standards. very shocking for a conrad a real shame. pros 1 big pool plenty of beach chairs 2 room is of good size, bathroom is beautiful biggest con poor service most of the staff seem to be trainees do not know what to do. incident 1 at check in i arrived just before 3pm was first told to wait as my room was currently being prepared. when asked how long i need to wait, i was told there was no room available they needed to check would not know how long i had to wait. i understand the check in time is 3pm but surely they can have a better idea if room would be ready at 3pm or later. incident 2 while waiting for my room to be ready, i headed to beach restaurant eight degrees south for lunch. i had to repeat explain the order on the menu to the waitress at least 5 8x as she did not understand. everything was clearly indicated on the menu that for capresse salad, can replace boccincini cheese to burrata. she did not understand what the menu said. i then ordered a beer asked for the wine list. she said she needed to check disappeared without coming back. i then had 3 other different servers, all equally clueless! surely the staff must be trained on what is available on the menu! incident 3 had to order my coffee twice at breakfast waited 30mins before they arrived. butter was not replenished as were many other items that had been consumed. it appeared like that the hotel was unable to cope with the volume of guests. incident 4 i called reception concierge for help from my room as i was checking out had 2 huge luggages to bring down. nobody picked up the call. after trying for 15mins, i brought my luggage down myself. didn t help that hotel was under renovation i wasn t informed when i booked it the temporary lobby was quite a distance away! conclusion will i stay here again? highly unlikely! i suggest you take your spend it elsewhere with better service. staff was very friendly and helpful, but the overall stay was unsatisfying. we shortened our stay and spent our last night at the mulia should have gone there the first day! . 3 things that really let us down. 1 we booked an ocean view room, the first room they gave us was only pool view with a glimpse of ocean if you caught the right angle, and the windows were extremely dirty. had to go back and ask them to double check and change rooms for us. 2 their spa is not at all what you pay for. at approx.100usd per person for 90 min, we expected to get pampered. however, they do not have an exclusive spa area! we were walked to the first floor of the suites, and had massages in suite rooms. 3 our second night was in their ocean view suite room, they told us no one booked the room above us, however, there was noise above the whole night and we saw lights and tv on. after 2 days of investigation, they told us a service team was their fixing some issues in that room, which sounds doubtful. it is more probable that staff stayed in the empty room, or the room was booked without showing up in their system. either way shows that there is something seriously wrong with the management. all in all, there are better managed resorts in the area with a wider beach front, in similar price range. really not what we would expect from a 5 star resort. rooms were nice but toilet smelt constantly and shower was hot and cold low to high pressure constantly. had 2 lunches and 3 dinners and all of them were poor in some fashion including but not limited to wrong food delivered, 2 hour wait to be served, entrees and mains served at the same time, steak served rare and well done on the same plate, excuses that they had a large table and they keep ordering so we had to wait. might have been excusable if the restaurants were busy but they were not. a number of guests left restaurants on different nights because of the slow service. we are a family of four inc 2 children aged 13 and 10 staying in a suite. we were told that we had to take afternoon tea and cocktails in the lobby lounge as under 12 are not allowed in the club lounge. we were happy to do that but later found out that the food offerings during cocktail hour was a poor, poor version of what was offered in the club lounge. there were only 4 types of canap each everyday, compared with a buffet spread of spring rolls, crab chuwammushi, chicken rolls, veloute, 4 types of crackers, 2 types of salads jellyfish and seaweed , fish croquette, potato croquettes, cookies, among others in the club lounge on the wednesday. i made a complaint to the duty manager on thursday to say that we were disappointed that families were discriminated against in such a blatant way. at check in, we were assured that we would receive all of the benefits offered in the club lounge but this was not the case. he promised that it would be different from thursday but it was in fact even worse on thursday, the cocktail offering was again just 4 canap including one spicy beef cubes, which is of course not suitable for many young children. i have also written to the gm but have received no reply. the duty manager has also not checked back with me to see if i m satisfied with how the complaint was handled. the lobby lounge is also next to the block that is being renovated and hence, families had to put up with hammering renovation noise at least the first three days that we were there. it was hard to understand why families couldn t be accommodated in suku for high tea and cocktails as the restaurant was farther away from the renovation. given that families pay the same to stay in a suite, i m not sure why the hotel has such casual disregard for us. if i were coming to bali again with family, i certainly wouldn t choose to spend my money at conrad. the treatment of families with young children is simply not up to scratch. i stayed at conrad hilton for the duration of 4 nights and have never received such poor service. we waited 1 2 hours for meals which was either incorrect or poor quality. the restaurants were severely understaffed. we requested twin share as i was staying with my father and we were put into a room with a king bed. they took some time to amend this. the bathroom drain had a very bad smell and there were loud renovations all over the resort. we would not recommend staying here. not one for reviews but hoping this will make a difference to someone when choosing the conrad the first impression is almost like its the training ground for all new recruits, most are running around ragged, little interaction not the whole bali experience the hotel is also very under staffed, long waits on the phone, room service is very slow and service is hit and miss around the pool breakfast is like a wild west show, staff giving orders on microphones, nobody doing the work, cutlery, aprons, clean tables not evident wifi keeps dropping out, queried multiple times, just provided with a 24hour access code, poor poor poor no business facilities at the hotel, five star hotel? concierge wouldn t allow us to even use the pc, wasn t authorised, really! hawkers around the pool, smell of petrol from the jet skis in abundance resturants empty, too expensive and as a consequence, no atmosphere on the plus side, the rooms are nice but be very wary feels from a uk perspective, a bad thomsons holiday ps half of the hotel under construction stayed for 5 nights at the end of july. everything was good until a note placed under the door on the night of the 31st. the hotel then turned into a construction site. the lobby and east lounge was all boarded up, construction workers everywhere, noise and associated mess appeared. our stay went from super relaxing to shocking. i wrote to the hotel to offer feedback but all i got was a reply from the rooms division manager outlining the extent of future works. i would have expected more as a diamond member especially when i mentioned i had a stay in november booked booked before i knew of these works . maybe a response from the guest relations hotel manager? there were also existing works in one of the suite blocks, with paint fumes prevalent, half the beach blocked off as a building site storage area and a lift out of order which meant carrying our daughter in her stroller in the dark down a steep flight of stairs. the hotel s housekeeping struggled to replenish the towels daily. on several occasions i had to call for towels despite the room having been serviced. i fear that our stay in november is going to be disastrous as this one. if at the booking stage we had been informed we would have booked elsewhere but we are stuck with a non refundable rate. huge room for improvement. a note under a door to inform guests of major disruption just doesn t cut it conrad bali. do yourselves a favour and book elsewhere. not worth the money even with my diamond status upgrade. my room was so far from the lobby it required a treacherous 15 min walk to the lobby. room is nice and big but that s the only highlight. this hotel is second rate compared to others. believe me, pay a little more and stay at newer resorts like kempinksi or mulia nearby. this resorted is dated and does not feel like bali some rooms are renovated some in process but not too noisy. however, an unrenovated room here is not a vacation but it feels more like a dark cold dungeon. they left the unrenovated rooms exactly the same as 2008. not one update in the room, not even a clock. for example, old coconut tissues boxes and bath room accessories boxes are over 10 years aged. light fixtures burning. they should have been replaced to bring up the darkness of the room. 10 years in an ocean environment, needs work. the unrenovated suites do not have music, not even a docking station clock for the iphone. how poor. on to the renovated rooms. yes they look nice, they are fresher and brighter, but do they work? they have a clock with music by bluetooth. our door didn t bolt lock. its a newly renovated door but poor quality workmanship it didn t work. the same for electric. two plugs pushed out our iphone chargers. we found another, but when we pulled our plug the entire socket came from the wall. one sink hot and cold faucet knobs both turn to the left, the other they turn opposite to out. get used to that. glass shower door bangs the cement. all very shoddy poor workmanship. in the executive lounge on the far side of the resort if you stay in a renovated room, the food is offered but you will be watched and counted on what you take. pretty uncomfortable. that is not the standard of hilton let alone a conrad, at least the many other conrad s we ve visited. generally they offer vegetarian snack for a couple of days mostly meatless everyday. the most surprising is they could not make a good tasting cocktail. they have several on offer. just flat and missing. coke cola in the bottle was the only drink that tasted like it should. the rin restaurant for breakfast which is a floor below the lounge was not nice. mostly due to the staff who were rude and sharp tongued. it was certainly not an exclusive offering, and many of us, including other guests being fed up headed for big restaurant for breakfast. their afternoon tea is a joke. it doesn t matter the flavor of the shake you order, they are all diluted with water. they bring a snack tray but overall the afternoon tea contents are not nice, certainly not of conrad standard. the property could look spectacular but everywhere you look while walking you will find alot of dying greenery and dying trees. there are staff working trimming and watering, but too much dead. there is a rustic look which goes along with the island. but they are a step behind on maintaining the walkways. on the whole, the service people are very good. with this large resort type property it requires management that knows this environment and how to make guests feel welcome and happy during their stay. it is certainly not a management place to count widgets. for example we never ever saw a manager around the property, for one week? if you are lounging in your room, you get the smell and fumes of gas from the boats which are very close to shore. it is sickening. that is certainly not the conrad we expected. we will not return and we would not recommend. hopefully hilton corp will take notice of our s and other s feedback and get to work here.
summary: we stayed at conrad for a total of 9 nights the hotel is old , first 3 nights we had a room in the right part of the hotel that smells like mold and its a very old fashion after we wanted to change and for an extra charge we got to the new left part of hotel were there is something else big rooms and clean clean and much better for the prices according not many things to say , food is not the best but staff is great, especially the staff at bar good cocktails, nice local coldbrew they definitely need a raise because they lift up the hotel vibes but not the hotel that is old and not renovated the japanese restaurant is a nice experience the hotel is huge, location is amazing, staff are lovely, friendly and polite. not one for reviews but hoping this will make a difference to someone when choosing the conrad the first impression is almost like its the training ground for all new recruits, most are running around ragged, little interaction not the whole bali experience the hotel is also very under staffed, long waits on the phone, room service is very slow and service is hit and miss around the pool breakfast is like a wild west show, staff giving orders on microphones, nobody doing the work, cutlery, aprons, clean tables not evident wifi keeps dropping out, queried multiple times, just provided with a 24hour access code, poor poor poor no business facilities at the hotel, five star hotel? the room and environment of the hotel is dirty included the swimming pool, the breakfast is very limited of choice and the taste of the food is very standard not line 5 star hotel more line 3 star hotel, the facility of the hotel is terrible we wereun this hotel for 4 night s. we booked 4 rooms.
bart_summary: Conrad bali has noticeably declined in service quality, operational standards, and attention to detail. soundproofing is extremely poor for a luxury property. concierge booking failure.

area, those are the primary point of this hotel. rooms are clean with good service, nice staff and good pool area. very good experience while staying here. adhi jaya sunset hotel is located on sunset road. convenient to the shops and easy to get taxi to the beaches. beautiful hotel with comfortable atmosphere. the staff are very nice and friendly. they are really willing to help. the ambassador restaurant is great with taste of bali!! the crispy duck is a must try!! it was yummmm.. i personally recommend you stay here when visiting bali. i stayed here over the span of 4 nights with a break in between going to flores island.. checking in was quick and simple, with the reception staff well educated in english and customer service..staff all over the hotel were courteous, offering good attention when needed and friendly offering advice if needed.. my first room on the 2nd floor higher up is better was nice and spacious, clean and comfortable..house keeping do a considerable job keeping it in shape..my second room ground level faced the pool and although was just as comfortable its too close to the pool and not sound proof so just about every sound from the room next door, to the guests at poolside carries through.. breakfast buffet style but did have a nice selection available of fruits, cereals and other things to choose from..the little restaurant facing the reception area has several offers of new dishes available and the food is quite good.. there is a spa available on site but its by request and you need to pre book so i didnt get a chance to try it since they seemed to be busy most of the time.. the surrounding area, is quite congested with traffic both ways, but its far enough from the beach area to not have clubs or bars around so its a nice place to chill and relax..walking up or down both ways have several restos around, a supermarket around the corner as well.. overall i really like this hotel and would consider staying here again for sure.. definitely family oriented.. we decided to stay at the adhi jaya sunset this trip my family and i checked in at this new hotel a nearly a month ago, we stayed there for few days. when we arrived, we were greeted by security which made me feel safe and big smile from the receptionistist. check in was quick and they spoke english well and the welcome drink was refreshing and surprising. we booked the deluxe pool room, the room was spotlessly clean and my kids were happy playing at the pool which is not really large but its ok. breakfast was great, lots of selection, but you need to order from the waiter for your fresh cooked eggs or pancake which is a normal thing to do at most places. also had their famous angry duck at the ambassador restaurant, it was amazing, the duck was delicious, and the chili was really hot which is what i like. the family loved it... definitely going back to this hotel again. this is a new place and all the facility s and rooms are great compared to some of the places we have stayed at. check it out love bali i ve gone several time to bali. for my own preference, i try to avoid some type of modern minimalist hotel since it is hard to feel the differences between the hotel, especially in bali which had a lo tof modern minimalist hotel. i stayed at hotel adhi jaya during my company outing. the hotel is situated at sunset road, so at least hopefully you won t meet traffic jam in front of your hotel. you could ask to be dropped to kuta or seminyak, just ask to the receptionist. the staffs are helpul. the hotel is clean. the room isn t too spacious, but it is enough if you only want to sleep there during the night. it has a little balcony area with the pool view. for breakfast, it used buffet system. i would recommend this hotel for traveller who only need place to sleep.but if you seek for a relaxation holiday maybe this hotel isn t really suitable for your need. what an awesome place to stay! you must consider staying here when visiting beautiful bali. the staff are amazing and so friendly, they cannot do enough for you. the hotel is beautiful, very clean, very comfortable and a wonderful atmosphere. the restaurant is awesome!! i would highly recommend you stay here. look no further this is the hotel of choice.............well done adhi jaya sunset you have everything to suit your customers and to keep them comfy during their stay! we stayed for 3 nights at adhi jaya sunset hotel. great room with balcony. close to kuta galeria shopping arcade, kuta beach, and legian beach. staff friendly and helpful. loved breakfast. we have two small children who loved the pool and spa. comfortable room, we went to celebrate a birthday, when we arrived in our room there was a card wishing us a happy birthday would highly recommend this hotel. thank you i stayed one night beginning of april 2013 in this hotel. i booked the room online and had to reschedule the reservation at the last moment. no problem at all. they confirmed it promptly. the room rate was very reasonable, included a very good buffet breakfast. i stayed in a room in the 4th floor. all rooms have pool view with a small balcony except the ground floor rooms . the room was modern, very comfortable, very clean and well equipped of everything you need including a lcd tv had all major international channels if you want to watch tv! and free wi fi. the ac unit was absolutely noiseless and temperature control responded accurately. the staff was very friendly and professional. i had to call their service to get the room safe unlocked twice, pressed wrong buttons!! . friendly service personal were quickly there both times. i had my breakfast buffet in the hotel restaurant 2nd floor . lots of indonesian food less western food and they were great. restaurant staff was also very friendly and efficient. the pool is a well maintained and good enough to have a good swim i didn t have time to use it . they also have underground secured parking area which i used to park my rented car. although hotel is situated on a main road, i didn t hear any notice as the rooms are not facing the road. i am not actually complaining the hotel because the staff is very friendly, the room is spotlessly clean although the ac does not seem to work well. my point is with the similar price, you can get room at more charming hotel at beach side in the tropical lush, or in touristic alley. for me, when i have holiday in bali, i am expecting to stay in a hotel with laid back, holiday ambiance and i don t find adhi jaya sunset hotel has that ambiance, it is more like a business hotel at the busy road in trading area. not my cup of tea but if you are looking for value for money, cleanliness and functionality and you don t mind the average ambiance then adhi jaya should be your choice i stayed here for 3d2n with my family, and it was a good experience. the hotel is located in sunset rd, at the heart of kuta. you can easily find carefour, a supermarket, at walking distance, and two souvenir shop just across the hotel. hotel staffs are so friendly and always ready to help with anything. fast and stable wifi available in every room. you ll find that this hotel is always clean and neat. they even have a pool and massage therapist, and even their own car rental. i would recommend this hotel if you stay with family or friends. i want to share my pleasant experience staying at this 3 star hotel but with 5 star service quality. the staff were really friendly and helpful. i was staying there to visit bali and to celebrate my birthday with my boyfriend. i got a shock when somebody knocked at our door on my birthday and 3 of the hotel staff were at the hotel room entrance holding a small and lovely cheesecake and a candle and they started to sing me birthday song. it was a pleasant surprise and i am very touched by their warmth and friendliness. in addition, the hotel restaurant food was one of the few best food we have had at bali. adhi jaya sunset hotel is definitely a fantastic hotel to stay in. we stayed for two nite at this hotel only for sixth month after opening, it s a brand new hotel at sunset road. there s so many hotel at this street, but if you want going to kuta beach approx by shuttle car or taxi is 70 100k idr. around the hotel you can found many restaurant and art shop, and this hotel have a restaurant with kopitiam style and the food was good. we stayed at ground floor room, poolfront. it s nice!! spend afternoon beside pool and the kids was so fun with splash water. just wanted to share my amazing experience from going to bali and staying at this hotel. the staff are amazingly friendly still speak to 3 of them via facebook , we ate in a few nights and the food was amazing as well. i got an animal made out of our towels every time we returned from being out from the cleaners and a little note wishing us a happy day and honestly it made my day ever day!! they were so nice it was nice and relaxing and not very busy which was great and we walked to kuta a few times and it wasn t too bad of a walk we would sit in traffic for much longer and it sometimes was a hassle trying to get back to the hotel but with everything being as great as it was the traffic being bad most nights didn t bother us! i d stay there again if location is not an issue then this is an ok hotel. like all of the newish 3 star hotels poping up around kuta,legian etc rooms are small. pool areas are basic. was on busy road not far from carefor but not many restaurants close by. i accidently booked this thinking it was the one in tuban. we upgraded to a lux suite which was nice but the hotel lacked atmosphere. if u pick it up for 40 a night for a stopover it would be ok we stay there for 4 days, the staffs are very helpful and friendly. the hotel is very quiet and peaceful, we are stay in front of pool but the pool was occupied. bali is a lovely place, will definitely go back again hotel is about 5 10 min drive from kuta town but taxis are easy to get, just inform front desk and they will help. breakfast was good. as room occupancy was low we were given ala carte. the room was simply awesome, we took the pool deluxe room. open the door and there is the pool. bed pillow was comfy. only complaint was there was no mini bar. one thing that i would like to say is .. a big thank you to the night staff on 29 8 2012. i had received a call from kl, my mother had passed away suddenly. i called reception about 1.30am, and immediately 3 of the staff came to our room to offer their condolences and help. the staff also helped us book our flight back, got us a cab at that hour, well .. they were truly amazing. we also left some money to give our driver as we had to leave in a rush and the next day they did. i wish we could have spent more time here, but what little time we had was just great. me and my family stayed at this hotel for 4 nights during our trip to bali. staffs are extremely friendly and helpful. the food they serve is a highlight. even though there are not much of food variety for breakfast, but what they serve is really good and taste great! they serve either buffet or ala carte. i think it depends on their room occupancy rate. the swimming pool is the best part of it. all the rooms are facing the pool. the pool is very clean. the only thing that i would like to complain about is their cleanliness of the linens. bath towels are dirty. there is stains on it. the bed sheets are also dirty, there are dust mites, me and my family s skin itchy after staying there and there is dust mites bites on our body. there s also hair on the bed sheet when i first get in to my room. other than that, everything was great! would recommend this hotel to my friends. just operated since dec 2011, it s a clean and grand hotel. stayed for 4 nights during my trip in bali with my cousin. staffs was awesome, friendly, helpful... hotel situated just opposite to agung bali sunset road and very near to the largest souvenir shop, krisna oleh oleh sunset road. 10 15 minutes to airport or kuta beach. will definitely stay in this hotel again for my trip to bali in future. my husband and i stayed at this new hotel, the staff was excellent and so friendly. rooms was huge and very clean, i love the king size bed. the hotel pool was excellent. food at th restaurant was excellent only bad thing was we had to wait for 1 hour for our food to be ready. but overall excellent place to stay. will b staying here again

bart_summary: Location is nice, the rooms and value also really nice, located next to supermarket which is really helpful and nice we stayed for four nights and arrived near midnight. reception taufiq was very kind helpful my husband s mobile phone was left behind at the hairdresser.

clean_text: ['i initially booked kampung sari gallery homestay via booking and was told that it was fully booked. they relocated me into in da lodge. the room was extremely noisy between the people in front in the pool and whatever is happening in the back office back kitchen despite all doors and windows closed. apart from that location is close to the center and wifi was working.', 'only stayed here for one night, but that was more then enough. the staff were all really rude, to the point where they woke up our entire room at 7 30am demanding money. the food... where do i start? the mie goreng was a packet of two minute noodles, the cheese on toast was bread with not enough cheese for a 3 year old. if they had a kitchen you could use even the poorest backpacker could whip up better food. your rooms don t have a lock on them and they don t have air con. honestly, don t do this to yourself.', 'the staff have stolen 600,000 idr from my wallet was proved after looking at the security cameras , got it back from the manager of the place after convincing him that it will worn the hostel reputation but still the worst experience that i had in 8 months of traveling. staff not friendly at all and the facilities in the hostel are the worst. location is great and very cheap but highly recommend to find a different place to stay in.', 'bed bugs!!! don t go! without any research we booked two nights. after swimming in the pool, our blonds hair became green. in the evening a lot of people figured out that they had bed bugs in the bed. we also had them. the staff was unfriendly so we decided to check out. when i started to read older reviews, i saw a lot of messages about bedbugs. so they dont solve the problem and the bedbugs are still there.', 'the hostel in itself is pleasant with a pool and lots of vegetation. it s really cheap and conveniently located between central ubud and the rice paddies. however, what you save by staying there may just end up being useful to clean and dry up all your clothes in order to get rid of bed bugs. my friend and i got bitten quite badly. it was our first time experiencing bed bugs and it added quite a lot of stress to our trip.', 'the hostel totally not friendly at all. i had my bag on the bed, lost 400 and about 20 of local money. so i asked the reception if there is cctv. she just said no, why you do not take money with you. no more words. so i said i am not blame you, just ask there is cctv would be helpful. and she just look at me and do not care. it s very unpleasant conversation, they donot care about guests here. and this place only the outside looks good. but the room and toilet very old, not clean. bed not cos at all. never stay again or introduce to others. 420', 'this hostel was great the pool was lovely and the rooms and showers were pretty basic which was okay for what i needed. i also made some great friends at this hostel, my only problem was the staff were so rude and unhelpful. i felt like anything i asked for was a problem and they also knew nothing about what to do around ubud and spent most of there time on there phones! food was awful there to, no one likes a dry stale burger and cold chips.', 'there were local men just waiting preying on young foreign girls all day at the bar and pool. each time we walked past them they would try talk to us. i did not expect this from a private hostel it felt more like a bar on the street that lets anyone come in if they want even if they are not staying there. not a safe environment.', 'pros nice view since the property overlooks green rice fields, comfy common rooms, and strategic location cons the bed creeks so much so when you move the entire bunk bed also moves, it s hard to get out of the bed because there s gap between the bed and the metal frame, no storage for toiletries in the shower so you gotta put everything on the floor, the wifi is unstable, and lack of switch plug means you gotta share the plugs with someone else.', 'booked a whole dorm out for 6 people even though there were only 5 of us as me and my friends wanted a private room together. we got a car from seminyak all the way to the hostel to be told sorry we ve put you in a 9 person dorm with 3 others. when we refused the room they said tough there s nothing we can do. they told us they had known the situation a couple of days before but hadn t bothered to let us know. we had to waste time looking around ubud for somewhere else that would fit 5 adults and also pay for a taxi to take us and our luggage when we had already paid to be dropped off at this place. the staff didn t care or apologize. and i was left to apologize to the rest of the group after being left without suitable accommodation. this could have been avoided if they would have just emailed the night before and we could have arranged our taxi to take us to somewhere that would accommodate us properly. we booked through booking.com and avoided using this app for the rest of the holiday as we weren t sure if our bookings would be a definite after this experience.', 'this place is very cheap, and the accommodation reflects that. however, despite the fact that it is very basic sleeping arrangements, i would probably have rated this place as very good if it weren t for the main staff. they seem only interested in being pleasant when they re trying to book you onto a trek, trip or other excursion, which they will multiple times a day. the rest of the time they come across as very rude. the pool area is very nice, with a view across a small paddy field and temple. the location of the hostel is also very good, walking distance from ubuds main attractions. the rooms have more than just human visitors, so if you re not used to wildlife or are squeamish then i would give this place a miss. there are a lot of geckos in the rooms, one even left some droppings on my sheets.', 'i love how green this hostel is... surrounded by lots of plants with nice common areas. food is decent enough although not the best for vegetarians, only a couple of options rooms noisy fans kinda damp humid mattress was really soft to the point it was dipping in the middle all night making it quite uncomfortable temperamental showers sometimes really hot and sometimes no pressure lockers not located near room my biggest pet hate is they have no facility to fill up a water bottle. this seems insane! you have to buy water each time. we have a global disposable plastic crisis and bali is an island with a trash problem! really sad to see. they also charge more than the local shop.', 'the room is basic with very soft mattres, the shower bathroom is not work properly, the room price is not include breakfast. they have a small swimming pool where you can swim or do sun bathing. it is located about 20 minutes walk from monkey forest.', 'our friend hyped about it so much that we decided to go and it was a terrible decision. the hostel itself is pretty and the communal areas are cool, with the pool, bar, sofas and stuff but the rooms, especially the 6bed dorm, are awful. the springs in the mattresses poke you in the back, fans make noise, the bathroom was disgusting and it s just generally not clean. we even saw a cockroach nest. the staff aren t very friendly or helpful either, except for the morning manager who s nice.', 'the location is very good and the lounge and pool area is nice. but when i saw my room, bed, bathroom etc i was happy i was only staying one night. im not that fussy and i have stayed in a lot of hostels but this place needs an update. the matress was non existant with springs poking me in the back, only a few sockets per 10bed dorm all away from the beds, sheets didnt fit my bed so i basicly slept on the disintegrated mattress, one toilet for the room with a broken shower in a dirty bath. it is a cheap hostel but id rather pay more and get a nights sleep and have a shower that works.', 'i ve stayed in a lot of hostels, all over the world and this has to be one of the worst. yes, it s a beautiful hostel but they don t even offer you water, the wifi does not reach the rooms,they have a rat problem,the top bunk beds have no railing so if you roll in your sleep you have quite a long way to fall and it seems to be that people who stay here get sick.... 4 people got incredibly sick whilst we were there and now both my partner and myself are horrifically ill! please rethink your stay, there is no point paying for wretched service, chunder and the runs!', 'guys please read the reviews on here, hostel world and booking.com. i didn t do so my fault until afterwards and found out that there had been a huge virus go round. people were sick in my dorm whilst i was there and now 1 day later creepy was enough for me to leave. the stuff was very helpful on my arrival, with any recommendations you d need they should be great help. overall, if you re young, or feeling young and do not mind 18 beds which attached another seven beds which made it and total a 32 bedroom dorm. i paid for five nights and i barely lasted one night they would not refund me my money so i had to lose the money and check out and check into something else. the pool looked disgusting couldn t even see through as it was cloudy. our toilet didn t work either', 'very nice place to chill at the pool and meet normal but had some other inconveniences. staff was very friendly and happy to help with day trips etc.', 'everything about in da lodge was just okay. the staff, the location, the rooms, cleanliness. we arranged a car to pick us up from the airport that never showed, they blasted music by the pool as we tried to sleep even though no one was even out there, staff had extremely loud conversations right outside our windows in the early morning and throughout the day. we did do a mt batur hike through them which went smoothly and was very nice. its right off the main road which is not where you want to be in ubud. i wouldn t stay here again. find a place a few more streets back off the main road for a more relaxing, better quality place to stay for the nearly the same price.', 'in da lodge was an okay place for a few nights. expect a lot of noise at night, the staff sat outside our room and talked for a long time. the walls are thin, so you ll be woken up. our room wasn t very clean rooms are big and al little bit messy and the bathrooms too, but i ve had no hot water for 2 days. the place is nice because of the architecture, garden, location, but nothing special. nothing was groups. so not easy to make friends when youre alone i think.', 'stayed here for 3 nights. booked his hostel about 2 months before and wanted the 24 bed room. got there and was told there was no location for this hostel too, close to lots of shops restaurants and not far from the market or monkey forest.', 'good place to stay for 3 or 4 nights..the location is great near to ubud place and ridge walking..i asked for a locker but they told me at it s not available !! so i had to leave my luggage without any security...te place and bathroom seem very old and beds aren t comfortable at all.. terrible wifi as it s not working at all.. anyway it s great value for money if you look for cheap accommodation at ubud', 'this was one of the worst hostels i stayed in so far. needed to wait 1 hour at the reception. after checking in the bed was ready after 3 hours asked several times i actually booked a different room 24 bed dorm where i knew my friends stayed in. but while i am checking in they told me that one is full? why would i book it for then.. in the 3 nights i havent slept much.. the noise was terrible. there was also a phone that kept ringing at the reception next to our holiday in the region.', 'stayed there for 6 days and it really made me feel as i m at home. the staffs there are very friendly and helpful.', 'thanks to jan, one of the owners of this jewel spot in though, strange and spooky for someone not used to living in the middle of village, finally my family enjoy the sounds of nature, specifically lizard locally known as tokek .', 'we live in indonesia dingy. despite the staff saying that the family rooms were the same price, the manager tried to give us a hefty changing room charge which we refused to pay. the manager pretends to be helpful and says he can get us local prices we live in indonesia so this is quite normal actually he charges ridiculous prices but refuses to tell you them until you leave! very rarely had any hot water. barking keep in mind when considering staying here pro quiet backroad and lovely entrance nice views from balcony superior room is really big con dirty feces on the bed, bedsheets had a moldy smell, no room from the roof. lighting in the room is terrible breakfast is extremely limited toast, eggs and awful jam and tea coffee are served in dirty cups. no juice in the bar. friendly management is hardly really interesting architectural caracteristics like the huge common balcony with a nice view of the rice field but everything looks like the owner just painted the walls and decide that it would be livable. the bathroom was old and very dirty so dirty that i ve really hesitated to use the toilets and to have a shower . everything is in need of real restauration and good cleaning. the garden is small but nice and well maintained. the staff was ok but i can t say the same about the ducth manager. at first he looks friendly, but as soon as he find he can t make more money of you, he become unfriendly he was not happy because i ve booked another taxi which was half price of his car . middle of june i was paying rs.300000 and after 30 minutes in ubud i found few other guesthouses with lovely clean room even for rs.150000. the breakfast included in the price at in da lodge is just a joke! in few words a dirty and old place where the staff looks ok but run by a greedy manager... i would not recommend this guesthouse to anyone, not even to backpackers who can find some other nice places to stay in ubud for half price.', 'we stayed three days as a family of 4 at in da lodge in ubud. we had been offered the royal suite or whatever it was named. we did not like it at all. although very large with two big rooms plus a separate kitchen and bathroom, we never had a feeling of confort in that room i do not know about the other rooms, ... . the cleaning was not satisfactory, we found every day little droppings from the roof, kitchen and bathrooms are from the middle ages, need serious refreshing...and cleaning. staff is friendly, but management was not present very often. breakfast is included but is very poor especially in quality. ready made noodles are served with a choice of eggs and toasts and local jam, all of which was not memorable... design of the breakfast place is all but trendy, and ants are running behind the seats... price of the room rp 500.000 was completely overrated... all in all, as a family we thought it might be a ok accomodation for bagpackers, but we would never go back there again as a family...']

summary: pros good location, 5 minutes away from ubud market quiet and peaceful surroundings gives you the balinese stay experience warm friendly staff serves breakfast to your room wonderful view of the padi fields if your room is on the 1st floor cons toilet not very clean, sink wasn t working, water may flow into the bedroom rooms not soundproof which would be bad if there are noisy people staying near your but the guest here seems more of the mature and considerate type, not the typical drunken youngsters you ll see in kuta, so i doubt there will be problems with this no reading lights doesn t have private parking areas, you ll have to park on the street outside i ve blogged more about this place here i ve booked 3 nights in in da lodge after reading the good reviews on tripadvisor. here are just a few points to keep in mind when considering staying here pro quiet backroad and lovely entrance nice views from balcony superior room is really big con dirty feces on the bed, bedsheets had a moldy smell, no room cleaning during our stay... the bathroom needs some serious renovation terrible smell coming out of the toilet, the shower floor is covered with small rocks which makes thorough cleaning impossible, shower floods into the bedroom, there s no wash bassin in the bathroom so you have to use the one in the kitchen . pros nice view since the property overlooks green rice fields, comfy common rooms, and strategic location cons the bed creeks so much so when you move the entire bunk bed also moves, it s hard to get out of the bed because there s gap between the bed and the metal frame, no storage for toiletries in the shower so you gotta put everything on the floor, the wifi is unstable, and lack of switch plug means you gotta share the plugs with someone else.
bart_summary: Kampung sari gallery homestay is located near the center of Jakarta. The food was terrible, the cheese on toast was bread with not enough cheese for a 3 year old. The rooms don't have a lock on them and they don t have air con.
