steep considering it just for 5 more hours, not really a whole day. we ended up using pool showers before flight home overall experience is ok, not a hotel i would recommend or stay in again. there are nicer hotels near by which i would try next time. massive down fall for us was the house keeping.', 'had booked for 6 nights accommodation but cut it short to 4 nights as they changed my booking from family room to deluxe room. was offered 2 separate deluxe as a compensation but that didnt actually serve my purpose as my kids are young and cant stay on their own in separate room. once i informed novotel hotel staff i was moving out they offered to upgrade me to tropical room with attached bedroom but it was too late by then. novotel did nothing more to compensate for my loss of booking, no refund was issued. overall the hotel has an ageing look, the rooms inside kind of give you a sick feeling as the interior looks quite outdated. air con is miserable with no adjustment of temperature available. the temperature set is quite high and it can be changed so it feels quite humid and stuffy inside the room.the pictures on website are far from what you experience in reality, was placed in a garden front room for name sake it hardly had any piece of grass or lawn. if you are looking for a 5 star resort style experience please stay away from this joint. novotel nusa dua is lot better then benoa facility wise.', 'this hotel has a lot of positive points. first of all the staff are extremely friendly as are 99.9 of the balinese people , which is a huge part of it all. we were concerned having read reviews about some of the rooms prior to arriving but we were happy with our room the only thing i would note is the water from the tap tastes exactly like sea water, don t use it to brush your teeth . we were travelling as a young couple and therefore the following comments probably won t apply to families the beach area is nice whenever it is quiet however it can become crowded and loud quickly we ended up moving from the beach area on our first day and never returned as we just wanted to enjoy the sun with a book. the hotel does have a quiet area with a pool, this was followed by most guests and when any guests made any noise we were happy to see it addressed by staff as its important to have an area where people can chill in peace and quiet. speaking of peace and quiet our room was situated over the smaller pool which was largely used for children playing again nothing can be done about this and if you re a family you will probably appreciate this, however for us a couple without kids we could hear children screaming right outside our window quite a bit not great when you re trying to relax in the early morning evenings. coco s beach restaurant very good service, very good food and we ate here 4 times, 2x dinner 2x lunch . our only minor critique was they ran out of coke on two occasions, once at 930pm which we kind of forgave and once at 7pm ?!! , perhaps having more stock of the worlds most popular soft drink would be a good idea. we had one more issue with a lizard in our room housekeeping arrived at our room to say he couldn t find it and that it had probably went up to the air conditioning and to call him if we seen it again. not exactly what we wanted to hear and we had a pretty sleepless night that night! although it is the tropics after all . overall it did the job we wanted it to it is a nice hotel however we would not stay again simply being a young couple there are too many kids around. not a fault with the hotel at all we probably just made the wrong choice. some days were really quiet, others were really loud. so we just wouldn t take the risk again. if you re a family definitely go for this hotel!', 'we stayed for 9 days in the beach cabana room number 828. we were assured the room had sea views, whilst if you look hard between the bar, restaurant , pool and trees you could see a small glimpse of the sea if you are standing up in other words we did not have sea views as described. i will say though other beach cabana rooms on the other side of the pool had perfect sea views. this really should be reflected when booking. our room was very clean and the daily cleaning was spot on . the rooms are old but full of balinese character which is what we were looking for. hot water was strong and plentiful. two small bottles of water in the room each day a little tight i thought and very good quality bathroom amenities, the body cream was lovely. wifi in our rooms is important to us when booking our travels. wifi in the room was shocking uploads and downloads were 2.4 this was extremely frustrating as my partner was on a working holiday and could not down load or send emails. in the end she made drafts in our room then had to take her laptop over to the business centre to send them. this was not good at all and for this reason we would not book again. we did notify reception! breakfast covered most tastes and faiths with hot dishes changing each day. a good selection of vegetarian was on offer each day. the pool located on the beach was clean and well maintained, whilst not overly large was fine. having a second pool across the road in the other section of the hotel helped i think. room service was very efficient and each and every time everything arrived hot. well done! we ate at cocos 3 times then just gave it a miss as the food was inconsistent, good one day not so good the next. there are plenty of places to eat outside the hotel so you won t go hungry and it s much cheaper for better quality. we had issues with our air conditioner , we were unable to change the temperature or fan speed on the wall panel so we called for maintenance. first chap turned up , didn t have a clue, then he called another colleague, same again no idea, then they call someone more senior so three workers playing with the wall panel got to give them credit for trying. then we get told the porkies of all porkies by the staff it s company policy to have it set on that setting . i said it s my room and i should be able to set it at what i want!!! they said sorry and walked away , the resort is located in a quiet area the the views with the boats and sunrise is stunning. it is not a swimming beach but a short walk will solve that. the majority of staff make this hotel, always polite and friendly. i would rank it a 3 star hotel.', 'i booked the hotel for a four night stay for myself and my daughter in the school holidays. we arrived at 2am after arranging an airport transfer which nearly didn t happen as i requested it but didn t confirm i thought the quote and follow up email would have been enough but apparently not! our book in was smooth and the staff were lovely. i was then a little surprised to be escorted across the road to the front of the complex there is always someone to help you cross but not to perturbed. punch drunk with exhaustion we fell into our beds for a long sleep! unfortunately we were awakened in the morning by very noisy neighbours who i can only assume were a large family with several rooms on our corridor. the doors were slamming to great effect and there were several very vocal conversations in the hall. all of which made for little to no lay in for us! the decor doesn t offer much sound absorbing and there are no slow quiet closing doors!! on our morning inspection of our accommodation i was a little disappointed. the room was adequate and clean which is great but the advertisements and price stars lead me to believe it would be a little more comfortable. the beds were comfortable and the linen clean but the aircon was next to useless and pretty noisy. we did have an over head fan which helped with cooling but the switch was temperamental and it took us a full day to get it to switch off again! we went for breakfast which is served until 10.30, great if you like a late start but at 190,000rph each it was fairly expensive. my daughter is vegan and we were surprised that there was a few options, toast, jam, fruit, cereal, soy milk, which was nice to see. although the fruit was very bland and tasteless unfortunately maybe we have been spoilt on previous trips the fruits is normally a highlight! the vista for breakfast over one of the three pools and on to the ocean was beautiful and a definite highlight. a low point for us, however was our terrace room which faced out of the complex now i know it is asia and they love their dogs but.... seriously, the never ending barking made our outdoor area unusable which was a shame as it was pretty and otherwise quiet. all in all, not a bad stay, would definitely not stay on the outside of the complex again. benoa is quiet and a bit dull on the evenings but if you are looking for somewhere less bustling it might fit the bill.', 'i arrived, a single lady,which i had paid quite a large single supplement. sadly to say i was placed in the garden area across the road, i did not want to walk home or across the road to eat on my own. requested i had a room nearer to the beach and restaurant. i had to pay a substantial amount as this was an upgrade. i found the staff very helpful and the room service really good, but the room i stayed in was quite tied and was in need of some maintenance. spa very good and price appropriate to its surroundings. i would not recommend this hotel for a quiet stay as i felt it was suitable for families.', 'novotel as a property is simply awesome. huge reception, good pool, great beach view, sumptuous breakfast, excellent service, aesthetic design are some of the points which makes this hotel a must visit.. but what let down was the room. we had chosen a garden view room. there was technically no garden, it was just a small portico with few plants. some of the wooden furniture are getting worn out. the bed was squeaky. the wash basin had a crack . so, they need to work on the interiors. the pricing is higher if you book through the hotel itself, but it comes with a bunch of options which also includes possible upgrades. but, i booked through a travel website which gave me a much lesser price. the location is pretty ok. it is on the beach line with some decent restaurants nearby. the best part about the hotel is their service. both the room service as well as the restaurant service was excellent. they have a very good team at the reception who take good care of all your concerns. overall, it is a good hotel only if you plan to stay in higher grade rooms.', 'my wife and i stayed 2 nights and 3 days at novotel benoa. from our entire bali tour, novotel benoa was the biggest disappointment, not because of the place but because of certain people. from the moment we came, the gentleman who was at the entrance did not help us unload our bags. instead he stood at his post talking to his colleagues. then i had to go ask him if he could help us take our bags up to the lobby. he acted as if i stopped him in the middle of a big task. then on our last day we went at 11am to the poolside restaurant. the lady there tried to get us to come later stating that they aren t ready yet, even though they were opening at 11am. my wife and i told her we don t mind if they take time so we decided to go in. the lady seemed utterly annoyed that we decided to come in for a meal. finally, when we were leaving the gentleman at the entrance once again did not help my wife and i load the vehicle with our luggage. instead he just stood away from us and looked away. bali is amazing because of it s people, but our experience with these specific people at novotel benoa was a massive disappointment. positives would be that the place itself is nice and we actually were given an upgrade by the lady at the reception, which we appreciate. it is unfortunate that the behaviour of certain staff members could spoil a stay.', 'the friendly and hospitable hotel staff did their utmost to ensure that our stay was comfortable and enjoyable, but unfortunately not all areas are within their control. hotel is old and in need of some major repair, in particular with their toilet and bathroom facilities. toilets are very difficult to flush and each day we found ourselves struggling with this several times a day. numerous calls to maintenance where made during our stay. we also ran out of hot water on a few occasions. food was very average, as was the selection of westernised foods for those not wanting to eat spicy foods. the handling of food was also an area that needed considerable review. we found that we ate out most of the time despite having an accor advantage plus card that would save us money. hotel is located on a beautiful clean beach and has a decent sized pool on the beach side, along with two other pools across the road that also belong to the garden wing rooms. hotel is on a main road with easy access to other restaurants, shops, massage and beauty parlors. sunur is located to the left of the resort with nusa dua on the right, both popular tourist destinations. hotel has an abundance of water sport activities both motorised and unmotorised that one can indulge in. all motorised water sports are at a cost.hotel had entertainment most evenings which was enjoyable to hear.overall we did enjoy our stay but it was definitely not of a 4 star rating.', 'my family have stayed at this property since 2006 and have always enjoyed coming back home to our home away from home they property is very tired and in need or a complete make over, we were quite shocked coming back after a year and a half.the staff are still absolutely the best feature of the hotel with many of them still serving faithful guests after 20 years and always remembering us when we return.we would like to mention a few special staff members who made our stay exceptional with their brilliant service, astawa sudiasih at the crocokiss bar and to wisnu and his fabulous staff who made us feel welcome and at home every night at dinner at coco s. the best omelettes in the world, as quoted by my 18 year old son from the lovely egg lady who has worked at the hotel since we can remember. there was a group of some very inconsiderate guests who were very noisy at 1.30am waking everyone on the garden wing and then again waking guest early morning before their next day tour and regularly shattering the peaceful ambience at the quiet swimming pool. the area around the hotel is a little remote these days, the pharmacy, alpha mart and a few restaurants have closed down, the bali collection bus service was not operating whilst we were there. there are quite a few chinese restaurants that have popped up around the hotel also.', 'i booked 3 rooms since 28th dec from 3 rooms 2 rooms have air conditioner problems even 1 room have to move twice and the other have to enjoy technicians service everyday even we can not get our 3 rooms side by side really the worse hotel service we ever had in bali ever', 'stay there for 1 night, check in was slow and very hot and humid at reception, fan was running at slowest speed. room was spacious but bid old. bathroom was also need major renovation. pool area and other setting did not appeal to us may be good for others. breakfast was the best in bal and still coming back in memory.location is very convenient to water sports.this hotels needs major uplift.', 'the only redeeming feature of this place is the staff who tries hard to address matters such as non working air conditioners or rooms located in front of a village with barking dogs. rooms are also very jaded, and in definite need of improvement. i wouldn t visit again.', 'the property is good a bit dated but has a small private beach section and multiple pools for you to relax and lounge in. the resort is divided into two half s with the lobby, villas and beach on side and across the road the other rooms and 2 pools and a restaurant. breakfast is served in the restaurant in the main lobby, with a good spread of food. we were disappointed that we had to be on the 1st floor on the other wing with an infant. with no lift or ramps we had to carry the stroller each time up and down which was a bit of a hassle. a decent property if you want to use he pool and the beach. lots of restaurants and warnungs nearby if you choose to dine elsewhere. good value for money property to stay at.', 'we stayed here for 8 days at the beggining of september as part of our honeymoon. due to this being part of a large hotel group and a well known brand, i was expecting good things after going on most of the positive reviews both on here and their website. on arrival you could not fault the service although it took half an hour to check us in. the lady checking us in asked me to fill out a preference sheet relating to what we d prefer re room, pillow etc.. before i d finished filling this out she had already handed me the room keys.. on arrival in the room we could see from the outset it hadn t been cleaned properly, the windows and mirrors were filthy, there was water stains on many of the walls, rubber peeling from the balcony door, bed sheets stained with what appeared to be old blood.. it goes on. we then complained and eventually after an hour was moved rooms into another deluxe room .. slightly better than the first but still very tired and in need of a tidy up. air con didn t work to start with so had to have a handy man fix it and cealing fan very noisy. at this point we were already angry and just wanted to enjoy our honeymoon so couldn t be bothered with moving or complaining further.. the staff and everything else is spot on. it s the hotel itself that needs updating and sprusing up. the hotel need to learn what deluxe means and maybe rebrand the rooms as standard rooms .', 'this hotel is in a great position. right on the beach, pool area is excellent and surrounding gardens are quite tropical. generally this hotel could do with a good spruce up. it s looking very tired and the rooms are in dire need of an overhaul. lights are broken ,drawers don t close and ornaments are broken in the courtyard area. the breakfast buffet is quite good but that is where it stops. food ordered in the restaurant was returned,stone cold and another meal was burnt. we ate outside the hotel on all other occasions. as is usual staff do their best but generally top management needs to be accountable for the overall running of the hotel.', 'this is definitely not a 4.5 star hotel. we were very disappointed to experience the dilapidated, old and in need of repair this hotel requires to bring up to a 4.5 star which they advertise as. the room we were given was a box made for a swine. no outlook, dirty balcony that dropped dust from the ceiling. covers, furniture and fixtures were old and stained. i feel sorry for the workers and staff here because anyone could see they were not happy with their surrounds and having to work in these dangerous conditions. yes dangerous. the location was nice on the beach, but again the atmosphere from the workers was not good. the setting was in need of revamping as most of it was falling apart. the decking around the pool needed ripping up and replacing as it was old and splitting. small children could have injured themselves easily. features were in the same condition. i will be looking at a complete refund. we left early by 5 days and they had the cheek to charge an early cancel fee as well. even though they knew we were quite right about the start rating of the hotel. sorry management but this is only a 1 star hotel. probably needs demolishing to bring it up to scratch of a 4.5 star as it was advertised. misleading advertising novotel.', 'together with my wife we visited the hotel as part of our honeymoon. i ll summarize in short the pros and cons of the hotel during our stay around 6 days pros very nice atmosphere, the hotel is just beautiful. we were in villa and it was very romantic and luxury excellent breakfast each evening there was thematic diner everyday activities,some of which were bali specific and interesting cooking for example nice hotel pool with lounge chairs cons the hotel doesn t have usable beach. keep it in mind. water sports behind the hotel are not recommended. this was the third hotel during our honeymoon trip and it was the only one that didn t make us feel special, regarding our marriage. all other hotels welcomed us with nice bed decoration, some sweets, bathtub decoration... if we can count it everyday we had fresh foods, which was nice, but i m not sure if this wasn t standard for all villas. front desk copied both sides on the credit card, which was used for the payment of the vacation. fortunately i saw it and insisted on giving me the copy. it was needed three times to ask for anti mosquito electrical machine. on our last night we had special bed decoration and a4 printed list with a reminder that tomorrow is our last day and it is good to clear out all financial related things with the hotel. very nice reminder. overall it is not a bad place, but if you plan to stay mainly in the hotel. hope they ll take a look at our review and try to make the experience better for their next guests.', 'we travelled all the way from england to stay in bali. when getting to mine and my sisters room, our pillows were stained and there was hair in the beds and floors. also noticed our room was unfinished as we had no bottled water or anything like our fathers room. for this to be the first thing we walk into in this hotel has really dissapointed us and even worse, the staff didn t take us seriously.', 'i stayed 2 nights here with my boyfriend. well, it was not that good experience especially check in time. we arrived around 1pm and we waited until check in time 2pm but they still haven t got a room for us. we got room around 2.30pm and the hassle start. the room is great actually, however, they have dog barking sound from the neighbour which was inconvenient for us. we asked to change the room. then they offered us a room with no view, blocking by the wall, of course we didn t want it. after like an hour or so, we still haven t got room until one of the staff came and take us to another room. but it was not a good one too, the garden access door was broken. my boyfriend was so upset and we insisted to see the manager. they said they were fully booked and offered us to upgrade the room with additional charge. and magically they open this one room for us. finally! it was okay, but we asked them to change the kettle coz it was old , fix the ac a bit leaking , and the garden access door. it was such an experience, the good thing is they are quite responsible. they gave us 30 mins massage and dinner buffet as complimentary to this hassle. however, we kinda waste our few hours time in bali. breakfast was great, but the dinner buffet was terrible sorry to say . the beach is not sandy beach. other than that, they do have outstanding service, staff was great. but then we felt we could get better room and beach with the same or a little bit more price.', 'i m a loyal customer of novotel and this hotel you don t get the service and extra s that you get in all other hotels. internet very poor quality stayed 9 nights complained several times about the internet and it s just not possible to relax and spent time on the internet. stay was during my birthday, no congratulations received like in other accor hotels. didn t get any upgrade of the room because loyal accor member. if you re across the street than you don t feel like you re in a hotel, you feel neglected and not part of it. rooms were not so clean, mould in the bathroom and the matress on sleeping bench and on terrace outside, the matresses looked terrible corridors had food trays that almost remained there for 1 day. breakfast tables took very long to be cleaned, so new people arriving to the breakfast couldn t find a close place to sit. staff is just friendly, not more not less an very bad hotel experience for a promising trip. ...', 'the hotel customer service did not live to the brand.below weee some of our experience. there was no welcome feel when we first arrived.we were told to take a seat and no futher explaination.the total experience was minimal and welcome drink was just guava juice. we requested for an upgrade with payment which led to change of room.we understood that there were no room at that moment,so ,we decided to have our lunch. after about 2 hours,we came back to obtain our new key ,no one seemed to know what was going on.we waited again for another 15 minutes.staffs were obviously not communicating and they were not apologetic for the delay. room was very nice but out of 4 electrical plugsc,two were unserviceble.again.we waited for almost an hour before they sent someone to repair.we were so eshausted waiting. tv channels were buzzing and nothing could be done, plus point toilet in this particular upgraded room is beautiful.', 'what can i say, the place is run down and does not look like the pictures in the adverts. the kids room stinks and the sheets smell. the electric fan in the bedroom is super noisy although does have air on with a nice bright blue light shinning in your eyes from above the bed. the main pool area by the beach was shabby and for my 3 year old was dangerous, we notified a staff member.....when we found one that by the kids pool one of the pillars was falling away and some of it was on the floor, he took a picture of it and left it without doing anything about the hazard. no life guards on duty. we were stupid enough to have dinner here and the food was some of the worst fish and the curry was well below average. at least they didn t mess up the ice cream i guess. i paid the same price to stay at the westin hotel....all go there so much better! trying to book a flight home and cancel the rest of my stay here and you have to send an email and still waiting on a reply...what a shambles this understaffed run down hotel is!', 'loved the location of the hotel. was very peaceful. the rooms were bright and clean. the buffet breakfast was very good. it was by far the best breakfasts we had during our stay in bali. the hotel had three pools. the ocean view was lovely and clean. not many restaurant options if you are looking for something different. they all seemed to serve the same type of menu. the hotel is also pretty close water sports. overall worth the money.', 'the room was very small. the bathrooms could have been more clean. there was water leakage in the toilet. the breakfast was pathetically awful. the variety could have been increased. i had mentioned in my request that i would like vegetarian items for breakfast. but there were hardly any items. the other places in the area provided way better breakfast. the property is nice. the pool overlooking the beach is also nice. but its not a very great hotel to be recommended. there are way better hotels in the same location at better rates with good food.', 'i was really dissapointed with novotel benoa. i had just come from another resort, and paid double the price of that to stay here. it was expensive, and i expected quality. the room, a beach villa, was old and run down. power points don t work, door locks are broken, and you get lots of little beads falling from the roof onto your sheets. the outdoor bath is very dirty and unkept. the wifi is terrible here. it never works properly, drops in and out, and is slow, making it hard to do business online. the staff are no where near as friendly as the other resorts i have stayed in, and the food wasn t anything spectacular. the grounds where lovely, and the pool was also great.', 'ho hum. we haven t stayed here for about ten years but have been associated with the hotel for 20 plus years . nothing much has changed and it still offers a more authentic bali feel with its decor. the facilities are good and our kids all those years ago had such a wonderful time here but we have matured some and maybe we were expecting too much from a mid range hotel. one of our previous complaints was lack of variety on the menu. this has been corrected but unfortunately everything has to have a twist of asian in it. international dishes just aren t international.....they are still asian. small things also irritate.....service, whilst always pleasant is abysmally poor. there are a lot of young servers and their english is very limited. room cleaning is variable sometimes you get a top up with coffee, water, toiletries, sometimes not. entertainment is pedestrian, breakfast is limited in choice and the themed buffets are very basic. we have taken to eating at the grand mirage next door as i just can t face another meal here. i admit we are picky but just disappointed in hotel. probably ok as mid range hotel but not somewhere i will come back to. bali has moved on with the times in regards service, food and entertainment but unfortunately this hotel has not.', 'this the longest check in we ever experiences. takes over 1 hour us to wait our room ready. with cranky exhausted toddler that feel even longer. the personel also gave us wrong detail about our room, first she said our room will be in main building but actually across the street. front desk lady wrongly over charges from my husband credit card, very un profesional from hotel personel. even through later on, they realize and ask to return it. breakfast was average, not so much warm food available. the property in this hotel are nice, pool and straight to private beach. but can they also cleaning the children pool, its quite dirty . we decide not coming back again in this hotel. bali still have better hotel to stay', 'i stayed two nights in this hotel just to be close to the airport. first of all, the area is not interesting at all. there is nothing around. if you would like to stay in the hotel 24 hrs, go for it. i arrived an hour earlier than the usual check in time and kept waited until the room was ready which is fair enough. however, the safety box was not working and i informed the front desk three times and i was happily ignored. rain shower was leaking water and it made noise the whole night and when i informed the front desk the other day, again all what they did was to note my room number. last day, i was asked by one of the managers how my stay was going, i informed about him about the issues and did not actually request them to fix them any longer as i was about to leave. but they fixed the problems only after i talked to the manager. i stayed in a building which was across the road and not in the main compound. room was facing to a greenery where some locals was living. a dog cried whole night. of course, hotel can t do anything about it, but it means some rooms are close to the local residences. i stayed 3 days in seminyak and 5 days in ubud. although i stayed in a guesthouse in ubud which was not 4,5 star hotel not sure how a hotel could be 4,5 stars . but i enjoyed it even more than this one. and ubud is full of nature and culture. so don t spend so much time in nusa dua...']
combined_reviews: the pool and beach facilities were quite underwhelming. the beach is man made, and the pool area is poorly managed. there were no sunbeds available by the pool for several days, as guests reserve them early with towels and then leave for hours. although pool staff are supposed to clear unattended beds after 30 minutes, this rule is not enforced. to make matters worse, the hotel sells day passes to non guests for 10 including breakfast, which only adds to the overcrowding. it s unacceptable that paying hotel guests, spending hundreds of pounds per night, can t get a sun lounger. the pool area was also full of children, as this is very much a family oriented hotel. even though there is a quieter pool across the road that s often used mostly by adults, the peace and relaxation were frequently disturbed by screaming and noisy kids. it would be far better if the hotel designated one pool as adults only to allow guests seeking tranquility to actually enjoy it. there are also not enough umbrellas, and while there are more loungers on the beach, many have no sun protection, even though numerous unused villas have umbrellas left unused. coffee and tea facilities was another disappointment, it was difficult to get coffee sachets replaced, and when they were provided, the quantities were minimal and some were even melted. i would have expected at least nespresso pods for a hotel of this price range. overall, this experience does not meet the standards expected from an accor premium property. very disappointing and not reflective of the brand s reputation. very disappointed, i waited 6 months and paid in advance for this. this is supposed to be a 4.5 to 5 star hotel and it was most certainly not. the good grounds, pools and public areas were lovely staff was amazing food was good the bad as an accor plus member i had hoped for the offer of an upgrade nothing yet a friend who was not a member received a free upgrade to a villa? i booked 4 rooms beds were hard and uncomfortable even with the topper pillows old and uncomfortable toilet paper and tissues were the cheapest available and hence you needed much more to accomplish their purpose cheap room accessories, the toilet roll holder was very cheap and difficult to use old bins suffering wear and tear chrome on shower fittings missing and old looking mold in the showers that was removed only when we mentioned it. nothing in the actual rooms suggested anything but 3 star. the beach is not able to be used due to garbage we were not offered late checkout so booked the ibis another disaster absolute waste of my money we are currently staying at novotel bali for 4 nights, and we had really been looking forward to this trip. it s not the cheapest option on the island, so our expectations were higher. the staff here are genuinely kind and welcoming, which we truly appreciate but unfortunately several issues have made the stay less enjoyable than we hoped. our first room was located right next to a rooster that wakes us up in the middle of the night, as well as a dog barking all day and night. one of the beds also had a broken spring, which made it very uncomfortable to sleep. we were then shown another room, but it had a very dirty wall and a strong musty smell, so we didn t feel comfortable taking it. we were eventually moved again to a different room, which is better, but here the safe doesn t work and the air conditioning remote is broken. on top of that, for the first three mornings we were given iced coffee with breakfast without any issues, but suddenly today we were told it wasn t included apparently just because a different staff member was on duty. it makes you wonder why such inconsistencies exist, especially for paying guests. and now we ve also been informed that the hot water will be shut off for three hours in the middle of the day, which makes it difficult to get ready. the hotel has a beautiful location and the staff are friendly, but overall the experience so far has not felt professional or consistent. we had hoped for much better. i am sorry to say but all the previous good reviews are completely off. the hotel was not up to standards under what should expected for a 5 star resort. at check in we were upgraded to a pool side view from garden view, so we were very excited to say the least. the fan was wobbly and made noise while on, so couldnt be used. there was no hot water in the rooms, once maintenance came by we were advised to let the water run for 10mins, by then there will be hot water. the noise level in the hallways was ridiculous and kept us up all night. smokers in neighbouring rooms would be smoking at odd hours which is their right however the smoke would seep into the rooms. out by the pool beds there was a horrid smell of sewage, so we couldn t enjoy the pool as much as we would have liked. after the second night, they were able to move us to another set of rooms. same disappointed to experience the dilapidated, old and in need of repair this hotel requires to bring up to a 4.5 star which they advertise as. the room we were given was a box made for a swine. no outlook, dirty balcony that dropped dust from the ceiling. covers, furniture and fixtures were old and stained. i feel sorry for the workers and staff here because anyone could see they were not happy with their surrounds and having to work in these dangerous conditions. yes dangerous. the location was nice on the beach, but again the atmosphere from the workers was not good. the setting was in need of revamping as most of it was falling apart. the decking around the pool needed ripping up and replacing as it was old and splitting. small children could have injured themselves easily. features were in the same condition. i will be looking at a complete refund. we left early by 5 days and they had the cheek to charge an early cancel fee as well. even though they knew we were quite right about the start rating of the hotel. sorry management but this is only a 1 star hotel. probably needs demolishing to bring it up to scratch of a 4.5 star as it was advertised. misleading the third hotel during our honeymoon trip and it was the only one that didn t make us feel special, regarding our marriage. all other hotels welcomed us with nice bed decoration, some sweets, bathtub decoration... if we can count it everyday we had fresh foods, which was nice, but i m not sure if this wasn t standard for all villas. front desk copied both sides on the credit card, which was used for the payment of the vacation. fortunately i saw it and insisted on giving me the copy. it was needed three times to ask for anti mosquito electrical machine. on our last night we had special bed decoration and a4 printed list with a reminder that tomorrow is our last day and it is good to clear out all financial related things with the hotel. very nice reminder. overall it is not a bad place, but if you plan to stay mainly in the hotel. hope they ll take a look at our review and try to make the experience better for their next guests. we travelled all the way from england to stay in bali. when getting to mine and my sisters room, our pillows were stained and there was hair in the beds and floors. also noticed our room was unfinished as we had no bottled water or anything like our fathers room. for this to be the first thing we walk into in this hotel has really dissapointed us and even worse, the staff didn t take us seriously. i stayed 2 nights here with my boyfriend. well, it was not that good experience especially check in time. we arrived around 1pm and we waited until check in time 2pm but they still haven t got a room for us. we got room around 2.30pm and the hassle start. the room is great actually, however, they have dog barking sound from the neighbour which was inconvenient for us. we asked to change the room. then they offered us a room with no view, blocking by the wall, of course we didn t want it. after like an hour or so, we still haven t got room until one of the staff came and take us to another room. but it was not a good one too, the garden access door was broken. my boyfriend was so upset and we insisted to see the manager. they said they were fully booked and offered us to upgrade the room with additional charge. and magically they open this one room for us. finally! it was okay, but we asked them to change the kettle coz it was old , fix the ac a bit leaking , and the garden access door. it was such an experience, the good thing is they are quite responsible. they gave us 30 mins massage and dinner buffet as complimentary to this hassle. however, we kinda waste our few hours time in bali. breakfast was great, but the dinner buffet was terrible sorry to say . the beach is not sandy beach. other than that, they do have outstanding service, staff was great. but then we felt we could get better room and beach with the complained several times about the internet and it s just not possible to relax and spent time on the internet. stay was during my birthday, no congratulations received like in other accor hotels. didn t get any upgrade of the room because loyal accor member. if you re across the street than you don t feel like you re in a hotel, you feel neglected and not part of it. rooms were not so clean, mould in the bathroom and the matress on sleeping bench and on terrace outside, the matresses looked terrible corridors had food trays that almost remained there for 1 day. breakfast tables took very long to be cleaned, so new people arriving to the breakfast couldn t find a close place to sit. staff is just friendly, not more not less an very bad hotel experience for a promising trip. ... the hotel customer service did not live to the brand.below weee some of our experience. there was no welcome feel when we first arrived.we were told to take a seat and no futher explaination.the total experience was minimal and welcome drink was just guava juice. we requested for an upgrade with payment which led to change of room.we understood that there were no room at that moment,so ,we decided to have our lunch. after about 2 hours,we came back to obtain our new key ,no one seemed to know what was going on.we waited again for another 15 minutes.staffs were obviously not communicating and they were not apologetic for the delay. room was very nice but out of 4 electrical plugsc,two were unserviceble.again.we waited for almost an hour before they sent someone to repair.we were so eshausted waiting. tv channels were buzzing and nothing could be done, plus point toilet in this particular upgraded room is beautiful. what can i say, the place is run down and does not look like the away and some of it was on the floor, he took a picture of it and left it without doing anything about the hazard. no life guards on duty. we were stupid enough to have dinner here and the food was some of the worst fish and the curry was well below average. at least they didn t mess up the ice cream i guess. i paid the same price to stay at the westin hotel....all go there so much better! trying to book a flight home and cancel the rest of my stay here and you have to send an email and still waiting on a reply...what a shambles this understaffed run down hotel is! loved the location of the hotel. was very peaceful. the rooms were bright and clean. the buffet breakfast was very good. it was by far the best breakfasts we had during our stay in bali. the hotel had three pools. the ocean view was lovely and clean. not many restaurant options if you are looking for something different. they all seemed to serve the same type of menu. the hotel is also pretty close water sports. overall worth the money. the room was very small. the bathrooms could have been more clean. there was water leakage in the toilet. the breakfast was pathetically awful. the variety could have been increased. i had mentioned in my request that i would like vegetarian items for breakfast. but there were hardly any items. the other places in the area provided way better breakfast. the property is nice. the pool overlooking the beach is also nice. but its not a very great hotel to be recommended. there are way better hotels in the same location at better rates with good food. i was really dissapointed with novotel benoa. i had just come from another resort, and paid double the price of that to stay here. it was expensive, and i expected quality. the room, a beach villa, was old and run down. power points don t work, door locks are broken, and you get lots of little beads falling from the roof onto your sheets. the outdoor bath is very dirty and unkept. the wifi is terrible here. it never works properly, drops in and out, and is slow, making it hard to do business online. the staff are no where near as friendly as the other resorts i have stayed in, and the food wasn t anything spectacular. the grounds where lovely, and the pool was also great. ho hum. we haven t stayed here for about ten years but have been associated with the hotel for 20 plus years . nothing much has changed and it still offers a more authentic bali feel with its decor. the facilities are good and our kids all those years ago had such a wonderful time here but we have matured some and maybe we were expecting too much from a mid range hotel. one of our previous complaints was lack of variety on the menu. this has been corrected but unfortunately everything has to have a twist of asian in it. international dishes just aren t international.....they are still asian. small things also irritate.....service, whilst always pleasant is abysmally poor. there are a lot of young servers and their english is very limited. room cleaning is variable sometimes you get a top up with coffee, water, toiletries, sometimes not. entertainment is pedestrian, breakfast is limited in choice and the themed buffets are very basic. we have taken to eating at the grand mirage next door as i just can t face another meal here. i admit we are picky but just disappointed in hotel. probably ok as mid range return it. breakfast was average, not so much warm food available. the property in this hotel are nice, pool and straight to private beach. but can they also cleaning the children pool, its quite dirty . we decide not coming back again in this hotel. bali still have better hotel to stay i stayed two nights in this hotel just to be close to the airport. first of all, the area is not interesting at all. there is nothing around. if you would like to stay in the hotel 24 hrs, go for it. i arrived an hour earlier than the usual check in time and kept waited until the room was ready which is fair enough. however, the safety box was not working and i informed the front desk three times and i was happily ignored. rain shower was leaking water and it made noise the whole night and when i informed the front desk the other day, again all what they did was to note my room number. last day, i was asked by one of the managers how my stay was going, i informed about him about the issues and did not actually request them to fix them any longer as i was about to leave. but they fixed the problems only after i talked to the manager. i stayed in a building which was across the road and not in the main compound. room was facing to a greenery where some locals was living. a dog cried whole night. of course, hotel can t do anything about it, but it means some rooms are close to the local residences. i stayed 3 days in seminyak and 5 days in ubud. although i stayed in a guesthouse in ubud which was not 4,5 star hotel not sure how a hotel could be 4,5 stars . but i enjoyed it even more than this one. and ubud is full of nature and culture. so don t spend so much time in nusa dua...

bart_summary: The hotel is a family oriented hotel. The pool and beach facilities were quite underwhelming. The beach is man made, and the pool area is poorly managed.

beach, don t pay the stupid high prices from the bar, walk 5 mins to warrung kak jenggot and buy it there. 1 large bottle of bingtang is cheaper than a small one in the hotel and you don t pay the extra 21 tax on top of it.', 'great location, the area is easily explorable by foot, both at the beach front and behind. the staff was great, the breakfast was amazing every day special shoutout to yoga for making the best soy latte! , and there are a ton of activities you can do. the gym is nicely equiped and the resort overall looks very nice. what i didn t love where a few things about our room. the bidet was unusable because when you turned it on, it smelled like rotten eggs. the shower head looked like it could fall down at any given moment. when we arrived, our sink was clogged fixed promptly and the glass of the peephole was broken, so we couldn t see who was outside when someone knocked on the door. the balcony also had a loose banister and some of the drawers desk, bedside smelled bad when opened. it gave me the impression that while the staff is doing their best to keep everything clean, management does not want to invest in needed upkeep and renovations. the food in the restaurants ranged from okay to bad. do not order local food there too expensive and zero taste. if you miss western food, then you ll be happy, they have great pasta, surprisingly. on the beach, the beds were comfy, but compared to the rest of the resorts seemed again, outdated and a little cheap. we got a 50 off deal, so it was fine for that price, but i would not go back.', 'the room was very clean,had plenty of hot water and the air conditioning worked very well. however, the room was outdated and we had to call housekeeping nearly daily to ask for towels and shower gel the used ones having been removed by housekeeping . i was also woken up at 08.30 by housekeeping asking to clean our room, although they did politely leave when i asked them to return later for which i was very grateful . housekeeping and the porters really are amazing though! there was also lots of improvement building works going on, which was noisy and woke us very early on our last day. the hotel did, after some persuasion by us, offer us complimentary late checkout. there s also no sun parasols on the beach, leaving sun bathers having to rely on shade being offered by the trees. the sun lounges around the pool and beach and the horrible white plastic ones. a real eye sore! very tenerife and not what you would expect from a luxury hotel. they especially stand out due to the neighbouring hotels having beautiful sun lounges and beach sofas etc. we opted to spend our designated relaxation days at neighbouring hotels at our additional expense . the hotel also charges for everything little thing possible. tennis rackets, tennis courts, renting a bike, using a bed style sun lounger the gym was free to use and was better than most hotels though. the food and drink was very average and expensive too. as a result, we opted to eat at the neighbouring hotels or at one of the restaurants in the bali collective centre a short walk away where, although the same price, was of much better quality and in nicer surroundings. also most luxury hotels offer a beautiful and tasty welcome drink, this hotel offers a tropical juice from a glass keg not very welcoming.', 'do not think you re luxury escape package is value for money! this is not a 5 star resort .old and dated, poor lighting in rooms, had to wrestle with the balcony door every day and bathroom showing signs of old with mould . 4 dining options and all terrible. did not have a hot meal the entire 10 days! le guests are also on a restricted all inclusive menu and free flow cocktails are a choice of 4 and watered down to lemonade status! gin and tonics are a thing of the past ..no tonic water to be seen! buffet and show dinner nights were mandatory for le guests 3 a week and the food was terrible. no heating under cloches so all hot foods were cold, overcooked and bordered on diarrhoea central . salads were dreadful and, outside of happy hours, alcohol prices were very inflated. if the hotel arranges transport for you expect to pay twice as much at least. airport transfer was 450,000rp as opposed to a metro taxi at 200,000rp! all i all a very disappointing 10 day experience and we would never do an all inclusive package again. luxury escapes need to decide who they align themselves with not doing their reputation any good at all!', 'stayed here june 9 to 14th on a luxury escapes package rooms ok package was not a good experience the told us we had to attend 3 themed dinners, first night friday 9 th we attended and were all sick that night and next morning, we did not go to one again although they tried to make us go, drinks included between 3pm to 6pm were limited no draught beer included cocktails were like kids cordials and everyone trying to drink as much as they could, they would be better including drinks with meals, staff were not helpful when we complained, sun lounges no cushions very low and uncomfortable, hard to get in and out of, pool access for older people very hard, big steps deep, and sandy areas on the other pool was unstable, also hard to get a sun lounge, meals packages were also limited. sorry i couldn t recommend this hotel it just misses all the big one percent s that make a good holiday, couldn t what to leave frank melbourne', 'this hotel is one of the worst we have ever stayed in. while on our honeymoon, my husband and i had done a lot of research on nusa dua and were excited to visit, but have been left extremely unsatisfied. we used a travel agency at home which partnered with a bali agency to help plan this important event. we have had an amazing trip until being at the nusa dua hotel and spa. the other hotels around are much nicer. the staff is untrustworthy and do not clean well. we had mold on our shower and asked to be moved to a cleaner room. it had started to make us sick after just a few hours of breathing it in. the sink was also not usable as the handles were coming off, the water spout had no pressure for even a toothbrush and they had wet rust all around the rim. after an extremely long battle they moved us to another room which had spider webs, gnats, and other bugs living in the tub and the counter tops had not been cleaned. we had to ask them to come back and clean. a few nights later they locked us out of the room after my husband left his wallet on the bed this was after they had cleaned the room and he left the wallet for 20 minutes to come help me off the beach. the staff had come into our room unattended for now reason at all. when he had gone back to the room for the bathroom, the room had been cleaned and it was assumed the staff would not be entering again. this had happened the night before as well when the staff came into our room unannounced as we were sitting on our balcony. it is extremely hard to come to a country and not feel safe in a 5 star hotel, but this unfortunately has been our experience. i hope this helps others as they come to bali and nusa dua.', 'this is the message i sent to the guest relations manager regarding our stay and we never even received a reply. hi arini, before i leave my reviews on social media i wanted to let you know how disappointed we were returning to your hotel to stay. so disappointed we checked put 2 days early losing a lot of money on our stay. the hotel room we received was in need of serious maintenance. the shower stunk of urine all of the time, the sink drain was blocked, the lights remained on all night in the cupboard. we did report all of these to the desk. the balcony doors jammed all of the time. there was way too much noise from events like dances, singers, wedding which made sleeping very hard until they finished. the one night we got to be early, we had housekeeping barge into our room at 9pm whilst we were asleep. the package we bought the food was far too restrictive especially if you didn t eat seafood. my husband got sick from the lamb curry he was served and spent the night on the toilet. the happy hour drinks were always warm and required so much ice to cool them down it ruined the flavour. breakfast was lovely. the prices the hotel charged were exorbitant compared to other 5 star hotels we have stayed at. in all we were very disappointed in our stay and would only consider the hotel a 3 star rating. hence, we will not be recommending your hotel or returning. i would like to point out that we had a luxury escapes all inclusive package, if you wanted to buy any food or drinks outside the package the price was ridiculous. a small bintang beer for example was 10 and a small sprite was 5.50.', 'as a resort hotel, it is reasonable to expect that you can get everything you want. but try getting a sun lounge by the pool, selfish people throw towels and hog them. a fair use policy is needed. couldn t get a seat at the bar for happy hour, and twice unable to get into a restaurant. food was ok but not genuine bali, no pork ribs or babi galong. rooms are fine but the resort is amongst a lot of other resorts and so no local business or competition and security seems to keep locals out. not really bali to me.', 'i recently booked an 8 night stay at this hotel through luxury escapes. unfortunately are flights were cancelled twice and the second time we had to change the dates from 1st may 9th to 3rd may 11th . the hotel wouldn t honour the originally booking and we had to pay for another two nights stay . originally we had an all inclusive meals package and drinks for 3 hrs daily. plus we had upgraded to palace club and had its benefits. . the extra money we paid only entitled us to palace club benefits only. plus they said we could stay in the same room , check out at 6pm and have afternoon noon tea on the last day. the palace club staff who were lovely and helpful told us if we had booked through them direct , they would have honoured are booking , at no extra costs. . they said it was an agreement they had with luxury escapes. anyway i don t know who s fault it is but i do know that my husband and i were caused much stress and had to pay a lot of extra money for the last two days of our trip . the least the hotel could have done was gave us the same benefits as they got paid twice for two days of our stay', 'we were a group of 8. 4 rooms booked for 4 nights. premium deluxe rooms. 1 couple were moved twice as the electricals in the rooms were faulty resulting in 1 x nights lost sleep. another couple lost one night s sleep because of noisy air con. we had all paid well in advance. when we were checking out to go to airport they would not let us take our luggage as they said we all had not paid. we didn t have the printed receipt. we did have a copy of the bank transfer eventually when we were going to miss our flight we put a credit card over pending more inspection. received an apology from them today transfer found. terrible end to a two week holiday thanks nusa dua beach', 'this was one of three hotels we stayed in during our trip and our least favourite. from the moment of check in, we were given a family room that essentially was a deluxe room with a bunk bed in it. the family rooms state there is a seperate alcove with a bunk bed and a kids desk. this wasn t what we were given! we went to the manager on duty and explained this, after some haggle and being told there were no other rooms she took us to see another family room. the room she took us too was what we expected, big and seperate area for bunk beds. i m shocked that someone will pay the same amount and get given the first room we were offered! be very careful if you book here that you get what you actually paid for. the staff weren t great at being attentive either. especially in the restaurant at lunch buffet was great . the only thing i liked about it was lily in kids club, the buffet breakfast was incredible and the common areas were beautiful a greats kids pool.', 'really dated rooms and the whole resort needs refreshing, definitely not 5 star. all in one package from luxury escapes was not worth it at all. limited food options with such long waits for dinner that guests often walked out of restaurants. we had a limited menu at every restaurant due to the package and couldn t order off the regular menus. alcohol drinks were only available from 3 6pm at one pool bar that had huge queues and seemed to have no ordering system at all. drinks outside the 3 6pm time slot were not included, except tap water. you couldn t even get an included drink with lunch or dinner. included massages we re almost impossible to book without 5 days notice and then we were offered 7pm during dinner time. staff were lovely and trying their best under incompetent management.', 'we came for 8 nights in a palace club room as part of a luxury escapes package. the whole thing was a misleading, falsely advertised mess from the arrival to the end. it is most definitely not 5 star 3 star at best . it is not an all inclusive dining package lunch and dinner are limited to 10 unchanging options of burgers, pizza and pasta from the equivalent of a kids menu . the free flow drinks package was actually 3 5pm happy hour only at the one pool bar which was obviously swamped everyday and only 3 half hearted bar servers allowing for an average of 2 drinks per person across the time window. despite the package no drinks are included with any meals you have to pay extra . atrociously dated room with appalling tv reception and broken showers we had to move rooms twice and were ultimately downgraded to the wing the furthest from the pool gardens and beach . even the buffet breakfast was the most basic offering. the 2 massages per person were basically fully booked the whole time, we were lucky to get ours in the only 2 available time slots left in the entire week. pathetically equipped gym that doesn t even have play music or have an available speaker. i ve been to bali many times and this by far rates as the worst. go stay at the sofitel or westin next door. avoid this dump, despite how attractive the very average escapes offer looks.', 'we were really looking forward to our stay and reading previous reviews it sounded like a amazing place but unfortunately it didn t live up to expectations. the room we were in and other areas of the hotel are getting very tired and shabby and it is ready for being refurbished which i suspect hasn t happened since the property was built. we stayed for 5 days and apart from breakfast we never had a good meal and service was extremely slow. i must say though the staff were lovely and very hard working which does point to a management and investment issue so please don t respond by saying you will speak to departments about our issues the staff could not work any harder. one reason we found for slow service was restaurants taking bookings for guests who aren t staying at the hotel but surely the hotel guest service should not be compromised by this practice which i assume leads to poor food as the kitchens are overwhelmed. we came to bali to finish off a trip we had just done in australia to relax and round off a excellent holiday but it wasn t to be.', 'very dated food average room cleaning items left on floor ie bedding towels had to pay for drinks with our meals even just water massages very rough and not worth the money good shuttle bus that was free to an outlet with shops restaurants needing to ask for water in room as they forgot to leave', 'would have loved to give this hotel a 5 5, but now ending our bali visit, and have stayed at several hotels i just cannot give them any more stars. positives amazing breakfast lots of choices for both me and my daughter . and super helpful staff great kids pool, with sand at the bottom which my toddler loved. small kids club, but a nice break from the sun. very friendly staff!!! especially the councierge who gave my daughter food for the fish every morning!! she loved it! quite spacious rooms! quiet rooms, and we weren t bothered by other guests or noise from the restaurants at night. negatives the most shocking and discriminating thing men are not allowed in at the kids club. because the rules in the hotel say so. none of the staff or the managers were able to explain why men couldn t accompany their child. so if you re a single dad or gay, you sadly won t be able to use the kids club with your child. which is crazy considering we re in 2023! we ordered food from the room service a few times. one time my daughters chicken was very undercooked. it was simply just raw. everyone knows how dangerous it can be to eat raw chicken, and for my 2 year old daughter this could have been very dangerous! i received an apology from the kitchen the following day, but did not receive any kind of voucher, and they only removed the dish from the bill. for something this serious, in such a hotel i would have expected a better treatment. when checking out they gave me a hard time with the bill, and after i while a manager explained that the dish was removed and that i wasn t charged for the raw chicken. they refused to remove the entire meal, and that was very upsetting. it was only a few more items, but as a principal they should have deleted the entire bill from that night and given some kind of voucher. all in all, a good stay! but wouldn t come back!', 'the hotel looks great from the outside but needs some work in the rooms. they are tired and the doors to our balcony were nearly off the hinges. the rooms have very bad lighting and no window in the bathroom. the staff are lovely and cant do enough for you. good size pool and the spa pool is a little quiet haven. lots of facilities available and its directly on the beach. stayed as part of le and the food was great apart from one restaurant which was awful went once and never again. have lots of themed buffets and the breakfast buffet has so much to offer for everyone. would i stay here again? no but i enjoyed my stay', 'what should have been a reasonable few nights was ruined at checkout. the reason during one of the inclusive entertainment events, we asked the waiting staff if drinks were also free, and the answer was yes. at the end of the entertainment we were asked to sign a chit our mistake for not checking it which was then produced as evidence that we had to pay for these overpriced drinks when we were checking out. having been delayed by 1 2 hour, and avoiding water coming through the reception ceiling, escalating our objection to 3 different staff ending with the f b manager we received an apology for the lack of trained wait staff, but the charge would remain! wow how to mess up what should have been a good holiday. i will also mention the patio door that was difficult to close was never repaired, the poor lighting, the master switch that failed but was quickly replaced , the unwatchable tv, the paths that developed deep puddles when it rained, odd tables for the outdoor events that didn t allow your legs to fit under, the never changing full board menus, the broken atm, the fresh coffee machines that are sooooo slow there was always a queue, the mosquito ridden spa reception, and the very diluted cocktails. under normal post covid circumstances much of this could be forgiven but not after the checkout fiasco. not all was bad. the breakfast has plenty of variety but don t expect the full english irish and concentrates on fruit pastry. the hotel photographer whilst expensive was very good and produced results despite the dull weather. the beds and sound proofing were to a high standard and the food was well presented. the grounds were well maintained with much effort going into repairs. if only they had done the decent thing over the drinks charge.........', 'this hotel was the go to place during the 90 s and it was almost 20 years ago since our last visit which at the time was exceptional fast forward to 2023 and the hotel is more nusa dire than nusa dua ! staying in a palace exec room was rather depressing , quite dark , worn out furniture , doors well worn out , sliding doors to bathroom and double sliding door to outside that really didn t want to open . outside doors are not sealed and we were somewhat eaten alive by mosquitoes staff offered to spray room the garden room patio was in need of a good power wash room has no curtains , rather than wooden style blinds which do not keep the light or insects out !! i realise that the hotel is marketed as being in a balinese style but it s now very very dated and in urgent need of a major facelift in every area on a positive note the people and service were exceptional. however, in our experience this hotel fell well short of a five star rating.']
combined_reviews: we stayed at the palace club room at this resort in september 2025 for 4 nights. our first bad experience of this hotel was when the driver assigned by the hotel to pick us up from the airport did not adhere to safety protocol. without informing us, the driver took my wife luggage and dashed across a busy road many lanes to the opposite car park. we so confused but have dash across the busy road anyhow. my wife was nearly knocked down by a fast moving car. this is totally unprofessional of the pick up service arranged by the hotel. why did the hotel assigned such reckless driver to pick us up? my second bad experience was when i was charged for a more expensive beer that i dd not ordered which was not in the menu. nevertheless, the hotel served good food and drinks at the club palace lounge. the hotel has beautiful and vast landscape. after this bad experience, we will not return to stay in the hotel again. we had the worst experience during our stay. check in was meant to be at 3pm, but we were made to wait over 2.5 hours for our room to be ready with two young kids in tow. completely unacceptable. when we finally got to our room, it was dirty and dusty. there were empty bottles under the bed from the previous guests, and the floors clearly hadn t been mopped just overall poor cleaning standards. the food was average at best, which was very disappointing considering this is marketed as a 5 star hotel. there was no mini bar in the room, our air conditioning didn t work, and the fridge was broken. we had to call maintenance to come and fix things in both of our rooms. the kids club was tiny. although the staff were friendly and welcoming, we were surprised to find that some of the kids activities came at an extra cost which felt unfair, especially given the already high price of staying here. the atmosphere of the whole resort was dull and lifeless. we genuinely wanted to leave and book somewhere else, but sadly we couldn t get a refund. while some staff members were kind, others came across as lazy and unhelpful definitely not what you expect from a high end resort. unfortunately, i would not recommend this place to anyone. it was a waste of money and a massive letdown. average. not 5 star level at all. great location. below average service and food. would pay double and get a great experience rather than this. most staff doesn t understand english well and had to be reminded for getting service. we stayed for four nights in a superior room. you cannot fault the overall charm of the hotel with its delightful pool, grounds and interesting gardens which are impeccably maintained, and of course it s wonderful beach front setting with the boardwalk and shaded sunbeds. the real pity here is that the hotel rooms are so dated and in desperate need of modernisation and a thorough clean at least. there is also a vast room for improvement in terms of the dining options but thankfully there are other establishments close at hand that offer far supreme dinner fayre than any of what is offered at the hotel s tamarind, cabana and beach pool dining choices that we tried. in terms of breakfasts, the range and high quality offering is superb and thoroughly enjoyable. the lunches and evening dining affairs at the aforementioned choices are of average quality in terms of choice taste freshness and is not good value for money. additionally, hospitality staff seem disinterested in the customer with poor and slow service, although in fairness the hotel was busy during our visit, and the initial interaction is always friendly. an added disappointment is that there is little ambiance to the evening dining affair with no background music playing around the dining drinking areas and thus a general discouragement for patrons to socialise or enjoy the occasion of sitting in the grounds under a large bright moon on a balmy evening. it was particularly evident that the staff at the cabana restaurant were more interested in wrapping things up as quickly as they could. if only they could wait until customers had left before starting the smoky smelly clean down process of the barbeque grill and avoid the stench and smoke blowing across patrons sitting outside. in fact, their cleaning efforts would be better served by cleaning down the greasy grimy dirty armrests of the chairs located in the cabana and other seating areas which are long overdue a clean down. in terms of the bedroom itself, ours was small, dirty and dated with old wooden cabinetry that is chipped, ill fitting and damaged with our balcony doors, wardrobe doors and fridge door all dysfunctional one way or another. the lighting levels are totally inadequate by modern day standards. the bar fridge is extremely small. the bed mattress is firm and uncomfortable. the bathroom is dated with numerous shoddy and unsightly repairs on view, the cleanliness is poor, and the sewer stench and fustiness smells are a constant. the hotel discretely tries to hide an electric air freshener under the cupboards but that does not particularly help. there are no bathrobes provided. the balcony contained rickety old furniture with stained cushions. we got the distinct impression that cost cutting measures are actively in place here with inadequate housekeeping standards evidenced by poor cleaning, the constant rationing of bottled water, tissues, toilet tissue, clean drinking glasses and even towels all of which resulted in the daily chore of having to telephone housekeeping to replenish supplies.... which they did. on the positive, the hotels key reception and housekeeping staff are friendly but that alone does not make for a 5 star hotel, and this it most certainly is not. oh, and a final point of note to those requiring an airport transfer do negotiate the price as for some bizarre reason the front lobby transportation desk provides for a cash rate that is considerably cheaper than that offered through the concierge, located a mere ten strides away across the lobby! having paid extra for the palace rooms was hoping for nothing but the best but sadly lacking. pros and cons pros comfortable large bed great shower. club breakfast room lovely good choice refined and staff lovely. cons rooms not 5 star quite dated pillows needs replacing asked for another and still no good room too close to restaurant therefore noisy as early start everyday. pool not enough loungers hotel could make much better use of area around large pool screens of greenery around restaurants and loungers and umbrellas all around the large main pool this would also enhance atmosphere and increase trade. which bring me to food and drinks restaurants all good selection but what i can t understand is the cost of drinks . at one dinner we ordered 2 drinks and it was just nearly the same price as the meal which is ridiculous. another time we ordered one lemon lime and bitters and the cost was 8.40. overall a costly stay that i believed would have been luxurious has been a let down would i return no i believe there is a lot better for the price. the hotel has beautifully maintained grounds and communal areas and the staff are lovely. the location is good and it was lovely to be able to access the beautiful beach. the breakfast was good and whilst it was busy at peak times, there was always plenty of choice. there is the usual sun lounger issue however there were always spare ones on the beautiful beach. people have commented on loose tiles in the pool. everyday the maintance man was trying to re cement these. overall we enjoyed our stay, however the rooms we were allocated were extremely poor. we had booked through an independent agent and had requested the premier rooms and paid the extra amount for these. however the information was not relayed to the hotel and we were booked into the deluxe rooms. on speaking to the manager, there was nothing that they could do as it was down to the third party booking the rooms incorrectly. we were told that the hotel is full and there were not any premier rooms available. an upgrade was offered for one of our 3 rooms to the palace rooms but that would have left the rest of our family in the other 2 substandard rooms. therefore we opted to stay put. there is no way that the rooms that we were allocated could be considered 5 star. the bathrooms were dark, dirty in the corners, appeared unfinished and the extractor fan was spewing old dust over the toilet and sink unit. there was still toothpaste spit marks on the mirror and the pots to put your toothbrush in were not clean. the bedrooms had furniture that was chipped and had seen better days. the bedside clock was faulty. the balcony furniture was also chipped and faded. after spending the past week in 2 other amazing 5 star hotels, it was a shock to see the contrast. after several discussions between our booking agent and the management of the hotel, you would think that one of the managers would come to see us and try to put things right. i understand that the booking was not their error but they should have spoken to us to see if anything else could be done. however, that was not the case. it is a shame because the staff working day to day are trying so hard to maintain the hotel but people need to be aware, that it really isn t a 5 star hotel. we had a lovely holiday in bali in spite of paying for 5 star hotel and this not really being one the hotel has a lovely entrance lobby and the grounds are well kept. however the pools have loose tiles and the bedrooms are in desperate need of renovation. the bathrooms in the standard rooms are inexcusable. they are dirty, moldy, cracked tiles, tiny, poor lighting. i complained and was promised a deep clean that did not happen and then we moved to a premium room. the bathroom is better bigger but still in need of renovation and with lots of mold in the shower. i did complain about this but i don t believe accor of which this hotel is part of seem to care as the response remains inadequate. as per previous reviews there are very few sun beds by the pools certainly not in keeping with the size of the hotel. we did therefore end up on the beach and that was quite nice with the breeze and the shade from the trees. we found loungers at 9 30 10am. we are too polite to reserve them although understanding. rooms were not even ready at the 3pm check in time and one we waited until after 6 30pm for. the tamarind restaurant is really good. one of our highlights was the ability to walk along the path in front of the beach and visit other hotel restaurants and walk to the bali collection where there are shops and restaurants. would i go back to bali would love to, but to this hotel? not same for breakfast even though the food is decent you have to wait to be seated or at the most busy hours you are not even seated yoiu have to fight your way to grab a table. however that is not the spears out of it, i had it and that was my entire dinner so goint to bed hungry. but that is not the worst, the worst is my kid at 11 god so badly injured from sharp edges at the pool so he has now of critique we got a room upgrade but the vacation is ruined more or less and it is their fault and they do nothing to real to compensate. we have traveled all over bali for some weeks and stayed at
