spots on tiles .yellwish type of thing where tiles and floor joins.patchy paintwork in the whole room.very very badly maintained,could have thought it s only with my room but whole group rooms have same condition.light in the rooms was very dim.we had to search where are the charging points.you have to pull a bedside table to plug in your charger. yes staff was courteous they listened to us but were helpless as maintenance of the rooms was worse .it seems no supervisor had ever gone to these rooms . rooms are spacious, staff are helpful. the hotel ground is beautiful and expansive. breakfast buffet is pricey but worth a try at least for a couple of days could use more variety in changing menus so guests don t get bored when they eat everyday. buildings are worn out with patchwork. housekeeping service was decent not five star level. there should be more resort activities too. the friday night dinner show with fire dancers was good. the cafe at mulia buffet has so much more varieties. the beachfront restaurant with mediterranean food was not bad kids enjoyed bread and pizza. where do i start.......firstly, they forgot it was out honeymoon on arrival. it was very well summary: lintang lintang was the best at greeting us and making us feel very welcome, will definitely come back next time, beds were very comfortable as well as good pool and air conditioner just back from 6 nights staying in this hotel, very enjoyable, from the security staff at the entrance right through reception, welcoming staff, restaurant staff and room service staff , one could only compliment there helpfulness and courtesy. private beach area fantastic the service was amazing, lintang deserves lots of money, the food was good along with the pool which i loved the menu for the alcohol a very good hotel, clean and spacious, with a super staff, caring and friendly, i can t forget lintang, was the funniest and solar guy i meet in the sanur watujimbar resort is the only hotel i ve visited in bali and it certainly is a great one. last year, to this year, a lot of improved staff was super friendly, especially lintang all ways keeping people happy food was excellent and tasty we loved hotel beach area bali icon mall waking distance and lots of restaurants around thank to all the hotel staff we will definitely come back next time this hotel is just amazing!
bart_summary: The rooms are a big letdown. The beds are good once you get into them but the room is jam packed with crazy furniture. The pool and the beach facilities were very enjoyable.

clean_text: ['we arrived after 4pm and our room was still not ready taking another hour to finally be given our room. the room we were provided was to be a junior suite but the room provided was not and was inferior. the room also was heavily perfumed to cover up the fact that it wasn t in use. the room was not very clean particularly the bathroom which was mouldy. there was also little furniture and storage space. we complained the following day explaining that the room provided was not the room pictured and described in the website, and was inferior. the management staff denied we had been provided an inferior room even after we repeatedly pointed them to their website. room servicing was also an issue with our room repeatedly being overlooked for cleaning. this went on for several days and we were finally moved to a room that was closer to what we had booked and paid for for our last two nights. on the positive side, the staff are generally vey friendly and the rear pool area is great.', 'disappointed with delux pool access room, overpriced, no drawers for clothes or spaces to put away luggage. were not informed of plumbing problems with our shower spitting out muddy water. were upgraded to one bedroom apartment which was comfortable. stove etc but no cooking utensils etc. staff lovely which made our stay more enjoyable. breakfast reasonable variety with sugi looking out for guests.', 'staff are nice and helpful. but the room is really basic, pillows are old, bedsheets and towels too. and the room was really noisy 24 7 with music from downstairs. i wouldn t book it again..', 'not the best stay with the hotel and i would not be coming back. the bathroom was not completely cleaned shower had hair and there were tiny bugs across the bathroom and on the floors next to the bed. the bed had hair inside and some stains on the sheets. the other reason which made it extremely difficult to stay was the air quality in the room. we stayed at the hotel 1 night and both woke up sick the next day. we moved hotels and requested a refund to which the hotel did not provide which was quite disappointing.', 'spacious room, great location!!!and breakfast had lots of variety. but on check out day, housekeeping entered my room without knocking, even though it wasn t 12 yet. one even opened the connecting door without warning, then told me i should ve locked it instead of just saying sorry. i really didn t like that part. i just wish there was better communication between the front office and housekeeping about whether guests are still inside or not. this could ve been a very embarrassing situation if i wasn t ready.', 'overall , the experience was positive. booking was done through trip.com and description of room options was not adequate most notably whether there was a walk in shower or shower over bath. a photo of the bathroom would have cleared this up.this resulted in booking the wrong room and subsequently needing to upgrade to a higher tariff room to get a walk in shower. during my stay the room was not cleaned until approximately 4.30pm and yet when i asked for late check out because of a late flight i was given a late check out of 1pm only. a little disappointing.', 'i don t normally review hotels but have to share my experience with the haven seminyak. be very wary of this hotel it is advertised as a 4 star property but it is far from that. the hotel is more like a motel style. travelling as a family of 2 adults and 2 kids. we booked 2 haven pool view rooms for 3 nights at the end of our bali holiday. the rooms are not only very basic but they are filthy dirty. floor was dirty, bath tub dirty, sink cracked, tiles off walls and toilet not flushing. after some discussion with management we were offered alternative rooms but these were very similar but alot further apart. we checked out. did not feel comfortable staying there with 2 children in a separate room and rooms were grotty.', 'negatives the photos of the room were deceptive as on arrival we were disappointed to realise that our room was dated and not as portrayed online. there was no hot water for a shower in the morning so had to shower at night. lock on sliding door was not the best. extremely noisy accommodation 24 hours a day there was music blasting hence the reason for supplying guests with ear plugs. positive lobby entrance, pool, great breakfast and friendly staff.', 'so it started when we checked in and paid but the staff member said the transaction didnt work even though the money had come out of my account, the wanted me to pay again with another card and was quite rude about the whole thing, obviously i did not just want to hand over another 2300k. then the next morning at 1 20am we get a call on the room phone whilst we were all fast asleep asking if we were missing a chair and requesting us to open our door, so weird and very scary and also upsetting as we all woke and couldn t sleep after that. when addressed with the staff member was only given an apology in writing which i felt was meaningless. the smell of sewerage in the room bathroom was absolutely disgusting and made me feel sick everyday, this was also addressed multiple times but never was fixed. the curtain in the room was broken and did not fully shut therefore woke me every morning at sunrise. the hotel was noisy with constant banging noises heard. our room card stopped working twice which caused a massive inconvenience both times especially when the 2nd time was when my son went to the room as he wasn t feeling well and needed the bathroom. there were flying bugs in our room and bathroom i had to buy fly spray daily to help remove them. we have visited bali 9 times and have never got sick however the second night there my family and i all got sick and needed a doctor. the flys all over the food at breakfast time was vile we either only ate scrambled eggs or we went out for breakfast. the wifi constantly cut out. the lamp in the family room was forever flickering and crackling like we were about to get electrocuted so had to be permanently turned off. the air conditioning units leaked water on the floors and the man hole in the ceiling had mould and water dropping all over my clothes i had stored there. this hotel gets a 1 star from me and i would not return ever or be recommending to anyone, our experience there was far from what i expected especially with the price tag it came with at just over 400 per night. very disappointing and extremely unhappy with our stay there.', 'the two stars are for the staff and the nice pool area. we stayed in the hotel section which was a mistake. the rooms are very small and not in great condition and they are just so noisy. every night is bedlam with music, presentations and we also had noise during the dayfrom people in the pool area below who had drunk too much and were on top note. to add to that we both got bali belly on our second day. we had only eaten at the hotel and were very careful with hand sanitising and taking travelan before eating. my husband was very sick for 2 days and then i became worse and we were both stuck in the awful room feeling like hell. the area around the hotel is awful. very dirty and noisy. we had never been to this part of bali before and ultimately we decided to cut our stay short by a week, forgo the money we had paid to stay and moved to a lovely resort in west bali where there is nature, space, fresh air and everything is very clean.', 'very underwhelming. facilities are basic compared to others in its price range. room not ready. given incorrect room type initially. run down. pool smaller than described. bad vibe. breakfast below par. bathroom rundown and tiny compared to pictures and description.', 'a little older hotel. staff in restaurant desi and at the pool angga and adi were marvellous. staff at reception could do with a little empathy. rooms have poor lighting and could do with refreshing. pool access rooms are not right next to the pool.', 'our first impression was that we had chosen well by booking the one bedroom villa. unfortunately, looks can be deceiving. the villa was very hot when we first arrived, despite all the air conditioners being set to 18 degrees, which was a bit concerning. however, we decided to sit around our swimming pool, have a drink, and take a swim. within minutes, both my wife and i were being attacked by mosquitoes and had to move inside. we asked if something could be done, and they brought us a can of mosquito spray to use around the pool ourselves. after going out for dinner and returning to our villa, we found the room was still very hot, and the kitchen area felt like an oven. as we don t like to complain, we decided to sleep on it and see how it was the next day. what a mistake that was! we had to sleep the other way around on the bed to try and get cool, but with no success. it was a very long night for both of us. in the morning, i sat around the pool, but before i did, i sprayed for mosquitoes with the can of spray they gave us. we went to reception at 9 30 am the next day to explain that we had not slept all night and that we were very exhausted due to the air conditioners not working properly in our villa. we asked if something could be done urgently for us. the reception said they would arrange for maintenance to look at our air conditioners straight away. it was then another two hours before someone came to look at the air conditioner. when the maintenance personnel finally arrived, they found that it was not working. we then went back to reception at 11 45 am to find out what was going to happen, as we were becoming very frustrated with the situation. reception informed us that they would move us to another villa and proceeded to phone housekeeping to clean the vacant villa straight away. we decided to go to lunch, hoping the villa would be cleaned and ready for us to move into. on our return from lunch at around 1 30 pm, we went to check if the villa had been cleaned. to our surprise, housekeeping was cleaning the villa we were staying in and not the one we were supposed to move into. we spoke to the staff who were cleaning the villa and informed them that reception was moving us into villa 607 due to the air conditioner not working in our current villa. we also mentioned that reception had informed housekeeping at 11 45 am to clean villa 607, which still had not been cleaned for us to move into. they said they would start cleaning it straight away and that it would take about 30 minutes. by the time we moved into villa 607, about 90 minutes later around 3 00 pm, we were totally exhausted and frustrated with the lack of urgency from the staff and the time it had taken. we had lost 24 hours of our 3 day holiday. when you are paying over 330 a day to stay in a villa, this is unacceptable and totally spoilt our stay. it has not left us with a very good impression of the haven.', 'the hotel fails to mention there are major alterations happening and constant banging and grinding of materials with work tools makes relaxation impossible. this noise jakarta to bali, very early morning around 09 00 hrs. however the standard check in time is 15 00 hrs, the receptionist was helpful, as they gave us an early check in. but, but there was a huge price give a twin bedroom for the first night. however they promised that next day early morning, we will be shifted to a double bedroom. this is absurd, as we booked via booking.com and the reception said the room. she said it will be ready in no time. we took the breakfast and went to buy some items and came back to hotel at around 12 00 hrs noon. because i wanted to rest and still my body was not well. when we came to the hotel, i went to the reception and i asked for the new room keys. the duty manager bluntly and in a very rude way said our room will only be ready by 15 00 hrs as it s the standard check in time. i was so mad and lost my temper! i told him what nonsense is this and what kind of hospitality is this? at first they put a couple in a twin bed room which ruined our first night and again they are forcing us to wait another 2 hours at the hot lobby to get our room. i shouted and yelled, from the top of my voice, because i am sorry, anyone would lose temper on this. but ruined our holiday, i will never recommend this hotel to anyone.', 'outstanding staff nice grounds unfortunately the accommodation is certainly not 4 star as they advertise on their site. we stayed in base of bed had water damage was peeling around the bath tub was moldy cracked with visible rust. buffet breakfast dinners were less than average.', 'awful buffet breakfast we looked and left caters more to asian culture did have some dried up danish and greasy chicken sausages very underwhelming no bacon no eggs no omelette no fresh juice definitely not like the website avoid avoid avoid', 'went there for the buffet breakfast, hardly any western food, unless you want noodles, i d give it a miss, lacks variety, no bacon, please come on lift your game. on a positive note the place looks nice, but we didn t end up staying and went elsewhere', 'pros location very walkable nothing really is too far close to double six beach friendly staff. reacts quickly to feedback and are accommodating grounds. well kept and very clean room size. the villa is massive such good space for you to relax and be at peace while on your vacation now for the not so good. there was a roach in our one bedroom villa .. granted once we told the team they apologized, got someone in right away to do a deep clean and went over the whole villa and we had no complaints after that we stayed 6 nights the villa outside of that is actually quite clean. we scheduled a couples spa treatment for our last day and they wrote down the wrong time. on top of that they didn t even call the room to check in once we landscaping is immaculate, and the rooms are clean and tidy. that being said, the hotel requires several improvements and renovations. the fixtures and furniture are worn and need replacing e.g., leaking faucets, broken shower holders, and bathroom cabinet doors held on with double sided mounting tape .', 'i ll start with good. staff brilliant. location perfect food in restaurant has heaps of variety. plenty of restaurants close by some of negatives rooms are large but as a previous comment mentioned so dark. few lights and some had failed globes that were never replaced. not a lamp in sight. after a few days foul smell from bathrooms. booked a shower room on last day before we left. booking made for 5 not available till 20 past. got there floor flooded, dirty used towels on floor but just as amazing staff uninterested.', 'positives the very friendly staff, many pools. negatives tooo many! firstly was shocked on entering the room soo old basic and nothing compared to the images from bed. shower broke 3 times in the 4 days we were there. hot water wouldn t switch off! then wouldn t come on, then again wouldn t switch off for the price you pay it definitely isn t worth it. looked like the room we got was in an extremely old part of the building.', 'great location the front of the hotel is the main seminyak legian street. everything with walking distance convenience housekeeping who did his best to calm a tired and upset late night check in. the restaurant waiter was really nice making sure my pizza crust was thin and crispy to my liking. room was fairly clean, sheets and towels were passably white for the price i paid for the room i guess. those were the positives. the disappointments 1. old and run down hotel. big property of two main buildings. the of...not enough lighting that part of the room was in shadow! 3. big bathroom but the drainage was clogged. first night, i stood in a few inches of dirty water as i showered because of it. next morning i called to complain and maintenance fixed the problem but it stilll flooded not as bed as the night before. also i cannot understand why the wall dividing the bathroom and the room is a glass wall. i dont particularly enjoy watching the flooded shower area or the commode from the bedroom. 4. bedroom door has no security chain. this was probably once a nice hotel but is now run down which is a shame as it has a good location for guests who just want easy access to things to do around the hotel.', 'breakfast was alright. staff were well mannered and kind. hotel was a bit run down, room to be cleaned so no one should have been in our room. it is quite a scary experience for 2 females travelling to come back not knowing who has been in your room and left the door open. for safety reasons i have to give a very low review. there was no explanation provided to us at all.', 'room was severely lacking. there was no safe in our room for the first half of our stay, the bathtub was before 2am. thankfully i was tired enough to sleep through but i imagine the average person wouldn t be able to. otherwise the pool was fantastic, security out the front to assist in crossing the busy road was incredibly useful. sabeen restaurant downstairs was handy for quick feed for lunch dinner but buffet brekky was nothing special. staff were very friendly.', 'the room was large enough for 4 chairs and table cloths in the dining room were not neatly finished, and there were sharp protruding parts, which made eating uncomfortable. but overall, i am satisfied with my hotel stay.', 'booked the room with pool view, they gave us the very end room 2015 we were only able to view pool partly as the half of it blocked with other view. we were not satisfied with room given but did not complain to receptionist . breaky was so so nothing was really special . the staff was basically friendly . however considering the price we paid we might not choose this hotel for our next bali holiday', 'we had a good stay at the haven. easy parking, affordable rooms, good balcony, great location, circle k right across the road for convenience. i would like to especially thank dharma, the security guard. he went out of his way to make us feel like valued customers. he went above and beyond for us. and a shout out to all security, who constantly stopped traffic to allow us to cross the road. they deserve more recognition from the hotel. the fridge was small and could only fit a few waters. aircon needs cleaning!!! works okay, but was very hot to wake up in the room. luckily bali wasn t overly hot when we went, otherwise we would not have coped in the heat.', 'stayed 1 night out of the 4 that had been booked. power switched on and off over 60 times, we took the card out to try get some combined_reviews: we arrived after 4pm and our room was still not ready taking another hour to finally be given our room. the room we were provided was to be a junior suite but the room provided was not and was inferior. the room also was heavily perfumed to cover up the fact that it wasn t in use. the room was not very clean particularly the bathroom which was mouldy. there was also little furniture and storage space. we complained the following day explaining that the room provided was not the room pictured and described in the website, and was inferior. the management staff denied we had been provided an inferior room even after we repeatedly pointed them to their website. room servicing was also an issue with our room repeatedly being overlooked for cleaning. this went on for several days and we were finally moved to a room that was closer to what we had booked and paid for for our last two nights. on the positive side, the staff are generally vey friendly and the rear pool area is great. disappointed with delux pool access room, overpriced, no drawers for clothes or spaces to put away luggage. were not informed of plumbing problems with our shower spitting out muddy water. were upgraded to one bedroom apartment which was comfortable. stove etc but no cooking utensils etc. staff lovely which made our stay more enjoyable. breakfast reasonable variety with sugi looking out for guests. staff are nice and helpful. but the room is really basic, pillows are old, bedsheets and towels too. and the room was really noisy 24 7 with music from downstairs. i wouldn t book it again.. not the best stay with the hotel and i would not be coming back. the bathroom was not completely cleaned shower had hair and there were tiny bugs across the bathroom and on the floors next to the bed. the bed had hair inside and some stains on the sheets. the other reason which made it extremely difficult to stay was the air quality in the room. we stayed at the hotel 1 night and both woke up sick the next day. we moved hotels and requested a refund to which the hotel did not provide which was quite disappointing. spacious room, great location!!!and breakfast had lots of variety. but on check out day, housekeeping entered my room without knocking, even though it wasn t 12 yet. one even opened the connecting door without warning, then told me i should ve locked it instead of just saying sorry. i really didn t like that part. i just wish there was better communication between the front office and housekeeping about whether guests are still inside or not. this could ve been a very embarrassing situation if i wasn t ready. overall , the experience was positive. booking was done through trip.com and description of room options was not adequate most notably whether there was a walk in shower or shower over bath. a photo of the bathroom would have cleared this up.this resulted in booking the wrong room and subsequently needing to upgrade to a higher tariff room to get a walk in shower. during my stay the room was not cleaned until approximately 4.30pm and yet when i asked for late check out because of a late flight i was given a late check out of 1pm only. a little disappointing. i don t normally review hotels but have to share my experience with the haven seminyak. be very wary of this hotel it is advertised as a 4 star property but it is far from that. the hotel is more like a motel style. travelling as a family of 2 adults and 2 kids. we booked 2 haven pool view rooms for 3 nights at the end of our bali holiday. the rooms are not only very basic but they are filthy dirty. floor was dirty, bath tub dirty, sink cracked, tiles off walls and toilet not flushing. after some discussion with management we were offered alternative rooms but these were very similar but alot further apart. we checked out. did not feel comfortable staying there with 2 children in a separate room and rooms were grotty. negatives the photos of the room were deceptive as on arrival we were disappointed to realise that our room was dated and not as portrayed online. there was no hot water for a shower in the morning so had to shower at night. lock on sliding door was not the best. extremely noisy accommodation 24 hours a day there was music blasting hence the reason for supplying guests with ear plugs. positive lobby entrance, pool, great breakfast and friendly staff. so it started when we checked in and paid but the staff member said the transaction didnt work even though the money had come out of my account, the wanted me to pay again with another card and was quite rude about the whole thing, obviously i did not just want to hand over another 2300k. then the next morning at 1 20am we get a call on the room phone whilst we were all fast asleep asking if we were missing a chair and requesting us to open our door, so weird and very scary and also upsetting as we all woke and couldn t sleep after that. when addressed with the staff member was only given an apology in writing which i felt was meaningless. the smell of sewerage in the room bathroom was absolutely disgusting and made me feel sick everyday, this was also addressed multiple times but never was fixed. the curtain in the room was broken and did not fully shut therefore woke me every morning at sunrise. the hotel was noisy with constant banging noises heard. our room card stopped working twice which caused a massive inconvenience both times especially when the 2nd time was when my son went to the room as he wasn t feeling well and needed the bathroom. there were flying bugs in our room and bathroom i had to buy fly spray daily to help remove them. we have visited bali 9 times and have never got sick however the second night there my family and i all got sick and needed a doctor. the flys all over the food at breakfast time was vile we either only ate scrambled eggs or we went out for breakfast. the wifi constantly cut out. the lamp in the family room was forever flickering and crackling like we were about to get electrocuted so had to be permanently turned off. the air conditioning units leaked water on the floors and the man hole in the ceiling had mould and water dropping all over my clothes i had stored there. this hotel gets a 1 star from me and i would not return ever or be recommending to anyone, our experience there was far from what i expected especially with the price tag it came with at just over 400 per night. very disappointing and extremely unhappy with our stay there. the two stars are for the staff and the nice pool area. we stayed in the hotel section which was a mistake. the rooms are very small and not in great condition and they are just so noisy. every night is bedlam with music, presentations and we also had noise during the dayfrom people in the pool area below who had drunk too much and were on top note. to add to that we both got bali belly on our second day. we had only eaten at the hotel and were very careful with hand sanitising and taking travelan before eating. my husband was very sick for 2 days and then i became worse and we were both stuck in the awful room feeling like hell. the area around the hotel is awful. very dirty and noisy. we had never been to this part of bali before and ultimately we decided to cut our stay short by a week, forgo the money we had paid to stay and moved to a lovely resort in west bali where there is nature, space, fresh air and everything is very clean. very underwhelming. facilities are basic compared to others in its price range. room not ready. given incorrect room type initially. run down. pool smaller than described. bad vibe. breakfast below par. bathroom rundown and tiny compared to pictures and description. a little older hotel. staff in restaurant desi and at the pool angga and adi were marvellous. staff at reception could do with a little empathy. rooms have poor lighting and could do with refreshing. pool access rooms are not right next to the pool. our first impression was that we had chosen well by booking the one bedroom villa. unfortunately, looks can be deceiving. the villa was very hot when we first arrived, despite all the air conditioners being set to 18 degrees, which was a bit concerning. however, we decided to sit around our swimming pool, have a drink, and take a swim. within minutes, both my wife and i were being attacked by mosquitoes and had to move inside. we asked if something could be done, and they brought us a can of mosquito spray to use around the pool ourselves. after going out for dinner and returning to our villa, we found the room was still very hot, and the kitchen area felt like an oven. as we don t like to complain, we decided to sleep on it and see how it was the next day. what a mistake that was! we had to sleep the other way around on the bed to try and get cool, but with no success. it was a very long night for both of us. in the morning, i sat summary: it s very clear that the management of the hotel has not been conducting basic room checks or maintenance on the rooms as they are currently well below what would be expected of a 4 star hotel throughout the room changes it became apparent that these issues were not limited to my stay, as there were a number of others experiencing similar issues and being shuffled around the hotel from one substandard room to another . the stay began poorly when i was allocated a twin single room on the roadside of the hotel even though i had paid extra for a pool view room in order to avoid the road noise , whilst i agreed to stay in that room for one night only, no compensation was offered at all to account for me having paid an additional fee for the pool view. i went here it took us 5 hours to get a room terrible staff and a was a 3 year old my grandma told me that it was horrific do not come here waste of money waste of time 1 star bad hotel bad i needed milk room had bug hive and one guy said his daughter almost died we spent 10 days at the haven and were throughly disappointed.
bart_summary: The room we were provided was to be a junior suite but the room provided was not and was inferior. The room also was heavily perfumed to cover up the fact that it wasn't in use. The staff are generally vey friendly and the rear pool area is great.

'this was my 2nd stay but first in a plunge pool room and 1st with a child. in 2022 the breakfast was amazing, this time it was ok, if you like pastries, doughnuts and muffins you ll be happy. hot savoury items were lacking unless you like the asian breakfast options and on one day half the breakfast area was closed off. we paid extra for the plunge pool room but only used it once. no pool towels provided in the room, so had to go looking for them. it was very cold as it doesn t get sun, we were ok with this, it was the swarm of mosquitoes that stopped us using the pool!! the room and hotel were clean and the staff as before were lovely and will go out of their way to help. the kids club was a bit disappointing, we used it twice and other than the person behind the desk there was everywhere. i met two girls staying in a bigger room with ensuite bathroom right next to mine and they paid the same price then i did! when i asked for the prices again at the reception i was told to just move further whether i m not fine with them... in comparison to other places i stood in indonesia the room prices are quite expensive for what you get and breakfast is not included. to make a long story short i think there are definitely better places to stay in padangbai!!', 'i am not usually one to write reviews but felt compelled to after our recent stay, to warn off any other potential guests! we had stayed in padangbai the year before and we ate at topi inn a few times and the food seemed ok and we liked the quirkiness of the place, however the service was not great and it seemed a little overpriced. being creatures of habit we decided to book a room and stay this year. we don t usually book ahead but we took the risk as we thought the place had seemed fine the year before! when we arrived and were shown to our room we wanted to leave straight away. there was only one shared smelly unclean bathroom upstairs and there were wild cats roaming in and out of it. the room was so thick with dust it was difficult to breathe, we turned on the fan which was so old and covered in dust it just blew it around the tiny room and made it worse. the mosquito net was grey and filthy, filled with bugs and literally had some kind of cocoon growing on it, we asked for it to be taken down. there was no lock on the door, only one towel between 2 guests and no blinds net curtains of any kind. there were inch wide gaps around all of the windows and our room at one point had 12 lizards in as well as mosquitos flies and bugs. i don t know what we were thinking but we decided to stick it out as we were travelling on a budget. when we climbed into bed we found that there was sand and hair in it as well as flies and other dead bugs. the rooms were separated by thin bamboo fences, you could see and hear everything going on in the next room and were woken up every time anybody moved as the floor was so creaky. we couldn t get to sleep as the music went on til around 1am and then after that we were kept awake by the sound of the ferry horns in the harbour, barking dogs and local workers. by 4am we had had enough and went to sleep on the beach. we hoped that the breakfast would be better than our nights sleep, but it wasn t. the pancakes were undercooked and soggy and our meals were bought out ten minutes apart which was an advantage at the time because it meant that one of us could eat while the other swatted flies and wild dogs away! we paid 24 dollars for b b which is hugely overpriced seeing as we had paid the same to stay in a bungalow the night before with air con our own bathroom and a pool. the waitresses were lazy and rude and always trying to flog you something or overcharge you. please please take my advice and do not stay there! if you read most of the positive reviews they are only from people who have eaten there. i wouldn t wish this place on my worst enemy!', 'the food is extremely expensive and the tiniest portions i have ever seen. the staff didn t care there was anyone there. we had to wait a half an hour for food and then twenty minutes more for the bill. we also got margaritas which were vile. all this happened while at least 15 flies where buzzing around us. never again.', 'worst staff..worst experienced in bali ..very expensive prices for the food received if you can see it on your plate not just for bali and flies everywhere. cocktails weren t what stated to be on menu..the pizza toast was a piece of toast with some cheese. fruit salad was bad. avoid', 'we stayed at room six which is a spacious loft. the place is rather exposed so don t expect your average hotel room. to explain, the room does not have a door but you enter the room by climbing a stairs and get through a hatch. we asked for a lock. we had an extra guest, it was a cat. there was no hot water. we paid 37 euro excluding breakfast. rather expensive but to be fair the room van sleep up to 4 people. it is a short walk from the port. very friendly staff. they were very helpfull in arranging boat tickets.', 'not clean, not a private bathroom, brocken windows, very loud during the night, bed was shaking whenever somebody walked outside the room, unfriendly and very nosy greedy staff always trying to sell something, even tried to make us pay for breakfast although it was included. would not come again. padangbai also not worth to stay more than more than 1 night just good for going to gili islands.', 'at the end of the beach road in padangbai is topi inn, with a delightful view over the harbour. it seems to be a place in transition. it doesn t quite know what it is any more. historically it was the only place in town, but now it fights for recognition with the many other restaurants that have sprung up in this town. we enjoyed our beers and watched the sun set over the harbour, but the menu was enormous really huge with everything under the sun and one wonders how it could be possibly maintained. go for the drinks, not for the meals.', 'this hotel if you can call it that is disgusting! to even call it a hostel would be giving it too much praise! a friend and i were booked in for two nights but we didn t even stay one! we had a room with a private bathroom. the room was filthy, the lights didnt work and the plug socket for the door stretched across thr sliding door for the bathroom, which the shower was behind! the bathroom smelt horrendous, the toilet didn t flush and the shower was no more than a cold trickle. you felt cleaner before you went in. the security was pretty much non existant...we had to ask for a padlock for our room. the final straw came when there were mich running about our room which makes you wonder what theur kitchen for the restaurant is like . we left before staying one night. ..and they didn t even question why. spend a bit more and go elsewhere!', 'this place is a mixed bag. first of all, it s cheap. super cheap! for serious backpackers they offer a row of mattresses on the floor with mosquito nets suspended above them. you will probably want to use the net because the area is open air, and the place is constucted of bamboo. i believe the cost was 75k per night which is just unbeatable. and quite honestly, i liked it! i thought it was a cool experience, and found it surprisingly comfortable in august when i visited. you can hear the waves crashing against the sand at night. with that said, it can be very noisy! some nights they feature live music at the restaurant downstairs, and you will hear it loud and clear. the staff is nice, but not necessarily attentive. they seem to always be busy with the restaurant operation. the food in my opinion is mediocre and overpriced. there are numerous options within walking distance for delicious and cheap food. overall, it was ok for me. the location just across from the beach at the end of the main strip was ideal. i would recommend going there in person to inquire about availability. i called and someone said they were sold out, but when i walked in 15 minutes later they had a space available. most likely just a miscommunication, which you will surely experience in indonesia as an english speaker. bottom line, i d stay here again. if you re a backpacker looking for something cheap, try it for a night. i ended up staying for 5! they also have private rooms available very limited . i saw numerous families in those rooms that seemed to enjoy it.', 'we arrived late with the farry from lombok and lonely planet was suggesting this place as top one! and we were supposed to spend two nights in this place, so the only room availble was the suite for 250.000irp without breakfast it has been the worst night in indonesia ever room is very nice all in bamboo but everything it open you can listen to everything, a cat was hanging around, in the bathroom there was one of those huge geko not normal one, enormous which i had to use the dorm bathroom, bed was so humid that i could feel it almost wet, full of bugs in the room..in the bed as well! in the morning for breakfast i had another nice sourprise we ordered food and my toast after 45 min did not arrived even though i ve asked twice how long should i wait for that...i finally went to another place to get my breakfast staff is smiley but extremely lazy and they try all the time to sell you tours or arrange you transfer...we had dinner there, big selection from the men , good enough but still very slow service. in the end they also try to add me a big beer which we didn t drink. this bad service it s a pity because the place itself it s really characteristic. they cherge 3 if you pay by credit card.', 'we stayed in topiinn for 2 nights and right after the first night we regretted our choice. we had a double room with an enclosed bathroom. the rooms are very tiny and the matress is aweful. the mosquito nets are full of holes and don t fit the bed. the walls are only made of bamboo so that you can hear and feel your neighbour move in the bed in the next room. there is only a small fan in the room which makes the whole room very warm and uncomfortable. the enclosed toilet is only seperated by a small wall from the public toilet so that you hear everything from that bathroom. we hardly slep, we were exhausted and full of mosquito bites in the morning. even if topiinn charges not much it is still too much for what you get. i would never come again for an overnight. the restaurant though is very good and it s a very nice place to relax.', 'we stayed here for one night to arrange a transfer to lombok. the rooms are really small, but nicely decorated. bathroom is fine with cold water shower. there is a mosquito net in the room. the staff helped us arranging tickets to senggigi, which indeed turned out the lowest price we could get. the restaurant cafe is a very nice place to relax, although the food is not good, except for the bakery we had to wait an hour for our breakfast pancake, not ideal if you have to catch a boat.', 'dorm is great and cheap but do not expect a comfortable and clean bed. also the guy on the reception and the whole staff act like they have everything about tourists. i made another mistake by ordering a breakfast here. oh god! an fried egg should take 40 mins... so good place for budget but do not expect more than you paid...', 'topi inn has a nice cafe which is well thought out. the food i tried was nice and the coffee was also good, although you are paying slightly more than you would elsewhere. the upstairs area is a nice place to relax with great views out over the bay. the rooms upstairs are small with bamboo partitions, which doesnt allow for much privacy or a quiet nights sleep. the rooms and shared bathrooms are very clean. due to the noise i ended up finding alternative accomodation for the same price which included breakfast, so if you have time it may be worth checking out some alternatives.', 'we walked in topi inn at 7pm one night. we were on a stopover to catch the morning boat to gili. the room rate was very cheap so we stayed, but quickly found out that it was ridiculisly small. the lighting was very poor. no privacy with the bamboo walls. worse mattress ever ! our room was located above the kitchen and the restaurant area. basically you hear everything that is going on downstairs, either in the kitchen, the restaurant or the bar until 1am in the morning, for the bar, the kitchen starts again very early . you can also smell everything we were woken up by garlic . to sum it up, we were exhausted in the morning, after this very short and uncomfortable night. the rate was very cheap, the staff was nice, wireless was free of charge and we were only staying one night, so it was not such a big deal. but you can probably find a better value for money elsewhere in the village.', 'i visited a couple of places in padangbai, before following the lonely planet advice to the topi inn. i was tired of carrying my backpack, and thougth this could be a nice place considering the ground level full of colors, and nice decoration. however, as many other describe, the rooms were overpriced compared to what you can find breakfast inclusive in the city, the bathroom is non private, the rooms small and poorly isolated... as a result it is quite hot, very noisy, aand you can indeed smell all the food being prepared... moreover, the staff is making a lot of noise in the morning, making it hard to sleep after 6am... the restaurant is really nice, and so is the bar, that i would recommend, but not the rooms...', 'stayed last weekend in topi inn together with my wife. first wanted to make a reservation, but the room price is much more expensive if you do 14 per night 5 booking costs, while if you just walk in it is only rp100.000 which i think is a bit strange. luckely they had one room available when we arrived. this room was a bit dark with no windows and during the night is was very hot, because we didn t had any fresh air. the next day we asked if we could move to another room and luckily they had a room available with window and even a private bathroom. the rooms are all the same price, so this was much better. the mattress is ready for replacement, but yeah you only pay rp100.000. the restaurant serves great food, although a bit expensive. furthermore the complete hostel hotel has a great atmosphere and is very cozy. the babylon bar near the hostel is a great place to hang out in the evening with cheap heineken. the nearby blue moon beach is good for snorkling, but on the otherside of the harbour is a beautiful beach with white sand and very clear water which is better for swimming you have to clime a small hill though . overall padangbai is a great place for a relaxing weekend and topi inn is a good choice if you have a room with window and didn t book in front to avoid the higher room price and booking costs.', 'after referring to our guide book we headed straight to the topi inn for our stay in padangbai, and weren t disappointed when we arrived to a colourful, chilled out place at the quiet end of the beach. the downstairs area had plenty of seating space, computers, books and blackboards advertising western dishes and fresh baguettes.it looked like the perfect hostel, and although our room 100 0000 rp wasn t the best we d seen by far, the relaxed atmosphere persuaded me to stay. however, my opinion of topi inn quickly changed for the following reasons, and i unfortunately left feeling disappointed. the computers were set at a fixed rate, which is completely reasonable. however, unlike any other guest house i have stayed in, they also made a charge for wifi, which began and ended when you alerted a waiter downstairs. the book swap was a 2 for 1 policy, which had to be approved by the staff. there were films shown in the upstairs common area in the evening. the guest house also made a charge for this again something which i have never experienced before. if you didn t want to watch the film this also left you with no where to sit and relax in the evening as downstairs as we learnt is first, and foremost, a restaurant in the evening. the fresh baguettes were very overpriced at 30 000 rp and came without any sort of garnish or chips. we later learnt if you wanted salad with your cheese it is an extra 6 thousand. not a lot of money, but you re just left wondering why! overall this is a place which offers very basic rooms, for those on a budget, but operates primarily as a relatively expensive restaurant. topi inn comes across as a confused establishment, and would work better as a restaurant alone. i wouldn t recommend this as a budget place to stay, you can find far better deals elsewhere.', 'i stayed here after booking the room through their website, the dutch owner contacted me shortly after insisting that i make full payment through before arriving. i replied to his e mail stating that this was the first time after travelling through asia for 4 months to be asked to make full payment for 7 days up front without getting an official reciept as it would be through paypal . he replied insisting that if we wanted the room we had to pay total up front. it was near christmas so we decided we had to book ahead and lonely planet rough guides made it sound like a great place.the following is one of the first e mail i recieved from the owner we still have a room available for the period mentioned however need to be quick.the price per room per night is 10 euro. breakfast is not included. for bookings there is a booking fee of 5 euro and require payment in advance. no restitution is possible upon arrival we were brought up to our hovel by the arrogant manager, the rooms were dark and dirty, the place was noisy and the staff are constantly asking you every day what you want to do today so that they can try to suggest more ways of emptying your pockets that they can conviently organise. we found out after a few days that because we had paid up front we had actually paid more than the people who had just walked in and asked for a room. there were numerous cockroaches and you could see through to the room next door because the bamboo laticework had not been maintained. i have stayed in traditional accomodation in other countries but at this place is a bit confused. we also suffered the indignity of aquiring a room which was not en suite and people who payed less got an en suite. we payed 70 euro for 7 nights, a 5 euro booking fee for no reason and 2 euro for booking through pay pal. the total was 77 euro and we could have got some thing better for 35 euro. the rate they charge for their room is insane if you inquire in other places of the same standard, one place for instance the kembar inn is much better value at a whopping half the price. the food at topi inn is substandard and completly overpriced, we ate there 3 times and were dissapointed, we had 18 meals over 7 days at other establishments and some were fantastic at the same price or less. the snorkelling equipment and other such things are all cheaper at other establishments. i also got this mail from the owner you can be picked up or dropped off to the airport or from to any place in bali . from the airport during daytime costs 30 euro and 35 euro in the evening, night or early morning we got a taxi at the airport for 15 euro!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! in my opinion the owner is yet another example of an european expat owning a business in asia and charging an inflated rate beacause he has a website and has a write up in lonely planet. also just as a last note, if he offers you the mattress beds on the communal upstairs area, you should know the following. the upstairs area is an overflow for the restaurant bar and the mattresses are not fit for a dog.', 'we were drawn to this place by the lonely planet s description of it as having a breezy second floor hangout area with hammocks strewn about, great food at the cafe below, good prices, etc. the food truly is excellent, and the atmosphere is certainly congenial, but the rooms in my opinion are totally overpriced and gob smackingly unappealing. between the four of us, we stayed in three of them there are only 5 or 6 in total . none was any bigger than a walk in closet. two had no windows to the outdoors. most are positioned directly above the cafe kitchen, so you can smell foods cooking and listen to the sound of pots clanging and knives chopping well into the night. they were hot as hell, with barely adequate fans, no breeze, and no view. i saw numerous cockroaches in the bathrooms, and one actually skittered out of my toiletry kit one evening as i reached in to get my toothbrush. the worst part of all was that the owner had insisted we pre pay for the rooms by depositing the full amount directly in his bank account so we couldn t afford to go anywhere else. i don t want to dissuade anyone from eating here, because the food is wonderful and the rooms are probably good enough if you re a young backpacker and all you re going to do is sleep in them. i would not recommend them for anyone else.']
combined_reviews: we went here to have lunch after diving. the lunch was over average, but it s a bit messy in regards to organsation and cleanliness. it offers you shower to clean yourself after being in the sea. there seem to be people sleeping pretty next to the tables, no idea who they are. but if you just wanna have a good meal you will be happy here. the most disgusting restaurant that i visited in bali! we went diving and after that took a shower in this place. bathing room in this restaurant doesn t even have a roof. when i was taking a shower, a boy peeked me and i felt extremely horrible. there is a path nearby the bathing room and a lot higher, that s the reason why people on that path could easily peek in the bathing room. it s apparently that this restaurant is responsible for this tribble arrangement but they don t care. i can even image that peeking becomes a leisure and entertainment for local people. i strongly do not recommend anyone come here!!! me and my wife were here for scuba diving in the padang bai with a local dive shop. topi inn seems to have deals with these dive shop so we can eat and shower here which is nice. however, it turn to nightmare when my wife was taking shower on the right hand side shower room close a wall without roof. she noticed someone was peaking on her behind the wall, since the other side of wall is a slope which is much higher than the restaurant side. so she screamed and the guy ran away. it was shocked and even more disappointing that the employees didn t take actions even after we complained to them. and we had to look for the people for sexual harassment on our own. this sex discrimination has to stop. food was tasty until i found a big cooked roach in my food. so disgusting! it s a shame because this is the spot where many divers come after their dives. the pineapple juice was good. they made a new dish for me but i m not sure i have the appetite anymore well. just had a chicken satay here after a morning snorkelling at white beach, and i must say it was the worst satay i have ever eaten, absolutely no flavour at all, i do not know how much it will cost as i am still in the restaurant, but i will definitely not be recommending it. this was our last dinner in indonesia after cycling bali and it was just awful from start to end the staff was inattentive and alouf, they charged loads for what i found out when i complained was a cheap cask wine and both my partner and i got very sick after the meal, it is a beautiful setting but that s the only good thing about it, avoid to go there! the place looks really nice and room is not so expensive 170.000idr 2people , so we decided to spend there nyepi day . ouje. the staff were not silence at all, their laughing awake us at morning,because everything you can hear there, but the worst was a food. imagine you are there, you can not move out of the hotel, so you have to eat there. well, we should check menu day before extremely expensive, but ok, maybe will be delicious. not at all specialy pizza is awful . terrible. and personal also 3x you ask for milk to coffee, 2x you ask for ice to cola and on the end you are without that. around you can find much better quality food for cheaper price and maybe you will get what you order. we wanted to try this place out for lunch as it looked nice and a little quirky. the food was terrible and service was worse. to top it off the food was very expensive in comparison to the other much better restaurants nearby. would recommend to give this place a miss. i would recommend puri rai instead as it is much better and cheaper. we had lunch there after our scuba dive and frankly did not find the food as amazing as some reviewers claim. but to be fair, it was a standard lunch that we were provided as part of the package and perhaps the other items are better. food is very nice but service is unbelievably slow. we ordered 2 baguettes and it took half an hour for the first one then we had to wait a further 20 minutes for the second one. nice setting and atmosphere but won t be back because of the service. we sat down near the fountain but discovered quite early that there were a lot of mosquitoes around it so we decided to move inside . once we got the menu it was very hard to catch a waiter to actually take our order. the first waiter didn t understand us and the second one also didn t get our order right. nothing too big of a deal. we got the cheese fondue which was ok. it was boiling and black at the edges. smelled a bit burned. for some reason it was 180.000rp which was more than twice as expensive as the other main course dishes, but we really felt like it. we were a bit disappointed that it was only cheese fondue with a baguette and a salad. for that price i would have expected a little more e.g. some vegetables to dip into the cheese fondue . i assume the price is that high because cheese is very hard to get. the kids had hotdogs which i heard no complaints about. the fries we ordered with them came 15 minutes before the rest of the meal which was too bad. the bloody mary cocktail was good, the gin tonic not so much. all in all a disappointing dinner for us. dropped in for lunch and at first it was quiet. ordered around of drinks whilst we perused a fairly well priced menu. then the dive boats arrived and the place was packed. despite getting our simple order in early it appears the dive groups get preference and it was nearly 40 minutes, plus at least three reminders before the last of our food arrived. food was good bet obviously under staffed. lots of apologies when we paid the bill but damage done. have since noted business for sale. great for a breakfast before the dives, or a late lunch drinks after diving. the place has a shower which is great for a wash after diving, and has a simple but good menu. they also provide the food for some of the boat dives. there team is pleasant and the location very convenient. good to return. had a mango coconut lassie here which was more sugar than anything else. decided against eating here because extensive menu was over my budget. seemed to be relaxed place, lots of books friendly staff. the restaurant service is very slow 1 hour to serve and the food is average, nothing special. they have showers and changing room service on the ground floor, which is good after the beach.the atmosphere is nice. i did not stay here because although i called booking a room when i arrived there were no rooms available. i had dinner there. the venue itself is very nice, the food is tasty but nothing of special. they have several vegetarian options but i was not sure whether they were vegan and made questions, the staff was clearly annoyed with my questions. came for coffee and cake. wasnt greeted so sat down and the staff just continued cleaning around us even mopping under our feet. more staff was sat at a table playing on their phones didnt budge or even acknowledge we were there. it took my 3 year old to ask the girl cleaning for a diet coke for her to call someone to serve us. the staff had no personality or interest in their customers. very disappointing. the only reason i am giving a 2 and not a 1 is because the apple crumble once we got it was great and a huge portion. the coffee was also good! maybe look at updating your staff or giving them some training on customer service. you do have one outstanding staff member which the others can learn from and that is putu! he is fantastic and a great young man! he is how your other staff members should be like. he wasnt working the day we came in though for this review! the room really is something out of an island retreat movie. bamboo rooms big enough to just fit the bed, open air with an al fresco bathroom. on first glimpse we thought it could be quite fun. however, bamboo walls meant you could hear every whisper throughout all the rooms, mosquito net had holes and noise from the harbour sometimes was a little loud. as we were packing to leave i picked up my bag and a pretty big rat jumped from under it and across the bed. thank god we were leaving and not just arriving otherwise we probably wouldn t have stayed breakfast not provided but decent prices and a takeaway service so you can catch the boat early in the morning with some food in your backpack. organic but cheap construction. very small rooms are separated by bamboo walls that don t give any privacy. every sound sight can be experienced by your neighbours vica versa in the next room. a mosquito net is provided but this has more holes than a sieve tastefully patched with a variety of tapes. there is one electrical socket which powers the electric fan so if you want to charge any equipment overnight it is going to be a very sweaty night. i pre paid for 3 nights on my 2nd i realised that i wasn t alone i was sharing my room with a rather large brown rat! i complained asked for my money back for the 3rd night but they weren t interested i was only offered an alternative room which wasn t a solution given the flimsy construction of the rooms which provides a perfect rat run . there are far superior budget places to stay in padangbia for less money most supplying free breakfast which topi inn doesn t . forget the beachside location as ferries entering leaving port all night long isn t indusive to a good nights sleep. try celagi inn or bamboo paradise. unless the thought of rats running over you during the night is appealing avoid dirty sub standard expensive topi inn like the plague. topi arranges activities and tours in addition to their rooms and restaurant. this review is only in regard to a transport arrangement. the shuttle topi arranged with wannen bali pizza toast was a piece of toast with some cheese. fruit salad was bad. avoid we stayed at room six which is a spacious loft. the place is rather exposed so don t expect your average hotel room. to explain, the room does not have a door but you enter the room by climbing a stairs and get through a hatch. we asked for a lock. we had an extra guest, it was a cat. there was no hot water. we paid 37 euro excluding breakfast. rather expensive but to be fair the room van sleep up to 4 people. it is a short walk from the port. very friendly staff. they were very helpfull in arranging boat tickets. not clean, not a private bathroom, brocken windows, very loud during the night, bed was shaking whenever somebody walked outside the room, unfriendly and very nosy greedy staff always trying to sell something, even tried to make us pay for breakfast although it was included. would not come again. padangbai also not worth to stay more than more than 1 night just good for going to gili islands. at the end of the beach road in padangbai is topi inn, with a delightful view over the harbour. it seems to be a place in transition. it doesn t quite know what it is any more. historically it was the only place in town, but now it fights for recognition with the many other restaurants that have sprung up in this town. we enjoyed our beers and watched the sun set over the harbour, but the menu was enormous really huge with everything under the sun and one wonders how it could be possibly maintained. go for the drinks, not for the meals. this hotel if you can call it that is disgusting! to even call it a hostel would be giving it too much praise! a friend and i were booked in for two nights but we didn t even stay one! we had a room with a private bathroom. the room was filthy, the lights didnt work and the plug socket for the door stretched across thr sliding door for the bathroom, which the shower was behind! the bathroom smelt horrendous, the toilet didn t flush and the shower was no more than a cold trickle. you felt cleaner before you went in. the security was pretty much non existant...we had to ask for a padlock for our room. the final straw came when there were mich running about our room which makes you wonder what theur kitchen for the restaurant is like . we left before staying one night. ..and they didn t even question why. spend a bit more and go elsewhere! this place is a mixed bag. first of all, it s cheap. super cheap! for serious backpackers they offer a row of mattresses on the floor with mosquito nets suspended above them. you will probably want to use the net because the area is open air, and the place is constucted of bamboo. i believe the cost was 75k per night which is just unbeatable. and quite honestly, i liked it! i thought it was a cool experience, and found it surprisingly comfortable in august when i visited. you can hear the waves crashing against the sand at night. with that said, it can be very noisy! some nights they feature live music at the restaurant downstairs, and you will hear it loud and clear. the staff is nice, but not necessarily attentive. they seem to always be busy with the restaurant operation. the food in my opinion is mediocre and overpriced. there are numerous options within walking distance for delicious and cheap food. overall, it was ok for me. the location just across from the beach at the end of the main strip was ideal. i would recommend going there in person to inquire about availability. i called and someone said they were sold out, but when i walked in 15 minutes later they had a space available. most likely just a miscommunication, which you will surely experience in indonesia as an english speaker. bottom line, i d stay here again. if you re a backpacker looking for something cheap, try it for a night. i ended up staying for 5! they also have private rooms available very limited . i saw numerous families in those rooms that seemed to enjoy it. we arrived late with the farry from lombok and lonely planet was suggesting this place as top one! and we were supposed to spend two nights in this place, so the only room availble was the suite for 250.000irp without breakfast it has been the worst night in indonesia ever room is very nice all in bamboo but everything it open you can listen to everything, a cat was hanging around, in the bathroom there was one of those huge geko not normal one, enormous which i had to use the dorm bathroom, bed was so humid that i could feel it almost wet, full of bugs in the room..in the bed as well! in the morning for breakfast i had another nice sourprise we ordered food and my toast after 45 min did not arrived even though i ve asked twice how long should i wait for that...i finally went to another place to get my breakfast staff is smiley but extremely lazy and they try all the time to sell you tours or arrange you transfer...we had dinner there, big selection from the men , good enough but still very slow service. in the end they also try to add me a big beer which we didn t drink. this bad service it s a pity because the place itself it s really characteristic. they cherge 3 if you pay by credit card. we stayed in topiinn for 2 nights and right after the first night we regretted our choice. we had a double room with an enclosed bathroom. the rooms are very tiny and the matress is aweful. the mosquito nets are full of holes and don t fit the bed. the walls are only made of bamboo so that you can hear and feel your neighbour move in the bed in the next room. there is only a small fan in the room which makes the whole room very warm and uncomfortable. the enclosed toilet is only seperated by a small wall from the public toilet so that you hear everything from that bathroom. we hardly slep, we were exhausted and full of mosquito bites in the morning. even if topiinn charges not much it is still too much for what you get. i would never come again for an overnight. the restaurant though is very good and it s a very nice place to relax. we stayed here for one night to arrange a transfer to lombok. the rooms are really small, but nicely decorated. bathroom is fine with cold water shower. there is a mosquito net in the room. the staff helped us arranging tickets to senggigi, which indeed turned out the lowest price we could get. the restaurant cafe is a very nice place to relax, although the food is not good, except for the bakery we had to wait an hour for our breakfast pancake, not ideal if you have to catch a boat. dorm is great and cheap but do not expect a comfortable and clean bed. also the guy on the reception and the whole staff act like they have everything about tourists. i made another mistake by ordering a breakfast here. oh god! an fried egg should take 40 mins... so good place for budget but do not expect more than you paid... topi inn has a nice cafe which is well thought out. the food i tried was nice and the coffee was also good, although you are paying slightly more than you would elsewhere. the upstairs area is a nice place to relax with great views out over the bay. the rooms upstairs are small with bamboo partitions, which doesnt allow for much privacy or a quiet nights sleep. the rooms and shared bathrooms are very clean. due to the noise i ended up finding alternative accomodation for the same price which included breakfast, so if you have time it may be worth checking out some alternatives. we walked in topi inn at 7pm one night. we were on a stopover to catch the morning boat to gili. the room rate was very cheap so we stayed, but quickly found out that it was ridiculisly small. the lighting was very poor. no privacy with the bamboo walls. worse mattress ever ! our room was located above the kitchen and the restaurant area. basically you hear everything that is going on downstairs, either in the kitchen, the restaurant or the bar until 1am in the morning, for the bar, the kitchen starts again very early . you can also smell everything we were woken up by garlic . to sum it up, we were exhausted in the morning, after this very short and uncomfortable night. the rate was very cheap, the staff was nice, wireless was free of charge and we were only staying one night, so it was not such a big deal. but you can probably find a better value for money elsewhere in the village. i visited a couple of places in padangbai, before following the lonely planet advice to the topi inn. i was tired of carrying my backpack, and thougth this could be a nice place considering the ground level full of colors, and nice decoration. however, as many other describe, the rooms were overpriced compared to what you can find breakfast inclusive in the city, the bathroom is non private, the rooms small and poorly isolated... as a result it is quite hot, very noisy, aand you can indeed smell all the food being prepared... moreover, the staff is making a lot of noise in the morning, making it hard to sleep after 6am... the restaurant is really nice, and so is the bar, that i would recommend, but not the rooms... stayed last weekend in topi inn together with my wife. first wanted to make a reservation, but the room price is much more expensive if you do 14 per night 5 booking costs, while if you just walk in it is only rp100.000 which i think is a bit strange. luckely they had one room available when we arrived. this room was a bit dark with no windows and during the night is was very hot, because we didn t had any fresh air. the next day we asked if we could move to another room and luckily they had a room available with window and even a private bathroom. the rooms are all the same price, so this was much better. the mattress is ready for replacement, but yeah you only pay rp100.000. the restaurant serves great food, although a bit expensive. furthermore the complete hostel hotel has a great atmosphere and is very cozy. the babylon bar near the hostel is a great place to hang out in the evening with cheap heineken. the nearby blue moon beach is good for snorkling, but on the otherside of the harbour is a beautiful beach with white sand and very clear water which is better for swimming you have to clime a small hill though . overall padangbai is a great place for a relaxing weekend and topi inn is a good choice if you have a room with window and didn t book in front to avoid the higher room price and booking costs. after referring to our guide book we headed straight to the topi inn for our stay in padangbai, and weren t disappointed when we arrived to a colourful, chilled out place at the quiet end of the beach. the downstairs area had plenty of seating space, computers, books and blackboards advertising western dishes and fresh baguettes.it looked like the perfect hostel, and although our room 100 0000 rp wasn t the best we d seen by far, the relaxed atmosphere persuaded me to stay. however, my opinion of topi inn quickly changed for the following reasons, and i unfortunately left feeling disappointed. the computers were set at a fixed rate, which is completely reasonable. however, unlike any other guest house i have stayed in, they also made a charge for wifi, which began and ended when you alerted a waiter downstairs. the book swap was a 2 for 1 policy, which had to be approved by the staff. there were films shown in the upstairs common area in the evening. the guest house also made a charge for this again something which i have never experienced before. if you didn t want to watch the film this also left you with no where to sit and relax in the evening as downstairs as we learnt is first, and foremost, a restaurant in the evening. the fresh baguettes were very overpriced at 30 000 rp and came without any sort of garnish or chips. we later learnt if you wanted salad with your cheese it is an extra 6 thousand. not a lot of money, but you re just left wondering why! overall this is a place which offers very basic rooms, for those on a budget, but operates primarily as a relatively expensive restaurant. topi inn comes across as a confused establishment, and would work better as a restaurant alone. i wouldn t recommend this as a budget place to stay, you can find far better deals elsewhere. i stayed here after booking the room through their website, the dutch owner contacted me shortly after insisting that i make full payment through before arriving. i replied to his e mail stating that this was the first time after travelling through asia for 4 months to be asked to make full payment for 7 days up front without getting an official reciept as it would be through paypal . he replied insisting that if we wanted the room we had to pay total up front. it was near christmas so we decided we had to book ahead and lonely planet rough guides made it sound like a great place.the following is one of the first e mail i recieved from the owner we still have a room available for the period mentioned however need to be quick.the price per room per night is 10 euro. breakfast is not included. for bookings there is a booking fee of 5 euro and require payment in advance. no restitution is possible upon arrival we were brought up to our hovel by the arrogant manager, the rooms were dark and dirty, the place was noisy and the staff are constantly asking you every day what you want to do today so that they can try to suggest more ways of emptying your pockets that they can conviently organise. we found out after a few days that because we had paid up front we had actually paid more than the people who had just walked in and asked for a room. there were numerous cockroaches and you could see through to the room next door because the bamboo laticework had not been maintained. i have stayed in traditional accomodation in other countries but at this place is a bit confused. we also suffered the indignity of aquiring a room which was not en suite and people who payed less got an en suite. we payed 70 euro for 7 nights, a 5 euro booking fee for no reason and 2 euro for booking through pay pal. the total was 77 euro and we could have got some thing better for 35 euro. the rate they charge for their room is insane if you inquire in other places of the same standard, one place for instance the kembar inn is much better value at a whopping half the price. the food at topi inn is substandard and completly overpriced, we ate there 3 times and were dissapointed, we had 18 meals over 7 days at other establishments and some were fantastic at the same price or less. the snorkelling equipment and other such things are all cheaper at other establishments. i also got this mail from the owner you can be picked up or dropped off to the airport or from to any place in bali . from the airport during daytime costs 30 euro and 35 euro in the evening, night or early morning we got a taxi at the airport for 15 euro!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! in my opinion the owner is yet another example of an european expat owning a business in asia and charging an inflated rate beacause he has a website and has a write up in lonely planet. also just as a last note, if he offers you the mattress beds on the communal upstairs area, you should know the following. the upstairs area is an overflow for the restaurant bar and the mattresses are not fit for a dog. we were drawn to this place by the lonely planet s description of it as having a breezy second floor hangout area with hammocks strewn about, great food at the cafe below, good prices, etc. the food truly is excellent, and the atmosphere is certainly congenial, but the rooms in my opinion are totally overpriced and gob smackingly unappealing. between the four of us, we stayed in three of them there are only 5 or 6 in total . none was any bigger than a walk in closet. two had no windows to the outdoors. most are positioned directly above the cafe kitchen, so you can smell foods cooking and listen to the sound of pots clanging and knives chopping well into the night. they were hot as hell, with barely adequate fans, no breeze, and no view. i saw numerous cockroaches in the bathrooms, and one actually skittered out of my toiletry kit one evening as i reached in to get my toothbrush. the worst part of all was that the owner had insisted we pre pay for the rooms by depositing the full amount directly in his bank account so we couldn t afford to go anywhere else. i don t want to dissuade anyone from eating here, because the food is wonderful and the rooms are probably good enough if you re a young backpacker and all you re going to do is sleep in them. i would not recommend them for anyone else.
